## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Sinistre Foundation**, premier sprint vertical metier d'Assurflow apres l'infrastructure complete (Phase 1 Foundation + Phase 2 Modules Transverses + Phase 3 SEL/SRF). Le sprint construit le **domain model Sinistre** avec ses entites principales (Sinistre, SinistreLine, SinistreType, SinistreBranch, SinistreLifecycleEvent), le **state machine lifecycle 8 etats** rigoureux avec transitions strict validees (declaration -> received -> under_review -> expertise_requested -> payout_decided -> payment_executing -> repair_completed -> finalized), l'**integration sagas Sprint 15 SRF** consume declare-claim saga 4 etapes pour declaration sinistre cross-organism via SEL Sprint 14, la **compliance ACAPS Circulaire 02-AS/2024 cas d'usage 4.7.2.2** declaration sinistre conforme reglementation marocaine, les **documents references Sprint 10** (constat amiable, photos sinistre, expertises, factures garage), les **notifications Sprint 9** sur chaque transition lifecycle, et les **endpoints REST** consommables par Sprint 18 Web Customer Portal + Sprint 19 Mobile Assure.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un domain model Sinistre production-grade avec 6 entites principales TypeORM + RLS multi-tenant strict, d'un service `SinistreLifecycleService` avec state machine 8 etats validees + transitions audit log immutable + integration sagas Sprint 15, d'un service `SinistreService` CRUD complet avec validations Zod schemas canonical Avro sinistre.v1 Sprint 15, d'un service `AcapsComplianceValidatorService` pre-flight check declaration conforme cas 4.7.2.2 (champs obligatoires complets + format CIN/plaque/police validation + montant estime range coherent), d'un `SinistreEventEmitter` publish Kafka events sur chaque transition lifecycle (declaration_submitted, received_by_insurer, under_review, expertise_requested, payout_decided, payment_executing, repair_completed, finalized) consume par Sprint 9 NotificationRouter + Sprint 13 Analytics + Sprint 15 sagas, d'endpoints REST `/api/v1/sinistres` avec permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires + OpenAPI 3.1, et la suite de tests E2E avec 30 scenarios couvrant happy path + lifecycle complete + validations ACAPS + integration sagas + compensations.

Le sprint pose les **fondations critiques** pour les sprints suivants Phase 4 : Sprint 17 Police Lifecycle (polices liees sinistres + adhesions), Sprint 18 Web Customer Portal (UX assures declaration sinistre + suivi), Sprint 19 Web Assure Portal Mobile (mobile-first declaration avec photos), Sprint 20 IA Estimation Photos (vision IA preview estimation dommages). Le scope est volontairement focus sur le **domain model + lifecycle + integration sagas**, l'UX assures et l'IA sont differes aux sprints suivants.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 16

Le sprint construit le domain model Sinistre + lifecycle complete + integration ecosystem. Concretement, il livre la **decision-017 ratification** documentant le state machine 8 etats + transitions strict + justification strategique alignment ACAPS, les **6 entites domain Sinistre** TypeORM avec relations + RLS multi-tenant + indexes performance (`Sinistre` aggregate root avec id + tenant_id + customer_id Sprint 8 + sinistre_type + sinistre_branch + status enum 8 etats + declaration_date + montant_estime NUMERIC(15,2) MAD + montant_decide_payout nullable + insurer_partner_id FK Sprint 14 Service Registry + police_id nullable FK Sprint 17 future + correlation_id UUID saga tracking + created_at + updated_at, `SinistreLine` multi-line claims pour sinistres complexes avec id + sinistre_id + description + montant_ligne + status, `SinistreType` taxonomy enum auto/dommages_corporels/dommages_materiels/multirisques/sante/responsabilite_civile/decennale/transport_marchandises, `SinistreBranch` complement enum specialise auto branches (auto_RC_obligatoire/auto_DC_dommages/auto_vol/auto_incendie/multirisques_habitation/multirisques_entreprise/sante_complementaire/...), `SinistreLifecycleEvent` audit immutable each transition avec id + sinistre_id + from_status + to_status + transition_reason + actor_id Sprint 5 + actor_type enum customer/tenant_agent/insurer/system_saga + timestamp + metadata JSONB + correlation_id, `SinistreDocument` references Sprint 10 documents (constat amiable + photos + expertises + factures) avec id + sinistre_id + document_id_sprint10 + document_type enum + uploaded_at + uploaded_by_id), le **service `SinistreLifecycleService`** orchestrate state machine avec methodes (`transition(sinistreId, toStatus, reason, actorId): LifecycleResult` valide transition + persist event + emit Kafka event, `getValidTransitions(sinistre): NextStatus[]` retourne transitions possibles selon current_state, `getCurrentStatus(sinistreId): SinistreStatus`, `getHistory(sinistreId): LifecycleEvent[]`), le **state machine 8 etats** strict avec 12 transitions valides documentees (declaration->received auto via saga Sprint 15, received->under_review manual ou auto 24h, under_review->expertise_requested si expertise needed, under_review->declaration backward si missing info, expertise_requested->payout_decided apres rapport, payout_decided->payment_executing si approved, payout_decided->finalized si rejected pas de payout, payment_executing->repair_completed si vehicle repair, payment_executing->finalized si pas de repair, repair_completed->finalized) + 4 transitions invalides explicit blocked (jamais declaration->payout_decided direct, jamais finalized->any reverse, jamais skip etats), le **service `SinistreService`** CRUD complet avec validations Zod schemas canonical Avro sinistre.v1 Sprint 15 SRF, le **service `AcapsComplianceValidatorService`** pre-flight check declaration conforme ACAPS cas 4.7.2.2 (champs obligatoires CIN customer + numero police + plaque immatriculation + date_sinistre + lieu_sinistre + description + montant_estime + photos minimum 3 + constat amiable si auto, format CIN MA 8 chars, format plaque AAA-NNN-LL strict, montant estime range coherent 100-10M MAD), l'**integration sagas Sprint 15 SRF** via SinistreService.declareClaim utilise `SagaOrchestratorService.startSaga('declare-claim', initialPayload)` Sprint 15 4.2.8 saga 4 etapes + integration request-expertise saga si under_review->expertise_requested, le **SinistreEventEmitter** publish Kafka events `insurtech.events.sinistre.{declaration_submitted,received_by_insurer,under_review,expertise_requested,payout_decided,payment_executing,repair_completed,finalized}` consume Sprint 9 NotificationRouter (email + push customer + agent tenant) + Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline sinistre, les **endpoints REST** complets `/api/v1/sinistres` (POST create declaration, GET list paginated filterable, GET by id, PUT update status manual super-admin only, GET lifecycle events, GET acaps-compliance-check pre-flight) avec permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires (`sinistre.read/create/update/transition`) + OpenAPI 3.1 + rate limit 100 req/min/tenant, 30+ tests E2E couvrant lifecycle complete + integration sagas + ACAPS validation + multi-tenant isolation.

### Exclus du Sprint 16 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas les **UX assures declaration sinistre** (frontend customer portal) qui est defere Sprint 18 Web Customer Portal (Sprint 16 fait backend domain model + endpoints REST consume par futur frontend), les **UX mobile-first declaration avec photos terrain** differe Sprint 19 Web Assure Portal Mobile (Sprint 16 backend supporte mais pas mobile UI), l'**IA estimation photos** vision computer dommages differe Sprint 20 (Sprint 16 stockage photos via Sprint 10 reference + montant_estime manuel, IA Sprint 20 enrichit avec auto-estimation), la **gestion polices liees sinistres** avec validation police active + branche coverage match defere Sprint 17 Police Lifecycle (Sprint 16 stocke police_id reference mais validation differe Sprint 17), la **dispatch automatique experts** differe Sprint 21-23 Dispatch Platform Uber-style (Sprint 16 saga request-expertise mock expert selection Sprint 15 4.2.8, real dispatch Sprint 21-23), les **workflows garage reparation** avec handoff cross-tenant differes Sprint 22 Web Garage App + Sprint 30 Garage Workshop (Sprint 16 backend supporte status repair_completed mais workflow garage differe), les **dispute workflows** (customer conteste decision insurer) differes Sprint 24 Flux Sinistre Client (Sprint 16 lifecycle supporte status sans dispute resolution), les **analytics avancees sinistralite** (taux sinistre par branche + saisonnalite + heatmaps geographiques) differes Sprint 28 Admin Reports Compliance (Sprint 16 fait events emission consume par Sprint 13 ETL, dashboards avances Sprint 28), et les **integrations vraies compagnies marocaines** (Wafa Assurance, Saham Assurance, AtlantaSanad, etc) differees Sprint 32 (Sprint 16 utilise fictional insurers AssurMaghreb/Atlas/MediAssur Sprint 14 4.1.8 + canonical schemas Sprint 15 SRF, transition vraies compagnies via Service Registry update transparent).

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 16 est le **premier sprint vertical metier** apres 15 sprints d'infrastructure. Quatre dimensions strategiques justifient l'investissement de 75 heures sur ce sprint critique.

Premierement, l'**inflection point vertical** : Phases 1-3 ont livre 15 sprints d'infrastructure (Foundation + Modules Transverses + SEL/SRF), mais aucun produit fonctionnel utilisateur. Sprint 16 est le premier sprint qui delivre une **capacite metier observable** (declarer un sinistre, suivre son lifecycle, recevoir notifications). Pour les co-fondateurs Saad CTO + Abla CEO, ce sprint marque la transition "infrastructure technique" -> "produit metier". Pour les investisseurs Demo Day, ce sprint est le **premier visible product capability** d'Assurflow. La qualite de cette foundation determine la perception globale.

Deuxiemement, le **state machine 8 etats** doit etre rigoureux car il est le **squelette de tous workflows Sinistre downstream**. Sprint 17 (Police), Sprint 18 (Customer Portal), Sprint 19 (Mobile), Sprint 20 (IA), Sprint 21-23 (Dispatch), Sprint 24-28 (Apps Sinistre), Sprint 29-33 (Garage) reposent tous sur ce lifecycle. Si transitions floues ou audit log incomplets, debugging Sprint 17+ devient cauchemar. Decision-017 ratifie state machine strict avec 12 transitions validees + 4 transitions invalides explicit blocked + audit log immutable each transition. Pattern industrie standard (Stripe payments lifecycle, Shopify orders lifecycle, Salesforce opportunity stages).

Troisiemement, la **compliance ACAPS Circulaire 02-AS/2024 cas d'usage 4.7.2.2** est non negociable pour le **Programme Emergence ACAPS**. La demande ACAPS d'Assurflow (en cours d'instruction par Saad CEO) requires demonstration capabilities specifiques. Le cas 4.7.2.2 documente requirements declaration sinistre conformes : champs obligatoires (CIN + police + plaque + date + lieu + description + montant + photos + constat si auto), format strict (CIN MA 8 chars + plaque AAA-NNN-LL), traceability complete (audit log every action + correlation_id end-to-end), retention 10 ans + 1 jour donnees fiscales (alignment decision-008). Service `AcapsComplianceValidatorService` pre-flight check garantit conformite avant submission insurer.

Quatriemement, l'**integration sagas Sprint 15 SRF** valide pratique l'investissement Phase 3. Sprint 15 a livre saga `declare-claim` 4 etapes (validate_claim_data + lookup_policy_at_insurer + submit_claim_to_insurer + notify_tenant_acknowledgement) mais Sprint 15 mocks Sprint 17 dependencies. Sprint 16 consume cette saga reellement avec entites domain + workflow lifecycle. Si saga integration breaks ou compensations failed, l'argument "SRF gere workflows complexes" devient vide. Sprint 16 est donc le **proof point pratique** de l'investissement SEL+SRF Phase 3.

---

## ARCHITECTURE CIBLE -- SINISTRE FOUNDATION DOMAIN MODEL

### Package @insurtech/sinistre

```
repo/packages/sinistre/
├── package.json                       # @insurtech/sinistre v1.0.0
├── src/
│   ├── entities/
│   │   ├── sinistre.entity.ts         # Aggregate root
│   │   ├── sinistre-line.entity.ts
│   │   ├── sinistre-lifecycle-event.entity.ts
│   │   ├── sinistre-document.entity.ts
│   │   └── enums/
│   │       ├── sinistre-status.enum.ts        # 8 etats
│   │       ├── sinistre-type.enum.ts          # taxonomy
│   │       └── sinistre-branch.enum.ts        # branches
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── sinistre.service.ts                # CRUD
│   │   ├── sinistre-lifecycle.service.ts      # State machine
│   │   ├── acaps-compliance-validator.service.ts
│   │   └── sinistre-event-emitter.service.ts
│   ├── state-machine/
│   │   ├── sinistre-state-machine.ts          # 12 transitions valides
│   │   └── transition-validators.ts
│   ├── controllers/
│   │   └── sinistre.controller.ts
│   └── public.ts                              # Public interfaces Sprint 17+
└── tests/
```

### State machine 8 etats avec 12 transitions valides

```
                                    ┌────────────┐
                                    │declaration │ (initial - customer submission)
                                    └─────┬──────┘
                                          │ auto via saga declare-claim Sprint 15
                                          ▼
                                    ┌──────────┐
                                    │ received │ (insurer ack)
                                    └─────┬────┘
                                          │ manual ou auto 24h
                                          ▼
                                    ┌─────────────┐
                            ┌───────┤under_review │
                            │       └─────┬───────┘
                            │             │
                            │ missing info│ if expertise needed
                            │             ▼
                            │       ┌──────────────────────┐
                            │       │expertise_requested   │
                            │       └─────┬────────────────┘
                            │             │ apres rapport expert
                            │             ▼
                            │       ┌──────────────────┐
                            │       │ payout_decided   │
                            │       └─────┬────────────┘
                            │             │
                            │     approved│       rejected
                            │             ▼          ▼
                            │      ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────┐
                            │      │payment_executing│   │finalized│
                            │      └────┬────────────┘   └─────────┘
                            │           │
                            │  if repair│       direct
                            │           ▼              ▼
                            │     ┌──────────────────┐
                            │     │repair_completed  │─────┐
                            │     └──────────────────┘     │
                            │                              ▼
                            │                          ┌──────────┐
                            └─────────────────────────▶│finalized │
                                                       └──────────┘
```

**12 transitions valides (whitelist strict)** :

| # | From | To | Trigger | Actor |
|---|------|-----|---------|-------|
| 1 | declaration | received | Auto saga declare-claim step3 success | system_saga |
| 2 | received | under_review | Manual insurer agent OR auto 24h timeout | insurer / system |
| 3 | under_review | declaration | Missing info backward (compensation) | tenant_agent / insurer |
| 4 | under_review | expertise_requested | If expertise needed | tenant_agent |
| 5 | expertise_requested | payout_decided | Apres rapport expert recu | tenant_agent / insurer |
| 6 | payout_decided | payment_executing | If approved | system_saga (saga approve-payout) |
| 7 | payout_decided | finalized | If rejected (pas de payout) | system_saga |
| 8 | payment_executing | repair_completed | If vehicle repair completed | tenant_agent |
| 9 | payment_executing | finalized | If pas de repair needed | system_saga |
| 10 | repair_completed | finalized | Closure complete | system_saga (saga finalize-claim) |

**Transitions invalides explicit blocked (4 exemples)** :
- declaration -> payout_decided direct (skip received + under_review)
- finalized -> any (immutable closure)
- repair_completed -> payout_decided (reverse)
- payment_executing -> expertise_requested (skip backward)

### SinistreType taxonomy

| Type | Branches associees |
|------|-------------------|
| auto | auto_RC_obligatoire, auto_DC_dommages, auto_vol, auto_incendie, auto_bris_glace |
| dommages_corporels | tous accidents personnes |
| dommages_materiels | habitation, entreprise |
| multirisques | habitation, entreprise, professionnel |
| sante | sante_complementaire, hospitalier |
| responsabilite_civile | RC professionnelle, RC vie privee |
| decennale | construction batiment 10 ans |
| transport_marchandises | logistique transport |

### Cas d'usage ACAPS 4.7.2.2 -- Declaration sinistre conforme

Champs obligatoires complets pour validation conforme :

| Champ | Validation | Source |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| customer_cin | Format CIN MA 8 chars (1 lettre + 7 chiffres OU 2 lettres + 6 chiffres) | Sprint 8 CRM (encrypted) |
| numero_police | Lookup Sprint 17 future ou Sprint 14 SEL insurer.policy.lookup | Sprint 14 SEL |
| plaque_immatriculation | Format AAA-NNN-LL strict (3 chiffres + dash + 1-2 lettres + dash + 1-3 chiffres) | Customer declaration |
| date_sinistre | Date passee ou today, max 1 an retroactif | Customer |
| lieu_sinistre | Adresse + GPS coordonnees | Customer |
| description | Min 50 caracteres prose | Customer |
| montant_estime | Range 100 MAD - 10M MAD | Customer (Sprint 20 IA auto-estimation) |
| photos | Minimum 3 references documents Sprint 10 | Customer upload |
| constat_amiable | Required si type=auto, document Sprint 10 reference | Customer upload |

Service `AcapsComplianceValidatorService.validate(declaration): ComplianceResult` retourne `{ valid: boolean, errors: string[], warnings: string[] }` pre-flight check avant saga declare-claim launch.

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Le sprint applique strictement les decisions suivantes.

**Decision-001 monorepo-structure** : nouveau package `repo/packages/sinistre/`.

**Decision-002 multi-tenant-3-niveaux** : entites Sinistre + SinistreLine + SinistreLifecycleEvent + SinistreDocument ont `tenant_id UUID NOT NULL` RLS active.

**Decision-003 typeorm-vs-prisma** : TypeORM 0.3.x.

**Decision-004 kafka-vs-rabbitmq** : events sinistre publies via Kafka topics `insurtech.events.sinistre.{declaration_submitted,received_by_insurer,under_review,expertise_requested,payout_decided,payment_executing,repair_completed,finalized}` consume Sprint 9 + Sprint 13 + Sprint 15 sagas.

**Decision-005 skalean-ai-frontier** : aucun IA. Estimation IA Sprint 20.

**Decision-006 no-emoji-policy** : strict.

**Decision-007 ai-3-deferred-sprints** : Sprint 30+.

**Decision-008 data-residency-maroc** : strict. Donnees sinistre + photos + documents Atlas Cloud Benguerir.

**Decision-009 signature-loi-43-20** : constat amiable signe via Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow si version dematerialisee.

**Decision-010 atlas-cloud-souverain** : strict.

**Decision-011 pci-dss-no-card-storage** : payout via Sprint 11 (no card data).

**Decision-012 payout-instant-mwallet-bam** : saga approve-payout integre Sprint 11.

**Decision-013 dgi-efacturation-2026** : si garage repair, factures via Sprint 12 + DGI Sprint 14 SEL.

**Decision-014 clickhouse-olap-separation** : events sinistre ETL Sprint 13 vers ClickHouse.

**Decision-015 skalean-exchange-layer-xroad** : SEL transport pour cross-organism (insurer policy lookup, claim declare, payout approve).

**Decision-016 srf-schema-registry-translation** : SRF Translation Engine pour format partner-canonical + sagas + idempotency keys.

**Decision-017 sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine** (NOUVEAU - ratification 1 juin 2026) : adoption **state machine 8 etats** avec 12 transitions valides + 4+ transitions invalides explicit blocked + audit log immutable each transition (table `sinistre_lifecycle_events` append-only). Justification : (a) Sprint 17-33 downstream reposent sur lifecycle stable, (b) ACAPS compliance traceability requise, (c) Pattern industrie standard (Stripe payments, Shopify orders, Salesforce opportunities), (d) State machine strict evite bugs subtils order workflow. Architecture : enum `SinistreStatus` 8 valeurs strict + table audit immutable + service `SinistreLifecycleService` orchestrate avec transition validators. Integration : sagas Sprint 15 SRF declenche transitions auto (declare-claim -> received, approve-payout -> payment_executing, finalize-claim -> finalized).

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege transversal 1 : State machine relachee bypass via direct UPDATE Postgres.** Developpeur fait `UPDATE sinistre SET status = 'payout_decided' WHERE id = ?` sans passer service lifecycle = bypass validation transitions + audit log incomplet. Solution : trigger Postgres `enforce_status_transition` execute avant UPDATE status verifie valid transition + INSERT audit event automatique + ROLLBACK si invalid, defense en profondeur application service + BDD trigger.

**Piege transversal 2 : Audit log lifecycle silent failures.** Saga step transitionne sinistre received->under_review mais Kafka event emit fail = audit log perdu silently. Solution : transactional outbox pattern strict -- transition + INSERT lifecycle_event + INSERT kafka_outbox dans meme transaction Postgres, publisher Kafka worker async lit outbox + retry sur fail (idempotent via event_id UUID).

**Piege transversal 3 : Validations ACAPS incomplets pre-saga.** Saga declare-claim launched sans validations pre-flight = compensations expensive si insurer rejecte. Solution : `AcapsComplianceValidatorService.validate()` pre-flight strict OBLIGATOIRE avant `SagaOrchestratorService.startSaga('declare-claim')`, throw `AcapsValidationError` si invalid, customer voit erreurs avant submit.

**Piege transversal 4 : CIN encrypted vs plaintext queries.** Customer CIN encrypted Sprint 8 mais sinistre query "find by CIN" peut leak via timing attacks ou indexes plain. Solution : query CIN via Sprint 8 CRM `CustomerService.findByCin(encryptedCin)` strict, never plaintext CIN dans logs/queries Sinistre, audit log access CIN dedicated.

**Piege transversal 5 : Concurrent transitions same sinistre race conditions.** 2 agents tenant cliquent "transition under_review" simultane = 2 events created + state machine confused. Solution : `SELECT FOR UPDATE` sur sinistre row pendant transition + lock duration < 500ms + retry safe si lock contention.

**Piege transversal 6 : Multi-tenant cross-tenant sinistre leak via expert assignment.** Expert tenant_B reviews sinistre tenant_A (cross-tenant) mais voit details PII customer tenant_A. Solution : cross-tenant access strict via Sprint 14 SEL cross_tenant_handoff service avec payload filtre (no PII unnecessary), customer name + plaque + montant only, full PII tenant_A scope strict.

**Piege transversal 7 : Sinistre lifecycle event volume explosion.** 1000 sinistres/jour * 5 transitions average = 5000 events/jour = 1.8M/an. Solution : table `sinistre_lifecycle_events` partition mensuelle + archive ClickHouse Sprint 13 apres 6 mois + drop partitions Postgres apres 1 an (donnees long-term ClickHouse).

**Piege transversal 8 : Saga compensation casse lifecycle state.** Saga declare-claim step3 fail apres step2 success = compensate cancel_claim_at_insurer + sinistre Postgres etat 'declaration'. Si compensation fail aussi, sinistre stuck. Solution : compensations idempotent strict (Sprint 15 4.2.7 pattern), si compensation exhaust retries 3x -> manual intervention via Sprint 16 endpoint admin (`POST /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/manual-compensate`).

**Piege transversal 9 : ACAPS retention 10 ans + 1 jour conflicting RGPD MA suppression.** Loi 09-08 CNDP permet customer demander suppression PII, mais ACAPS Circulaire 02-AS/2024 requires 10 ans + 1 jour retention. Solution : pseudonymisation customer apres customer_deletion_request (PII -> hash + reference Sprint 8 CRM), sinistre data preserve avec customer_id pseudonyme, audit log dedicated GDPR-compliance event.

**Piege transversal 10 : Cas usage ACAPS 4.7.2.2 evolutif circulaires futures.** ACAPS publie regulierement updates circulaires (annuelles typiquement). Sprint 16 codifie cas 4.7.2.2 actuel mais futures circulaires modifieront. Solution : `AcapsComplianceValidatorService` version-aware (`validate(declaration, version: '2024' | '2025'...)`), default version current, evolutions via nouveau version+adapter sans breaking changes existing.

---

## LISTE DES 12 TACHES -- SCOPE DETAILLE

### Tache 5.1.1 -- Architecture Base + Decision-017 + Design Doc Sinistre Lifecycle

**Effort** : 5 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : Phase 3 closure | **Densite cible** : 110-150 KB

Cette task ratifie decision-017 (state machine 8 etats + 12 transitions valides) + redige design doc 20+ pages avec diagrams state machine + integration sagas + ACAPS 4.7.2.2 compliance + peer-review obligatoire.

Livrables : fichier `decisions/017-sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine.md`, fichier `docs/architecture/sinistre-foundation-design.md` design doc 20+ pages avec state machine diagram Mermaid + sequence diagrams sagas integration + ACAPS 4.7.2.2 compliance matrix + risk analysis, fichier `00-pilotage/reports/phase-4-kickoff-report.md` rapport ouverture Phase 4 Sinistre Core, peer-review Saad CTO + Abla CEO signatures audit trail.

### Tache 5.1.2 -- Entites Sinistre + RLS + Indexes Performance

**Effort** : 6 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : 5.1.1 | **Densite cible** : 130-170 KB

Cette task implemente les 6 entites domain Sinistre + relations + RLS multi-tenant + indexes performance critiques.

Livrables : package `@insurtech/sinistre` skeleton, migration `CreateSinistreEntities` avec 5 tables (`sinistres` aggregate root, `sinistre_lines` multi-line claims, `sinistre_lifecycle_events` audit append-only partition mensuelle, `sinistre_documents` references Sprint 10), entites TypeORM completes avec relations OneToMany/ManyToOne, RLS multi-tenant active toutes tables, enums TypeScript `SinistreStatus` 8 valeurs + `SinistreType` 8 valeurs + `SinistreBranch` 12 valeurs, indexes performance critiques (tenant_id+status partial, tenant_id+customer_id_sprint8, tenant_id+declaration_date DESC, correlation_id pour saga tracking, sinistre_id+timestamp pour audit queries), seeds initial 8 SinistreType + 12 SinistreBranch, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 5.1.3 -- State Machine 8 Etats + 12 Transitions Validees

**Effort** : 7 heures | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE LIFECYCLE | **Dependances** : 5.1.2 | **Densite cible** : 140-180 KB

Cette task implemente le state machine 8 etats avec 12 transitions valides whitelist strict + 4+ transitions invalides explicit blocked + trigger Postgres defense en profondeur.

Livrables : module `state-machine/sinistre-state-machine.ts` defines 12 transitions matrix avec validators per transition, service `SinistreLifecycleService.transition(sinistreId, toStatus, reason, actorId, metadata?): LifecycleResult` execute (SELECT FOR UPDATE sinistre + check valid transition via matrix + INSERT lifecycle_event audit + UPDATE status + emit Kafka event + COMMIT atomic), service `SinistreLifecycleService.getValidTransitions(currentStatus): NextStatus[]` retourne possibles next states, helper `TransitionValidators` per transition specific validators (e.g., expertise_requested requires expert_id in metadata), trigger Postgres `enforce_status_transition` defense en profondeur (cas direct UPDATE bypass service), 18+ tests unitaires couvrant 12 transitions valides + 4+ invalides + edge cases (concurrent transitions, missing actor, invalid status from finalized).

### Tache 5.1.4 -- ACAPS Compliance Validator + Cas Usage 4.7.2.2

**Effort** : 6 heures | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE | **Dependances** : 5.1.3 | **Densite cible** : 130-170 KB

Cette task implemente `AcapsComplianceValidatorService` avec validations strict cas usage 4.7.2.2 + tests exhaustifs scenarios reglementaires.

Livrables : service `AcapsComplianceValidatorService.validate(declaration: SinistreDeclaration, version: '2024'): ComplianceResult { valid, errors, warnings }` orchestrate (champs obligatoires complet + format CIN MA strict 8 chars regex + format plaque AAA-NNN-LL regex + date_sinistre range max 1 an retroactif + montant range 100-10M MAD + photos minimum 3 documents Sprint 10 references + constat_amiable required si type=auto + lookup customer_id Sprint 8 + lookup police_id Sprint 17 future ou Sprint 14 SEL), version-aware structure pour evolution circulaires futures, telemetry `acaps_validation_failures_total{error_type}`, helper exhaustif `errorCodes` (ACAPS_001 missing_cin, ACAPS_002 invalid_cin_format, ACAPS_003 missing_police, ..., ACAPS_020+), 14+ tests scenarios reels (CIN valide, CIN invalide 7 chars, plaque invalide format, montant hors range, photos insuffisantes, constat manquant auto, etc).

### Tache 5.1.5 -- SinistreService CRUD + Validations Schemas Avro

**Effort** : 7 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : 5.1.4 | **Densite cible** : 130-170 KB

Cette task implemente service `SinistreService` CRUD complet avec validations Zod schemas canonical Avro sinistre.v1 Sprint 15 SRF.

Livrables : service `SinistreService.create(declaration): Sinistre` (ACAPS pre-flight via 5.1.4 + Avro schema sinistre.v1 validation via Sprint 15 Schema Registry + INSERT sinistre + INSERT lines + emit Kafka declaration_submitted + return entity), `SinistreService.update(id, partial)` (validations + transition lifecycle si status change + audit), `SinistreService.findById(id, includeHistory=false)`, `SinistreService.findByCustomer(customerId, filters)`, `SinistreService.findByCorrelation(correlationId)` saga tracking, validations Zod entree+sortie strict via canonical schemas, integration Sprint 8 CRM CustomerService pour customer lookup (CIN decryption on-demand), integration Sprint 15 SRF Schema Registry pour Avro validation, telemetry `sinistre_created_total`, `sinistre_validation_failures_total{error_type}`, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 5.1.6 -- Integration Saga declare-claim Sprint 15 SRF

**Effort** : 7 heures | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE | **Dependances** : 5.1.5 | **Densite cible** : 140-180 KB

Cette task integre le saga declare-claim 4 etapes Sprint 15 SRF dans le workflow declaration sinistre. Validation pratique de l'investissement Phase 3 (SEL+SRF).

Livrables : service `SinistreService.declareClaim(declaration, idempotencyKey?): SinistreDeclarationResult` orchestrate (ACAPS validation pre-flight + INSERT sinistre status='declaration' + generate correlation_id UUID v7 + `SagaOrchestratorService.startSaga('declare-claim', { sinistre_id, customer_id, vehicle_info, montant_estime, idempotency_key, correlation_id })` Sprint 15 4.2.8 + return acknowledgement avec saga_id), saga steps consume real Sprint 16 entities (step3 submit_claim_to_insurer via SEL Sprint 14 with canonical schema declaration_sinistre.v1 Sprint 15, retourne acknowledgement par insurer trigger transition declaration->received), error handling (saga step fail trigger compensation cancel_claim_at_insurer + sinistre transition received->declaration backward + notification customer), tests integration end-to-end saga + entities + lifecycle, 14+ tests integration couvrant happy path + step failures + compensations + idempotency retries.

### Tache 5.1.7 -- SinistreEventEmitter + Kafka Events Lifecycle

**Effort** : 5 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : 5.1.6 | **Densite cible** : 110-150 KB

Cette task implemente `SinistreEventEmitter` publish Kafka events sur chaque transition lifecycle consume par Sprint 9 + Sprint 13 + Sprint 15 sagas.

Livrables : service `SinistreEventEmitter.emit(event: SinistreLifecycleKafkaEvent): void` publie Kafka topic `insurtech.events.sinistre.{event_type}` avec 8 topics distincts per status, event schema canonical Avro saga_state.v1 Sprint 15, transactional outbox pattern strict (INSERT outbox dans transaction Postgres lifecycle + publisher worker async + idempotent via event_id UUID), integration Sprint 9 NotificationRouter listeners 8 topics (email + push customer + agent tenant template per transition), integration Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline `sinistre-pipeline.ts` consume topics + sync ClickHouse, integration Sprint 15 sagas listeners specifiques (approve-payout saga listen payout_decided trigger automatically, finalize-claim saga listen repair_completed), telemetry `sinistre_events_published_total{event_type,status}`, 12+ tests integration.

### Tache 5.1.8 -- SinistreDocument References Sprint 10 + Photos Upload

**Effort** : 5 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : 5.1.7 | **Densite cible** : 110-150 KB

Livrables : entite `SinistreDocument` (id + sinistre_id + document_id_sprint10 reference + document_type enum constat_amiable/photo_dommage/photo_lieu/expertise_rapport/facture_garage/expertise_complementaire/autre + uploaded_at + uploaded_by_id + ordre_affichage), service `SinistreDocumentService.attachDocument(sinistreId, documentId, type, actorId)` via Sprint 10 DocumentService lookup + verify document exists + INSERT reference + emit Kafka event, service `SinistreDocumentService.listDocuments(sinistreId): SinistreDocument[]`, validation type-specific (constat_amiable accepts PDF only, photos accept JPG/PNG/HEIC, factures accept PDF), permission Sprint 7.5a `sinistre.document.attach`, 12+ tests integration.

### Tache 5.1.9 -- Endpoints REST Sinistres

**Effort** : 6 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : 5.1.8 | **Densite cible** : 130-170 KB

Cette task expose endpoints REST `/api/v1/sinistres` complets consumables par Sprint 18 Customer Portal + Sprint 19 Mobile + Sprint 26 Admin UI.

Livrables : controller `sinistre.controller.ts` avec 12 endpoints (`POST /api/v1/sinistres` create declaration declenche saga + return acknowledgement, `GET /api/v1/sinistres` list paginated filterable status/type/customer/period, `GET /api/v1/sinistres/{id}` details complets + lifecycle events history, `GET /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/lifecycle-events` history paginated, `POST /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/transition` manual transition specific status reason + actorId, `POST /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/documents` attach document Sprint 10 reference, `GET /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/documents` list documents attached, `POST /api/v1/sinistres/{id}/acaps-compliance-check` pre-flight validation cas 4.7.2.2, `GET /api/v1/sinistres/by-correlation/{correlationId}` saga tracking, `GET /api/v1/sinistres/statistics` summary metrics tenant, `POST /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/manual-compensate` super-admin force compensation lifecycle, `DELETE /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/pseudonymize` super-admin GDPR/CNDP customer right erasure), OpenAPI 3.1 schemas via decorateurs NestJS, validation Zod via canonical schemas Sprint 15, permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires (`sinistre.read/create/update/transition`, `sinistre.document.attach`, `sinistre.admin.compensate`, `sinistre.admin.pseudonymize`), rate limit 100 req/min/tenant, 16+ tests E2E.

Livrables : service `SinistrePseudonymizationService.pseudonymizeCustomer(customerId, requesterId, reason): PseudonymizationResult` (verify Sprint 8 CustomerService customer deletion_request exists, hash CIN -> sha256 unique identifier, replace customer_id with pseudonymous_id dans sinistres + lines + lifecycle_events + documents, audit log GDPR-event dedicated, preserve data structure operational pour ACAPS audit 10 ans + 1 jour), endpoint admin `DELETE /api/v1/admin/sinistres/{id}/pseudonymize` (super-admin Skalean only + audit critical + co-signature Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow 2 personnes minimum), idempotent (re-run safe), telemetry `pseudonymization_events_total`, 10+ tests integration.

## TESTS REQUIS NIVEAU SPRINT

Tests unitaires minimum 130 cumules, coverage 90% lignes + 85% branches. Tests integration minimum 50 cumules. Tests E2E minimum 30 scenarios. Tests securite : multi-tenant cross-tenant isolation, CIN encryption preservation, lifecycle transitions bypass via direct UPDATE blocked. Tests charge : 1000 sinistres concurrent creation + lifecycle, P95 endpoints < 500ms. Tests compliance : ACAPS 4.7.2.2 scenarios reels exhaustifs.

## 19. B-17-sprint-17-police-lifecycle.md

```md
# B-17 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 17 : POLICE LIFECYCLE

> ## CORRECTION v3.1 (2026-06-02) -- PRIORITAIRE SUR LE RESTE DU FICHIER
>
> Ce fichier a ete genere avant le pivot v3.1 (ecosysteme 7 acteurs, decision-012-ecosysteme-7-acteurs). En cas de divergence, ces regles PRIMENT sur toute mention residuelle plus bas (qui parle encore de 6 acteurs, 26 roles, 7 types cross-tenant, 130 permissions, PartsHub, ou d'un e-constat genere/signe par Assurflow) :
>
> - Ecosysteme a 7 acteurs : Broker, Garage, Carrier, Expert, Customer/Assure, Tow, et CONSTATEUR (NOUVEAU v3.1).
> - Tenancy 3 niveaux : N1 tenants dedies (Garage, Broker, Carrier, Expert) ; N2 rattache (Customer/Assure) ; N3 sans tenant, acteur reseau leger style Uber-driver (Tow, Constateur).
> - RBAC : 28 roles (retrait de garage_parts_manager car PartsHub abandonne ; ajout de constateur_admin, constateur_dispatcher, constateur_agent).
> - Cross-tenant : 8 types (ajout de claim_to_constateur_dispatch). La ressource parts_order est remplacee par constat.
> - Permissions : 131 (module parts retire ~7 ; module constateur ajoute ~8).
> - PartsHub (decision-014) est ABANDONNE : il ne doit plus apparaitre comme fonctionnalite active.
> - e-constat : Assurflow ne cree, ne remplit ni ne signe JAMAIS le constat. Le constat est gere par l'application TIERCE e-constat de l'ecosysteme AFMA (saisie, signatures conformes loi 43-20, emission d'un numero officiel) ; Assurflow recupere les donnees du constat via API a partir de ce numero.

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 4 Sprint 2, design from scratch)
**Phase** : 4 -- Sinistre Core (Sprints 16-20)
**Sprint** : 17 / 37 -- Phase 4 Sprint 2 (POLICE LIFECYCLE)
**Position** : Apres Sinistre Foundation (Sprint 16), avant Web Customer Portal (Sprint 18)
**Numerotation taches** : 5.2.1 a 5.2.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~78 heures developpement
**Cadence** : 1-2 jours avec Claude Code
**Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE METIER (foundation Police, depend Sprint 16)
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Police Lifecycle** domain model + workflow long-terme, deuxieme sprint vertical Phase 4 apres Sinistre Foundation. Le sprint construit le **domain model Police d'assurance** avec ses entites principales (Police aggregate root, PoliceAdhesion multi-personnes, PoliceCoverage garanties + plafonds + franchises, PoliceBranch branches couvertes, PoliceLifecycleEvent audit), le **state machine lifecycle 5 etats** long-terme avec timer-driven renewal (draft -> active -> renewal -> expired, plus cancelled side-state), l'**integration sagas Sprint 15 SRF** pour workflows souscription nouvelle police + renouvellement annuel, la **compliance ACAPS Circulaire 02-AS/2024** cas d'usage 4.7.2.1 souscription + 4.7.2.3 renouvellement conforme reglementation marocaine, l'**implementation PoliceLinkInterface Sprint 16** pour validation coverage (police active + branche match + montant dans plafond), les **timers cron** declenchent renewal automatique 1 mois avant expiration + alertes 7j avant expiration + expiration automatique post date_fin, et les **endpoints REST** consommables par Sprint 18 Web Customer Portal + Sprint 19 Mobile Assure.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un domain model Police production-grade avec 6 entites principales TypeORM + RLS multi-tenant strict, d'un service `PoliceLifecycleService` avec state machine 5 etats + transitions validees + integration timer cron renewal, d'un service `PoliceService` CRUD complet avec validations Zod schemas canonical Avro police.v1 Sprint 15, d'un service `AcapsPoliceComplianceValidatorService` pre-flight check souscription cas 4.7.2.1 (champs obligatoires + format CIN customer + numero police unicite + branches valides + prime range coherent + date_debut/date_fin range coherent) et renouvellement cas 4.7.2.3 (continuite + prime evolution justifiee + branches preserved ou notification customer), l'implementation `PoliceLinkInterfaceImpl` Sprint 16 dependency (methodes `linkPolice(sinistreId, policeId)` + `validatePoliceCoverage(sinistreId, policeId): CoverageValidationResult`) verify police active OR renewal + branche sinistre coverage match + montant dans limites couverture + franchise applicable, le `PoliceEventEmitter` publish Kafka events sur chaque transition lifecycle (`police.draft_created`, `police.activated`, `police.renewal_initiated`, `police.renewed`, `police.expired`, `police.cancelled`) consume Sprint 9 NotificationRouter + Sprint 13 Analytics + Sprint 16 Sinistre future, le **PoliceRenewalCronService** scheduled job daily check polices avec expiration_date < now + 30j -> trigger renewal saga, des endpoints REST `/api/v1/polices` avec permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires + OpenAPI 3.1, le **cross-tenant police lookup via SEL Sprint 14** (insurer.policy.lookup endpoint consume Sprint 16 SinistreService.declareClaim), et la suite de tests E2E avec 30 scenarios couvrant souscription full + renouvellement full + cancellation + integration Sprint 16 coverage validation + cross-tenant lookup + timer cron simulation.

Le sprint **complete Phase 4 foundation** : Sprint 16 (Sinistre) + Sprint 17 (Police) constituent ensemble le domain model assurance complet. Sprints 18-20 suivants utilisent ces foundations pour delivrer UX assures + IA estimation.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 17

Le sprint construit le domain model Police + lifecycle complete + integration ecosystem. Concretement, il livre la **decision-018 ratification** documentant Police lifecycle 5 etats + coverage validation cross-branch + ACAPS souscription + renouvellement, les **6 entites domain Police** TypeORM avec relations + RLS multi-tenant + indexes performance (`Police` aggregate root avec id + tenant_id + customer_id_sprint8 + insurer_partner_id FK Sprint 14 Service Registry + police_number UNIQUE per insurer + police_type enum auto/dommages/multirisques/sante/responsabilite_civile/decennale/transport_marchandises + status enum 5 valeurs draft/active/renewal/expired/cancelled + souscription_date DATE + expiration_date DATE + renewal_date_target DATE auto-calculated + prime_annuelle_mad NUMERIC(15,2) MAD + previous_police_id nullable reference renewal predecessor + correlation_id UUID saga + created_at + updated_at + version, `PoliceAdhesion` multi-personnes coverage avec id + police_id FK + customer_id_sprint8 reference + role enum souscripteur/conjoint/enfant/parent_a_charge/employe_couvert + nom_complet + date_naissance + cin_pseudonymous nullable apres pseudonymisation, `PoliceCoverage` garanties specifiques avec id + police_id + garantie_code enum RC_obligatoire/DC_dommages/vol/incendie/bris_glace/RC_familiale/exclusion_specifique + plafond_mad NUMERIC(15,2) + franchise_mad NUMERIC(15,2) + exclusions TEXT + ordre_affichage, `PoliceBranch` branches couvertes via association table police_id + branch enum 12 valeurs Sprint 16 SinistreBranch reutilise, `PoliceLifecycleEvent` audit append-only partition mensuelle avec id + police_id + from_status + to_status + transition_reason + actor_id + actor_type enum + timestamp + metadata JSONB + correlation_id, `PoliceRenewalRequest` historique renouvellements avec id + previous_police_id + new_police_id + renewal_initiated_date + renewal_completed_date nullable + prime_evolution_mad NUMERIC(15,2) signed + branches_changes JSONB), le **service `PoliceLifecycleService`** orchestrate state machine 5 etats avec methodes `transition(policeId, toStatus, reason, actorId): LifecycleResult`, `getValidTransitions(currentStatus): NextStatus[]`, `getHistory(policeId)`, le **state machine 5 etats** strict avec transitions valides documentees (draft -> active souscription validee, active -> renewal timer 1 mois avant expiration, renewal -> active renouvellement valide, renewal -> expired timeout sans renouvellement, active -> cancelled resiliation, active -> expired date_fin atteinte sans renewal, draft -> cancelled abandon souscription), le **service `PoliceService`** CRUD complet avec validations Zod schemas canonical Avro police.v1 Sprint 15 SRF, le **service `AcapsPoliceComplianceValidatorService`** pre-flight check :
- Cas 4.7.2.1 souscription (champs CIN customer Sprint 8 + numero_police format + insurer_partner_id valid + branches array non vide + coverages array non vide + prime_annuelle range 100 MAD - 1M MAD + date_debut today or future + date_fin > date_debut + duree 12 mois standard + max 36 mois exceptional)
- Cas 4.7.2.3 renouvellement (previous_police_id valid + status='active' or 'renewal' + continuity_check expiration_date previous - 1j == souscription_date new + prime evolution justified si > 20% increase + branches preserved or customer notified Sprint 9)

L'**implementation `PoliceLinkInterfaceImpl`** Sprint 16 5.1.11 dependency :
- `linkPolice(sinistreId, policeId): void` valide police existe + tenant scope + INSERT sinistre.police_id reference
- `validatePoliceCoverage(sinistreId, policeId): CoverageValidationResult` retourne `{ valid, errors, applicable_coverages, franchise_applicable, plafond_remaining }` orchestrate :
  1. Verify police status in ['active', 'renewal']
  2. Verify sinistre.declaration_date in [police.souscription_date, police.expiration_date]
  3. Verify sinistre.sinistre_branch in police.branches[] array
  4. Verify sinistre.montant_estime <= sum(applicable_coverages.plafond_mad) - franchise
  5. Return applicable coverages + remaining plafond annuel (deduct previous claims same year)

Le **`PoliceEventEmitter`** publish Kafka events 6 topics distincts (`insurtech.events.police.{draft_created,activated,renewal_initiated,renewed,expired,cancelled}`) consume Sprint 9 NotificationRouter (templates per transition souscription confirmee, renouvellement disponible J-30, renouvellement effectue, expiration alerte J-7, expiration effective, resiliation) + Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline + Sprint 16 Sinistre future listening police.expired pour rejet declaration future, le **`PoliceRenewalCronService`** scheduled job daily 02h00 UTC (Skalean MA quiet hours) : query polices status='active' AND expiration_date BETWEEN now() AND now() + 30 days -> trigger renewal saga via Sprint 15 SagaOrchestrator -> sinistre lifecycle transition active -> renewal + publish event renewal_initiated + Sprint 9 notification customer "Votre police arrive a expiration dans 30 jours, renouvellement disponible", deuxieme cron job daily 02h30 query polices status='active' AND expiration_date < now() -> auto-transition expired si pas dans renewal status (timeout protection), troisieme cron job daily 03h00 query polices status='renewal' AND renewal_initiated > 30 days ago AND new_police_id IS NULL -> auto-transition expired (renewal abandoned by customer).

Les **endpoints REST** complets `/api/v1/polices` (POST create police draft + activate + renewal + cancel + 8 endpoints CRUD complets) avec permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires + OpenAPI 3.1 + rate limit 100 req/min/tenant, le **cross-tenant police lookup via SEL Sprint 14** : implementation endpoint `insurer.policy.lookup` exposed via Sprint 14 Security Server consume par Sprint 16 SinistreService.declareClaim step2 lookup_policy_at_insurer (canonical schema police_lookup.v1 Sprint 15 -> response police.v1 canonical + coverages array + active status), 30+ tests E2E couvrant lifecycle complete + integration sagas + ACAPS validation + cross-tenant lookup + timer cron simulation + integration Sprint 16 coverage validation.

### Exclus du Sprint 17 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas l'**UX souscription customer self-service** (frontend) qui est defere Sprint 18 Web Customer Portal (Sprint 17 fait backend domain model + endpoints REST consume par futur frontend), l'**UX renouvellement mobile-first one-click** differe Sprint 19 Web Assure Portal Mobile (Sprint 17 backend supporte renouvellement programmatic mais UX customer differe), les **calculs primes IA dynamiques** (tarification ML based customer profile + historique sinistres) differes Phase 7+ (Sprint 17 utilise primes definies insurer + customer accept/refuse, pas calculation engine), les **commissions courtiers / brokers** workflow paiement differes Sprint 30+ (Sprint 17 supporte broker_id reference dans Police entity mais workflow commission paiement future), les **integrations vraies compagnies marocaines** (Wafa Assurance, Saham, AtlantaSanad) differees Sprint 32 (Sprint 17 utilise fictional insurers AssurMaghreb/Atlas/MediAssur Sprint 14), les **workflows endossements** (modifications mid-contract police active) differes Phase 7+ (Sprint 17 supporte version BIGINT optimistic locking mais endossement workflow specifique differe), les **resiliations preavis 60 jours** workflow ACAPS-conforme defere Phase 7+ (Sprint 17 supporte cancellation immediate + 30j notice configurable, full resiliation reglementaire 60j workflow differe), les **anciens contracts migration** (existing portefeuille insurer importe Skalean) differes Sprint 32 (Sprint 17 cree polices nouvelles via API, import bulk batch defere), et les **calculs sinistralite ratio S/P** (sinistres/primes per customer + branche + portefeuille) differes Sprint 28 Admin Reports Compliance (Sprint 17 events emission consume par Sprint 13 ETL, ratios dashboards Sprint 28).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 17 est le **second pilier de Phase 4** apres Sinistre Foundation Sprint 16. Quatre dimensions strategiques justifient l'investissement de 78 heures sur ce sprint critique.

Premierement, Police et Sinistre forment un **couple indissociable** dans le metier assurance. Un sinistre sans police = declaration impossible (rejet ACAPS). Une police sans sinistre = pas de business case. Sprint 16 a livre Sinistre Foundation avec `PoliceLinkInterface` defini en interface stable. Sprint 17 livre l'implementation reelle. Sans Sprint 17, Sprint 16 fonctionne avec polices mockees uniquement -- pas viable Demo Day. Le **cross-link Police-Sinistre** via `validatePoliceCoverage` est le **pont metier critique** entre les deux verticals.

Deuxiemement, Police Lifecycle est **fondamentalement different de Sinistre Lifecycle**. Sinistre = workflow event-driven court terme (declaration -> finalized en 30 jours - 1 an). Police = workflow time-driven long terme (souscription -> renouvellement annuel x N annees = 1 - 30 ans typical). Cette difference fondamentale impose pattern architectural distinct : Sprint 17 introduit **timer-driven workflows** avec PoliceRenewalCronService scheduled jobs daily, alors que Sprint 16 utilise event-driven Kafka. Pattern industrie standard (Stripe subscriptions, Salesforce renewals, AWS reserved instances) tous utilisent timers cron pour lifecycle long terme.

Troisiemement, la **compliance ACAPS 4.7.2.1 souscription + 4.7.2.3 renouvellement** est l'autre moitie de Programme Emergence ACAPS Skalean apres 4.7.2.2 Sprint 16. Le Programme Emergence requires demonstration capabilities exhaustives across declaration sinistre + souscription + renouvellement. Sans 4.7.2.1 + 4.7.2.3, le dossier ACAPS d'Assurflow reste incomplet. Sprint 17 livre AcapsPoliceComplianceValidatorService pre-flight check souscription (champs obligatoires + format CIN + numero police unicite + branches valides + prime range coherent + dates coherentes) et renouvellement (continuite + prime evolution justifiee + branches preserved ou notification customer Sprint 9).

Quatriemement, le **cross-tenant Police lookup via SEL Sprint 14** est le second proof point critique de l'investissement Phase 3 apres saga declare-claim Sprint 16. Sprint 17 expose endpoint `insurer.policy.lookup` via Sprint 14 Security Server consume par Sprint 16 SinistreService.declareClaim step2. Si cross-tenant lookup casse, Sinistre declaration ne peut pas valider police existence cote insurer = workflow declare-claim broken. Test integration end-to-end Sprint 16 saga step2 + Sprint 17 cross-tenant endpoint valide architecture distribuee Skalean.

---

## ARCHITECTURE CIBLE -- POLICE LIFECYCLE DOMAIN MODEL

### Package @insurtech/police

```
repo/packages/police/
├── package.json                       # @insurtech/police v1.0.0
├── src/
│   ├── entities/
│   │   ├── police.entity.ts           # Aggregate root
│   │   ├── police-adhesion.entity.ts

│   │   ├── police-lifecycle-event.entity.ts
│   │   ├── police-renewal-request.entity.ts

│   │       ├── police-status.enum.ts        # 5 etats
│   │       ├── police-type.enum.ts          # types
│   │       ├── garantie-code.enum.ts        # garanties
│   │       └── adhesion-role.enum.ts        # roles family
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── police.service.ts                # CRUD
│   │   ├── police-lifecycle.service.ts      # State machine
│   │   ├── acaps-police-compliance-validator.service.ts
│   │   ├── police-event-emitter.service.ts
│   │   ├── police-renewal-cron.service.ts   # Scheduled job
│   │   └── police-link-interface-impl.service.ts  # Sprint 16 dependency
│   ├── state-machine/
│   │   ├── police-state-machine.ts
│   │   └── transition-validators.ts
│   ├── sagas/
│   │   └── police-renewal.saga.ts           # Sprint 15 saga
│   ├── controllers/
│   │   ├── police.controller.ts
│   │   └── policy-lookup-sel.controller.ts  # Cross-tenant SEL
│   └── public.ts                            # Public interfaces Sprint 18+
└── tests/
```

### State machine 5 etats avec transitions valides

```
                                ┌───────┐
                                │ draft │ (initial - police negotiation)
                                └───┬───┘
                          ┌─────────┤
                          │         │ activation souscription validee
                  cancellation       ▼
                          ▼     ┌──────────┐
                     ┌────────┐ │  active  │ (police effective)
                     │cancelled│ └─────┬────┘
                     └────────┘       │
                                       │ timer 1 mois avant expiration
                                       ▼
                                ┌──────────┐
                          ┌─────│ renewal  │
                          │     └─────┬────┘
                          │           │
                renouvellement      timeout
                validee 30j      sans renewal
                          ▼           ▼
                    ┌──────────┐  ┌─────────┐
                    │ active   │  │ expired │
                    └──────────┘  └─────────┘
                          ▲              │
                          │              │
                     active -- expiration_date atteinte -> expired
```

**8 transitions valides** :

| # | From | To | Trigger | Actor |
|---|------|-----|---------|-------|
| 1 | draft | active | Souscription validee + payment received | tenant_agent / system_saga |
| 2 | draft | cancelled | Abandon souscription customer | customer / tenant_agent |
| 3 | active | renewal | Timer cron 1 mois avant expiration | system_cron |
| 4 | renewal | active | Renouvellement valide + payment received | tenant_agent / system_saga |
| 5 | renewal | expired | Timeout 30j sans renouvellement | system_cron |
| 6 | active | expired | Expiration_date atteinte + pas dans renewal | system_cron |
| 7 | active | cancelled | Resiliation customer ou insurer | customer / insurer / tenant_agent |
| 8 | renewal | cancelled | Resiliation pendant renouvellement | customer / tenant_agent |

**Transitions invalides explicit blocked (4 exemples)** :
- expired -> active direct (besoin renewal workflow)
- cancelled -> active direct (police annulee immutable, nouvelle souscription requise)
- draft -> renewal direct (pas encore active, can't renew)
- expired -> cancelled (deja terminal, no-op)

### Decision-018 -- Police lifecycle state machine + coverage validation

**Justification** :
- (a) Police lifecycle long-terme require timer-driven workflows distinct event-driven Sinistre Sprint 16
- (b) Coverage validation cross-branch matching critique integration Sprint 16 sinistre declaration
- (c) ACAPS 4.7.2.1 souscription + 4.7.2.3 renouvellement compliance requise Programme Emergence
- (d) Cross-tenant police lookup via SEL Sprint 14 proof point distribute architecture

**Architecture** :
- State machine 5 etats strict + 8 transitions valides + 4+ invalides blocked
- Timer cron daily 3 jobs (renewal initiation 30j avant, expiration check, renewal abandoned)
- Coverage validation algorithm 5 checks sequentiels (status + dates + branche + montant + franchise)
- Cross-tenant endpoint exposed Sprint 14 SEL Security Server

### Integration Sprint 16 -- PoliceLinkInterface implementation

```typescript
@Injectable()
export class PoliceLinkInterfaceImpl implements PoliceLinkInterface {
  // Defined in Sprint 16 5.1.11
  async linkPolice(sinistreId: string, policeId: string): Promise<void> {
    const police = await this.policeService.findById(policeId);
    if (!police) throw new PoliceNotFoundError(policeId);
    if (police.status === 'cancelled') throw new PoliceCancelledError(policeId);
    await this.sinistreService.updatePoliceReference(sinistreId, policeId);
    await this.eventEmitter.emit('police_linked_to_sinistre', { sinistreId, policeId });
  }

  async validatePoliceCoverage(sinistreId: string, policeId: string): Promise<CoverageValidationResult> {
    const police = await this.policeService.findById(policeId, { includeCoverages: true });
    const sinistre = await this.sinistreService.findById(sinistreId);
    
    // Check 1: Police active or renewal
    if (!['active', 'renewal'].includes(police.status)) {
      return { valid: false, errors: ['POLICE_NOT_ACTIVE'], ... };
    }
    
    // Check 2: Date range
    if (sinistre.declaration_date < police.souscription_date || sinistre.declaration_date > police.expiration_date) {
      return { valid: false, errors: ['DATE_OUT_OF_RANGE'], ... };
    }
    
    // Check 3: Branche match
    if (!police.branches.includes(sinistre.sinistre_branch)) {
      return { valid: false, errors: ['BRANCH_NOT_COVERED'], ... };
    }
    
    // Check 4: Montant dans plafond
    const applicableCoverages = police.coverages.filter(c => c.applies_to_branch(sinistre.sinistre_branch));
    const plafondTotal = applicableCoverages.reduce((sum, c) => sum + c.plafond_mad, 0);
    const previousClaimsThisYear = await this.getPreviousClaimsAmount(policeId, police.souscription_date);
    const plafondRemaining = plafondTotal - previousClaimsThisYear;
    
    if (sinistre.montant_estime > plafondRemaining) {
      return { valid: false, errors: ['MONTANT_EXCEEDS_PLAFOND'], applicableCoverages, plafondRemaining };
    }
    
    // Check 5: Franchise applicable
    const franchiseApplicable = applicableCoverages.reduce((max, c) => Math.max(max, c.franchise_mad), 0);
    
    return {
      valid: true,
      errors: [],
      applicableCoverages,
      plafondRemaining,
      franchiseApplicable
    };
  }
}
```

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

**Decision-001 monorepo-structure** : nouveau package `repo/packages/police/`.

**Decision-002 multi-tenant-3-niveaux** : 6 entites Police + Adhesion + Coverage + Branches + LifecycleEvent + RenewalRequest ont `tenant_id UUID NOT NULL` RLS active.

**Decision-003 typeorm-vs-prisma** : TypeORM 0.3.x.

**Decision-004 kafka-vs-rabbitmq** : events police publies via Kafka topics `insurtech.events.police.{draft_created,activated,renewal_initiated,renewed,expired,cancelled}` consume Sprint 9 + Sprint 13 + Sprint 16.

**Decision-005 skalean-ai-frontier** : aucun IA. Tarification IA Phase 7+.

**Decision-006 no-emoji-policy** : strict.

**Decision-007 ai-3-deferred-sprints** : Sprint 30+.

**Decision-008 data-residency-maroc** : strict. Donnees police Atlas Cloud Benguerir.

**Decision-009 signature-loi-43-20** : souscription police signee via Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow customer + agent tenant.

**Decision-010 atlas-cloud-souverain** : strict.

**Decision-011 pci-dss-no-card-storage** : paiement prime via Sprint 11 (no card data).

**Decision-012 payout-instant-mwallet-bam** : remboursement prime cancellation via Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.

**Decision-013 dgi-efacturation-2026** : factures primes annuelles via Sprint 12 + DGI Sprint 14 SEL.

**Decision-014 clickhouse-olap-separation** : events police ETL Sprint 13 vers ClickHouse.

**Decision-015 skalean-exchange-layer-xroad** : SEL transport pour cross-tenant police lookup (insurer.policy.lookup endpoint exposed).

**Decision-016 srf-schema-registry-translation** : SRF Translation Engine pour format partner-canonical police.v1 + sagas renouvellement + idempotency keys.

**Decision-017 sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine** : reference pour pattern state machine + audit log append-only.

**Decision-018 police-lifecycle-state-machine** (NOUVEAU - ratification 2 juin 2026) : adoption **state machine 5 etats** Police lifecycle long-terme avec **timer-driven workflows** (PoliceRenewalCronService scheduled jobs daily) + **coverage validation cross-branch matching** integration Sprint 16 + **ACAPS compliance 4.7.2.1 souscription + 4.7.2.3 renouvellement**. Justification : (a) Police lifecycle long-terme distinct event-driven Sinistre, (b) Coverage validation cross-branch critique Sprint 16 integration, (c) ACAPS compliance Programme Emergence ACAPS, (d) Cross-tenant lookup proof point Sprint 14 SEL architecture. Architecture : enum `PoliceStatus` 5 valeurs + 8 transitions valides whitelist + 4+ invalides blocked + 3 cron jobs daily + coverage validation algorithm 5 checks + AcapsPoliceValidator version-aware. Integration : Sprint 16 PoliceLinkInterfaceImpl, Sprint 14 SEL endpoint insurer.policy.lookup, Sprint 15 sagas renouvellement.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege transversal 1 : Timer cron race conditions on renewal initiation.** Cron daily 02h00 detecte 100 polices expirant 30j -> triggere 100 sagas renewal simultane mais base Postgres lock contention. Solution : cron batch process avec rate limit 10 sagas/seconde + queue Redis + Sprint 15 SagaOrchestratorService idempotency strict.

**Piege transversal 2 : Coverage validation algorithm complexity.** 5 checks sequentiels + previous claims aggregation peut etre slow (1000 polices + 10000 sinistres history). Solution : query previous_claims_amount pre-aggregated via Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialised view + cache Redis 5 min, P95 < 200ms target.

**Piege transversal 3 : Cross-tenant Police lookup via SEL latency.** Sprint 16 SinistreService.declareClaim step2 lookup_policy_at_insurer round-trip Sprint 14 SEL = 500ms - 2s. Solution : timeout configurable + circuit breaker Sprint 14 + cache Redis policy details TTL 5 min cote consumer Sinistre.

**Piege transversal 4 : Renewal saga abandoned customer state.** Customer initie renewal puis ne complete pas -> police stuck status='renewal' jusqu'a timer cron 30j expire. Solution : cron job daily 03h00 detect renewal stale > 30j + auto-transition expired + notification Sprint 9 "votre renouvellement n'a pas ete finalise".

**Piege transversal 5 : ACAPS continuity check renouvellement.** Renouvellement valid requires expiration_date previous police - 1 day == souscription_date new police strict. Si gap > 1 jour = couverture trouee = non-conforme. Solution : `ContinuityValidator.validate(previousPolice, newPolice)` strict check + throw `ContinuityGapError` si gap detected + notification Sprint 9 customer "couverture interrompue".

**Piege transversal 6 : Police number unicite cross-insurer.** Police number unique per insurer mais 2 insurers peuvent emettre meme numero. Solution : UNIQUE constraint composite (insurer_partner_id, police_number) Postgres + validation Zod cote application.

**Piege transversal 7 : Multi-tenant cross-tenant police visibility.** Customer tenant A souscrit police AssurMaghreb tenant_insurer_B = data exists both tenants. Solution : police stored insurer_tenant primary + reference customer_tenant via customer_id_sprint8, queries scoped properly + cross-tenant lookup via SEL strict.

**Piege transversal 8 : Branches array vs PoliceBranch entity.** Stockage branches couvertes = array enum column OR table association ? Solution : table `police_branches` association strict (police_id, branch enum) UNIQUE per police, queries efficient + flexible additions branches futures.

**Piege transversal 9 : Renewal prime evolution justification.** Prime augmente > 20% par rapport previous police = customer doit notifier explicit. Solution : `AcapsPoliceValidator.validateRenewal()` strict check prime evolution + si > 20% increase warning + notification customer Sprint 9 + audit log critical.

**Piege transversal 10 : PoliceCoverage exclusions ambigues.** Exclusions text libre customer/insurer interprete differently. Solution : exclusions structured JSON + standard codes (e.g., `{ exclusion_code: 'GUERRE', description: '...', severity: 'critical' }`) + audit log critical exclusions activated lors sinistre validation.

### Tache 5.2.2 -- Entites Police + RLS + Indexes

**Effort** : 6 heures | **Priorite** : P0 | **Dependances** : 5.2.1 | **Densite cible** : 130-170 KB

Cette task implemente 6 entites domain Police + relations + RLS multi-tenant + indexes performance.

Livrables : package `@insurtech/police` v1.0.0, migration `CreatePoliceEntities` avec 6 tables (`polices` aggregate root, `police_adhesions` multi-personnes, `police_coverages` garanties, `police_branches` association, `police_lifecycle_events` audit append-only partition mensuelle, `police_renewal_requests` historique), RLS multi-tenant active, enums TypeScript explicit, indexes performance critiques (tenant_id+status partial, tenant_id+customer_id_sprint8, tenant_id+expiration_date pour cron queries, insurer_partner_id+police_number UNIQUE, previous_police_id pour renewal tracking), seeds initial 7 PoliceType + 7 GaranteeCode + 5 AdhesionRole, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 5.2.3 -- State Machine 5 Etats + 8 Transitions + Timer Cron

**Effort** : 8 heures | **Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE | **Dependances** : 5.2.2 | **Densite cible** : 150-200 KB

Cette task implemente state machine 5 etats + 8 transitions valides + 4+ invalides + 3 timer cron jobs + audit trigger append-only.

Livrables : module `state-machine/police-state-machine.ts` defines VALID_TRANSITIONS matrix 8 entries + INVALID examples, service `PoliceLifecycleService.transition()` execute atomic SELECT FOR UPDATE + audit + status update + Kafka emit transactional outbox, service `PoliceLifecycleService.getValidTransitions()`, service `PoliceLifecycleService.getHistory()`, helper `TransitionValidators` per transition specific, trigger Postgres `enforce_police_status_transition` defense en profondeur + trigger `prevent_police_lifecycle_delete` append-only, **3 cron jobs daily** :
- `PoliceRenewalCronService.initiateRenewals()` daily 02h00 UTC : query polices status='active' AND expiration_date BETWEEN now() AND now() + INTERVAL '30 days' -> trigger renewal saga via Sprint 15 + transition active -> renewal + Sprint 9 notification
- `PoliceRenewalCronService.expirePolices()` daily 02h30 UTC : query polices status='active' AND expiration_date < now() AND id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM polices WHERE status='renewal') -> transition active -> expired + Sprint 9 notification
- `PoliceRenewalCronService.expireAbandonedRenewals()` daily 03h00 UTC : query polices status='renewal' AND renewal_initiated_at > now() - INTERVAL '30 days' AND new_police_id IS NULL -> transition renewal -> expired + Sprint 9 notification "renouvellement abandonne"

18+ tests unitaires + integration covering 8 transitions valides + 4+ invalides + concurrent serialised + cron jobs simulation (time-travel via @nestjs/schedule mocking).

```

```md
# B-18 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 18 : WEB CUSTOMER PORTAL

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 4 Sprint 3, design from scratch)

**Numerotation taches** : 5.3.1 a 5.3.12 (12 taches)

**Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE UX (premier visible product Demo Day)
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Web Customer Portal** Assurflow, premier sprint frontend apres 17 sprints d'infrastructure backend. Le sprint construit l'application web Next.js 14 (App Router) qui consume les APIs backend Sprint 16 (Sinistre) + Sprint 17 (Police) + Sprint 9 (Notifications) + Sprint 10 (Documents) + Sprint 11 (Payment) avec UX assures de niveau production-grade : tableau de bord polices + sinistres + notifications, page Polices avec details + coverage + renewal one-click, page Sinistres avec declaration form ACAPS-conforme + lifecycle tracker visuel + documents upload, real-time updates via Server-Sent Events sur transitions lifecycle, internationalisation FR (defaut) + AR (RTL) complete, accessibility WCAG 2.1 AA strict, et performance Core Web Vitals (LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1).

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'une application web Next.js 14 App Router avec **decision-019 ratifiee** documentant stack frontend (Next.js 14 + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui + TypeScript strict + i18n next-intl + auth NextAuth.js v5 + SSE pour real-time + WCAG 2.1 AA + Core Web Vitals strict), une **bootstrap monorepo** integration `repo/apps/customer-portal/` avec Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind + shadcn/ui configures, une **authentication JWT integration Sprint 5** avec NextAuth.js + middleware protected routes + refresh token + secure httpOnly cookies, une **internationalisation FR/AR avec RTL** via next-intl + translations completes + tests RTL layouts, un **design system** shadcn/ui customise design tokens Skalean (colors brand + typography + spacing + shadows) consume par tous composants, un **dashboard customer** affichant summary polices actives + sinistres en cours + notifications recentes + actions rapides, une **page Polices** avec list paginated + filters + details complete + coverage visualization + button renewal one-click, une **page Sinistres** avec declaration form multi-step ACAPS-conforme (cas 4.7.2.2) + lifecycle tracker visuel 8 etats + documents upload + suivi temps reel, une **real-time updates SSE** Server-Sent Events listening lifecycle transitions + toast notifications + auto-refresh data, une **documents upload** multipart vers Sprint 10 DocumentService + drag-drop + preview + types validation, une **accessibility WCAG 2.1 AA audit** complete avec automated tools (axe-core + Lighthouse) + tests Playwright + 100% sans warnings critical, et la suite de tests E2E Playwright avec 30+ scenarios couvrant authentication + dashboard + polices + sinistres + accessibility + i18n + responsive mobile.

Le sprint **inflection point UX** : transition "backend invisible" -> "produit visible customers Demo Day". La qualite UX customer determine la perception globale Assurflow. Sprint 19 (Mobile) reutilise foundation Sprint 18.

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 18 est l'**inflection point UX** d'Assurflow. Quatre dimensions strategiques justifient l'investissement de 85 heures sur ce sprint critique.

Premierement, Sprint 18 est le **premier sprint visible Demo Day**. Les 17 sprints precedents ont livre infrastructure backend invisible aux investisseurs + Programme Emergence ACAPS jury. Sprint 18 est le sprint qui transforme "Skalean a une plateforme backend" en "Skalean a un produit utilisable". Pour Demo Day 30 juin 2026 Marrakech, la qualite UX customer determine la perception globale Assurflow. Un dashboard pixel-perfect avec lifecycle tracker visuel et real-time updates marque les esprits plus que toute architecture backend.

Deuxiemement, **i18n FR/AR avec RTL strict est obligatoire** pour marche marocain. Le Maroc est officiellement bilingue arabe + francais (constitution 2011), avec arabe darija dominant communications customers. Sans support RTL strict + traductions complete + CSS logical properties, l'application est inutilisable pour majorite customers MA. WCAG 2.1 AA + RTL accessibility strict est requirement strict marche MA (pas une option).

Troisiemement, **Core Web Vitals strict est requirement Google + Demo Day perception**. Google Search depriorise sites avec LCP > 2.5s ou CLS > 0.1 (depuis 2021). Investors Demo Day connaissant Web Vitals : lentement chargement = signal startup amateur. Sprint 18 doit livrer LCP < 2.5s via SSR + image optimization + FID < 100ms via code splitting + CLS < 0.1 via skeleton loaders. Ces metriques sont mesurables objectivement et inflexibles.

Quatriemement, **integration backend Sprint 16 + 17 valide pratique l'investissement Phases 1-4**. Les 17 sprints precedents ont construit Sinistre Foundation + Police Lifecycle + sagas Sprint 15 + SEL Sprint 14 + tous modules transverses. Sans frontend qui consume tout cela, l'investissement reste theorique. Sprint 18 est le **proof point complet end-to-end** : customer ouvre dashboard -> voit polices + sinistres -> declare nouveau sinistre -> saga Sprint 15 -> SEL Sprint 14 -> insurer fictif Sprint 14 -> notification Sprint 9 -> real-time update SSE -> customer voit lifecycle progress visual. Si ce flow casse, tout l'investissement Phase 1-4 est compromis Demo Day.

---

## ARCHITECTURE CIBLE -- WEB CUSTOMER PORTAL

### Application Next.js 14 App Router

repo/apps/customer-portal/
├── package.json                       # @insurtech/customer-portal
├── next.config.mjs

├── tsconfig.json
├── middleware.ts                      # Protected routes

│   │       └── sse/lifecycle/route.ts
│   ├── components/

│   ├── i18n/
