│   │   └── config.ts
│   └── styles/
└── tests/
    ├── e2e/                           # Playwright
    └── unit/                          # Vitest
```

### Decision-019 -- Frontend Stack + i18n + Accessibility + Real-time

**Justification** :
- (a) Next.js 14 App Router : SSR/SSG mix + nested layouts + parallel routes + standard industrie 2024+
- (b) Tailwind + shadcn/ui : design system flexible + components copy-paste customizable + ownership code
- (c) i18n FR/AR RTL : marche MA obligatoire bilingue
- (d) WCAG 2.1 AA : reglementaire MA + accessibility universelle
- (e) Server-Sent Events vs WebSocket : SSE simpler + uni-directional suffit lifecycle updates
- (f) NextAuth.js v5 : production-ready + Sprint 5 JWT integration

**Architecture** :
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript strict
- Tailwind CSS 3.4 + shadcn/ui customized design tokens
- next-intl@3 i18n FR/AR avec RTL strict
- NextAuth.js v5 + Sprint 5 JWT + refresh tokens
- Server-Sent Events `/api/sse/lifecycle` consume Kafka topics
- Playwright E2E + Vitest unit + axe-core accessibility automated
- Core Web Vitals strict (Lighthouse CI score >= 95)

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

**Decision-001 monorepo-structure** : nouvelle app `repo/apps/customer-portal/`.

**Decision-002 multi-tenant-3-niveaux** : tenant_id propage via JWT + AsyncLocalStorage cote API consumption.

**Decision-003 typeorm-vs-prisma** : non applicable frontend (API consumption only).

**Decision-004 kafka-vs-rabbitmq** : SSE backend listens Kafka topics Sprint 16 + 17.

**Decision-005 skalean-ai-frontier** : aucun IA frontend Sprint 18. UX preview Sprint 20.

**Decision-006 no-emoji-policy** : strict (UI emojis bannis, icones uniquement via Lucide React).

**Decision-007 ai-3-deferred-sprints** : Sprint 30+.

**Decision-008 data-residency-maroc** : strict. CDN Atlas Cloud Casablanca pour static assets.

**Decision-009 signature-loi-43-20** : signatures cote Sprint 10 backend, frontend redirige modal Sprint 10.

**Decision-010 atlas-cloud-souverain** : strict.

**Decision-011 pci-dss-no-card-storage** : payment redirect Sprint 11 page web.

**Decision-012 payout-instant-mwallet-bam** : non applicable Sprint 18 (Sprint 11 widget).

**Decision-013 dgi-efacturation-2026** : non applicable Sprint 18.

**Decision-014 clickhouse-olap-separation** : non applicable Sprint 18.

**Decision-015 skalean-exchange-layer-xroad** : transparent frontend (API REST consumption).

**Decision-016 srf-schema-registry-translation** : transparent frontend.

**Decision-017 sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine** : utilise pour lifecycle tracker visuel 8 etats.

**Decision-018 police-lifecycle-state-machine** : utilise pour police lifecycle tracker 5 etats.

**Decision-019 frontend-stack-i18n-accessibility-realtime** (NOUVEAU - ratification 3 juin 2026) : adoption stack **Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui + TypeScript strict + next-intl FR/AR RTL + NextAuth.js v5 + Server-Sent Events + WCAG 2.1 AA + Core Web Vitals strict**. Justification : (a) Next.js 14 SSR/SSG production-ready + standard 2024, (b) i18n FR/AR obligatoire marche MA, (c) WCAG 2.1 AA reglementaire + accessibility universelle, (d) SSE simpler que WebSocket pour lifecycle uni-directional, (e) Core Web Vitals Google + Demo Day perception. Architecture : Next.js 14.2+ App Router + Tailwind 3.4+ + shadcn/ui customized + next-intl@3 + NextAuth.js v5 + Playwright + Vitest + axe-core + Lighthouse CI.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : RTL layouts cassent CSS standard.** Margin-left/right + padding-left/right cassent en RTL mode. Solution : CSS logical properties strict (margin-inline-start + padding-inline-end), Tailwind logical utilities (ms-4 instead of ml-4), tests RTL Playwright + visual regression chromatic.

**Piege 2 : Server Components vs Client Components hydratation errors.** Components Sprint 18 melangent server fetch + client interactivity = hydration mismatches. Solution : "use client" directive strict premier ligne, server components pour data fetching + client components pour interactivity, schema clair separation.

**Piege 3 : Authentication tokens expiry middle-session.** JWT expire 15min mais user actif depuis 60min = 401 errors brutals. Solution : refresh token rotation automatic via NextAuth.js v5 + interceptor axios 401 -> refresh + retry, customer experience seamless.

**Piege 4 : SSE connection drops behind proxies.** Server-Sent Events failant via Cloudflare/nginx avec timeouts 60s. Solution : heartbeat ping 30s + reconnect strategy exponential backoff + session recovery via last-event-id.

**Piege 5 : i18n keys missing fallback.** Missing translation key affiche cle technique `dashboard.welcome.title` visible. Solution : fallback FR strict si AR missing + CI hook detect missing keys + tests verify 800+ keys present both locales.

**Piege 6 : Image optimization Next.js large bundle.** Sprint 18 images photos sinistres 5MB+ slow LCP. Solution : Next.js Image component strict + sizes attribute + lazy loading + WebP/AVIF auto + CDN Atlas Cloud Casablanca cache.

**Piege 7 : Form validation client vs server divergence.** Client validation accepte input que server reject = customer frustrant. Solution : Zod schemas partages frontend/backend single source truth (via `@insurtech/shared-schemas` package).

**Piege 8 : Real-time updates excessive re-renders.** SSE event triggere re-render entire page = performance degradee. Solution : React Query/SWR cache invalidation granular + memoized selectors + immer immutable updates.

**Piege 9 : Accessibility audit false negatives.** axe-core scanner missed manual issues (focus management, color contrast complex backgrounds). Solution : manual audit + screen reader testing NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver + tester users disabled + WCAG checklist exhaustive.

**Piege 10 : Mobile responsive breakpoints inconsistent.** Designer specs en pixels figes vs reality responsive. Solution : design system breakpoints standard Tailwind (sm 640px / md 768px / lg 1024px / xl 1280px / 2xl 1536px) + container queries futures.

---
```

```md
## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Web Assure Portal Mobile** application React Native + Expo SDK 51 pour iOS App Store + Android Google Play. Le sprint construit l'application mobile native qui reutilise le design system Sprint 18 + APIs Sprint 16 (Sinistre) + Sprint 17 (Police) + Sprint 9 (Notifications) avec features mobile-specifiques : **camera native** pour capture photos sinistres in-situ + **GPS native** geolocalisation automatique declaration + **biometric authentication** FaceID/TouchID iOS + Android fingerprint + **push notifications natives** FCM Android + APNs iOS + **offline mode** SQLite local + sync on connect + **performance native** 60fps animations + bundle size optimal + startup time < 2s.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'une application mobile native production-grade avec **decision-020 ratifiee** documentant stack mobile (React Native 0.74+ + Expo SDK 51 + design system shared Sprint 18 adapted RN + biometric auth + push native + offline SQLite + Detox E2E tests + Fastlane CI/CD), une **bootstrap Expo monorepo** integration `repo/apps/mobile-portal/` avec Expo SDK 51 + TypeScript strict + EAS Build + EAS Submit configures, un **design system shared** reutilisant tokens Sprint 18 adapted React Native (StyleSheet.create + Tailwind via NativeWind + composants UI library + theme provider), une **authentication biometric** integration Sprint 5 JWT avec FaceID/TouchID via expo-local-authentication + secure storage via expo-secure-store + refresh token automatic + fallback password si biometric unavailable, une **internationalisation FR/AR mobile** via expo-localization + i18n-js + RTL support via I18nManager.forceRTL + tests RTL layouts mobile, une **navigation React Navigation v7** avec Bottom Tabs (Dashboard, Polices, Sinistres, Notifications, Profil) + Native Stack per tab + Deep linking universal links iOS + intent filters Android, un **dashboard mobile** card-based + pull-to-refresh + skeleton loaders + responsive a different screen sizes, une **page Sinistres mobile** declaration form 5 etapes optimise mobile (touch-first inputs + camera in-app + GPS auto + photos preview swipe gallery + signature pad native), un **camera native** integration via expo-camera + photos capture + permissions handling + crop/rotate basic, un **GPS native** via expo-location avec permissions handling + reverse geocoding optional, un **push notifications natives** Firebase Cloud Messaging Android + Apple Push Notification service iOS via expo-notifications + permission handling + deep linking notifications -> screens specifiques, un **offline mode** SQLite local via expo-sqlite avec tables miroirs polices + sinistres + sync queue + reconciliation conflicts on connect, app store preparation iOS (Bundle ID + icons + splash screen + screenshots 5 tailles + metadata + privacy policy URL + EULA) + Android (Package name + adaptive icon + screenshots 5 tailles + Google Play store listing), et la suite de tests Detox E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant authentication biometric + dashboard + polices + sinistres declaration camera + GPS + push notifications + offline mode + i18n + accessibility mobile.

Le sprint est **critique marche MA** : 75%+ des customers marocains utilisent smartphone primary device, mobile-first declaration sinistre + suivi temps reel = enabler adoption massive Assurflow. Sans mobile native, perception "Assurflow desktop-only" handicape adoption versus competitors Wafa/Saham/AtlantaSanad qui ont apps natives.

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 19 est l'**enabler adoption massive** marche MA. Quatre dimensions strategiques justifient l'investissement de 90 heures.

Premierement, **75%+ customers MA utilisent smartphone primary device** (rapport ANRT 2024 Maroc + GSMA Mobile Economy MENA). Sans application mobile native, Assurflow rate la majorite des customers potentiels. Les competitors marocains (Wafa Assurance avec Wafa Mobile + Saham avec Saham Assistance + AtlantaSanad avec My Atlanta) ont tous des applications mobiles natives. Sprint 19 est un table stake pour competitivite.

Deuxiemement, la **declaration sinistre mobile-first avec camera native** est game-changer UX. Sprint 18 web supporte upload photos mais customer doit transferer photos depuis smartphone vers PC = friction. Mobile native = customer accidente sur autoroute -> ouvre app -> camera native capture photos -> GPS auto lieu_sinistre -> signature pad native -> saga declare-claim trigger en 2 minutes. Cette friction reduction = adoption massive.

Troisiemement, les **push notifications natives** sont superieures emails/SMS pour engagement. Notification "Votre sinistre est passe en phase d'expertise" push native -> customer tap -> directement dans app suivi lifecycle. Email/SMS = customer doit ouvrir email/SMS -> cliquer lien -> login -> retrouver sinistre = 5 clicks vs 1 push tap. Sprint 19 active engagement metrics critique Demo Day.

Quatriemement, le **mode offline** est essentiel marche MA (couverture 4G inegale + zones rurales). Customer agriculteur Marrakech rural -> 4G intermittente -> doit pouvoir consulter polices offline + voir history sinistre offline + queue actions offline -> sync on connect. Sans offline mode, app inutilisable 30%+ situations reelles. Sprint 19 SQLite + sync queue = robustesse production marche MA reel.

---

## ARCHITECTURE CIBLE -- MOBILE PORTAL REACT NATIVE

### Application Expo SDK 51 + React Native 0.74+

```
repo/apps/mobile-portal/
├── package.json                       # @insurtech/mobile-portal
├── app.json                           # Expo config
├── eas.json                           # EAS Build
├── tsconfig.json
├── metro.config.js
├── babel.config.js
├── src/
│   ├── app/                           # Expo Router
│   │   ├── (auth)/
│   │   │   ├── login.tsx
│   │   │   └── biometric.tsx
│   │   ├── (tabs)/
│   │   │   ├── _layout.tsx            # Bottom Tabs
│   │   │   ├── dashboard.tsx
│   │   │   ├── polices/
│   │   │   ├── sinistres/
│   │   │   ├── notifications.tsx
│   │   │   └── profile.tsx
│   │   └── _layout.tsx                # Root navigation
│   ├── components/                    # UI library
│   │   ├── ui/                        # 25+ composants
│   │   ├── camera/
│   │   ├── LifecycleTrackerMobile.tsx
│   │   └── SignaturePad.tsx
│   ├── hooks/                         # useAuth, useOffline, etc
│   ├── services/                      # API adapters
│   ├── db/                            # SQLite schemas
│   ├── i18n/                          # shared messages Sprint 18
│   └── theme/                         # design tokens
├── ios/                               # Generated by Expo
├── android/                           # Generated by Expo
├── assets/
│   ├── icons/
│   ├── splash/
│   └── screenshots/
└── tests/
    └── e2e/                           # Detox
```

### Decision-020 -- Mobile Stack React Native + Expo

**Justification** :
- (a) React Native cross-platform iOS + Android (single codebase)
- (b) Expo SDK 51 abstraction native modules (camera, GPS, push, biometric)
- (c) Reuse Sprint 18 design tokens + composants conceptuels
- (d) Detox E2E tests native iOS + Android
- (e) Fastlane CI/CD App Store + Google Play

**Architecture** :
- React Native 0.74+ + Expo SDK 51 + TypeScript strict
- Expo Router v3 file-based navigation
- NativeWind v4 (Tailwind RN) + composants UI library mobile
- expo-local-authentication + expo-secure-store biometric + JWT
- expo-camera + expo-location + expo-notifications + expo-sqlite
- expo-localization + i18n-js + I18nManager RTL
- Detox E2E + Maestro UI testing (alternative)
- EAS Build cloud + EAS Submit + EAS Update OTA

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-019 + **decision-020** (NOUVELLE, mobile stack).

**Decision-020 mobile-stack-react-native-expo** (NOUVEAU - ratification 4 juin 2026) : adoption **React Native 0.74+ + Expo SDK 51 + biometric auth + push native + offline SQLite + Detox E2E + Fastlane CI/CD**. Justification : (a) Cross-platform single codebase 70% reuse Sprint 18 logic, (b) Expo abstraction native modules acceleration developpement, (c) Marche MA mobile-first 75% requirement, (d) Push native + biometric superieur email/SMS engagement, (e) Offline mode robustesse 4G intermittent. Architecture : Expo SDK 51 + RN 0.74+ + NativeWind v4 + Expo Router v3 + biometric + camera + GPS + push + SQLite + Detox + Fastlane.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Camera permissions denial customer.** Customer refuse camera permission -> declaration sinistre photos impossible. Solution : explanation modal first request + fallback "Importer depuis galerie" via expo-image-picker + degraded mode acceptable.

**Piege 2 : GPS permissions denial + accuracy variance.** GPS accuracy 5-50m variable + customer denies permission. Solution : permission explanation + fallback manual address input + accuracy threshold check + suggest WiFi geolocation fallback.

**Piege 3 : Push notifications token churn.** FCM/APNs tokens rotation periodic + token expiry sur uninstall/reinstall. Solution : token registration backend Sprint 9 chaque app launch + cleanup stale tokens > 90j.

**Piege 4 : Biometric auth fallback complexity.** Device sans biometric configure + biometric scan fail apres 5 attempts. Solution : fallback password input strict + max 5 biometric attempts then password required + UX clear messaging.

**Piege 5 : Offline SQLite sync conflicts.** Customer modifie sinistre offline + meme sinistre modified server-side = conflict. Solution : last-write-wins default + manual review queue critical conflicts + UI banner indicator pending sync.

**Piege 6 : Bundle size native too large.** App APK/IPA > 100MB rejected stores. Solution : Hermes engine + ProGuard Android + bitcode iOS + assets compression + max 50MB target Demo Day.

**Piege 7 : Startup time slow > 5s.** Cold start time degraded performance. Solution : Hermes engine + RAM bundle splitting + lazy load screens + skeleton splash + < 2s target.

**Piege 8 : RTL layouts complex on mobile.** FlexDirection row vs row-reverse + ScrollView + horizontal lists. Solution : I18nManager.isRTL helper + logical direction utilities + tests RTL Detox.

**Piege 9 : iOS App Store review rejection.** Apple guidelines strict (privacy + functionality + design). Solution : compliance checklist exhaustive + privacy nutrition labels + app review notes detailed + Skalean MA InsurTech context expliquer.

**Piege 10 : Push notification scheduling timezone.** Notifications scheduled timezone MA Africa/Casablanca vs UTC. Solution : Date.toLocaleString timezone MA + tests scheduling boundaries + audit log delivered notifications.

---

### Tache 5.4.9 -- Push Notifications Natives FCM + APNs
```

```md
# B-20 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 20 : IA ESTIMATION PHOTOS

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 4 Sprint 5 -- DERNIER PHASE 4)

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer l'**IA Estimation Photos** -- premier deploiement IA Skalean operational pour estimation automatique des montants de sinistres via vision computer sur photos uploaded. Le sprint construit le **pipeline IA complet** : photos input -> detection bbox dommages (YOLOv8) -> classification severite (ResNet) -> regression estimation montant MAD avec confidence scoring -> integration Sprint 16 `PoliceEstimationProvider` 5.1.11 interface (defined Sprint 16) avec **fallback human review** si confidence < threshold. Le deploiement utilise **Atlas Cloud souverain MA** (decision-010) avec datasets training photos vehicules + degats specifique marche marocain (modeles Dacia, Renault, Peugeot, Hyundai dominants vs datasets generaux US/EU).

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un service IA production-grade avec **decision-021 ratifiee** documentant AI Vision stack + deployment souverain + ethics safeguards + cas usage specific autorisation (decision-007 clarification : general IA differe Sprint 30+, mais Sprint 20 estimation photos AUTORISE comme cas usage specific), une **dataset training photos vehicules MA** curee (10000+ photos labeled + bbox annotations + severity grades + montants reference) collectee aupres compagnies fictives + partenaires garage + photos publiques licensed, des **modeles IA trained** (YOLOv8-medium detection bbox dommages + ResNet50 classification severity 5 niveaux + LightGBM regression estimation montant MAD), un **deployment Atlas Cloud souverain** Benguerir avec GPUs NVIDIA T4 inference + Kubernetes autoscaling + Triton Inference Server serving, une **API IA estimation** endpoint REST `/api/v1/ai/estimation/sinistre` consumant photos URLs + retourne estimation_montant_mad + confidence_score + bbox_detections + severity_grade + human_review_required boolean, l'**implementation `PoliceEstimationProvider`** Sprint 16 5.1.11 dependency (interface defined Sprint 16), **confidence scoring** sophistique avec threshold 80%+ confidence proceed automatic + < 80% confidence trigger human review fallback Sprint 22+ workflow, l'**integration Sprint 16 sinistre lifecycle** appel IA estimation lors transition `received -> review` automatic + pre-fill `montant_estime_ia` field + augment human reviewer Sprint 22+, des **safeguards privacy + ethics** (audit log decisions IA + traceabilite + explicabilite via Grad-CAM heatmaps + opt-out customer optional + recommendation IA seulement jamais decision finale), des **monitoring + drift detection** (prediction confidence distribution + model accuracy weekly retraining + alerts MLOps), et la suite de tests E2E avec 25+ scenarios couvrant pipeline complete + integration Sprint 16 + ethics + performance + drift simulation.

Le sprint **cloture Phase 4 SINISTRE CORE** : Sprint 16 (Sinistre Foundation) + Sprint 17 (Police Lifecycle) + Sprint 18 (Web Customer Portal) + Sprint 19 (Mobile Portal) + Sprint 20 (IA Estimation) = pile verticale complete fonctionnelle Demo Day. Phase 5 Skalean Dispatch Platform (Sprints 21-23) constitue prochaine etape avec Uber-style sinistre dispatch garage networks.

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 20 est l'**element differentiateur** d'Assurflow + **proof point IA Skalean**. Quatre dimensions strategiques justifient l'investissement de 92 heures sur ce sprint complexe.

Premierement, l'**IA estimation est element differentiateur fort** vs competitors marocains (Wafa Mobile + Saham Assistance + My Atlanta utilisent processes manual + delays 5-15 jours estimation). Assurflow avec IA estimation auto -> processing time 30 secondes Demo Day = wow factor investisseurs Programme Emergence ACAPS jury. Sans IA estimation, Assurflow reste "encore une app InsurTech" -- avec IA, "premiere plateforme IA-powered MA".

Deuxiemement, **l'IA Vision Computer photos sinistres est cas usage proven industrie** (Tractable.ai US/UK + Solera US + Lemonade US tous utilisent IA Vision Computer pour estimation sinistres auto). Le risque technologique est faible (state-of-the-art models YOLOv8 + ResNet matures), risque execution est moderate (dataset MA-specific + integration Sprint 16 + deployment souverain). Sprint 20 derisks proof point IA Skalean pour roadmap Sprint 30+ general IA.

Troisiemement, le **deployment Atlas Cloud souverain** est element differentiateur strategique Skalean. Concurrents utilisent cloud Google/AWS/Azure -> data residency Maroc questionnable + dependencies US/EU geopolitique. Sprint 20 prouve Skalean opere IA production-grade entierement souverain MA -- compelling narrative regulators ACAPS + government CDG + clients enterprise concerns souverainete data. Decision-021 ratifie commitment souverainete IA.

Quatriemement, le sprint **cloture Phase 4 SINISTRE CORE complete** : Sinistre Foundation + Police Lifecycle + Customer Portal + Mobile + IA Estimation = pile verticale complete. Phase 4 etait foundation Skalean Demo Day. Sprint 20 closure permet entrer Phase 5 Skalean Dispatch Platform (Sprints 21-23) avec architecture solide. Sans Sprint 20 IA, Phase 4 reste "infrastructure + UX sans intelligence" -- avec Sprint 20, "plateforme IA-powered InsurTech complete".

---
```

```
### Decision-021 -- AI Vision Stack + Deployment Souverain + Ethics

**Justification** :
- (a) PyTorch 2.x + Ultralytics YOLOv8 + ResNet50 + LightGBM : state-of-the-art proven industry
- (b) Triton Inference Server : multi-model serving production-grade NVIDIA
- (c) Atlas Cloud souverain Benguerir : data residency MA + souverainete strategique
- (d) Decision-007 clarification : general IA Sprint 30+, cas usage specific Sprint 20 autorise
- (e) Recommendation only design : never automatic decision (ethics + ACAPS compliance)

**Architecture** :
- PyTorch 2.x + Ultralytics YOLOv8m detection + ResNet50 classification + LightGBM regression
- Triton Inference Server NVIDIA + Kubernetes Atlas Cloud + autoscaling
- MLflow tracking + DVC dataset versioning + reproducibility
- Grad-CAM heatmaps explicabilite + audit log persistent Sprint 7
- Confidence threshold 0.80 default + tuned per claim amount

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-020 + **decision-021** (NOUVELLE, AI Vision).

**Decision-007 ai-3-deferred-sprints** : applicable -- general IA differe Sprint 30+. **Clarification Sprint 20** : cas usage SPECIFIC estimation photos sinistres AUTORISE (justifie par differentiation + low-risk proven industry + recommendation-only design).

**Decision-010 atlas-cloud-souverain** : strict. AI deployment Benguerir region MA-1.

**Decision-021 ai-vision-estimation-photos** (NOUVEAU - ratification 5 juin 2026) : adoption **AI Vision Computer pour estimation photos sinistres specific + deployment Atlas Cloud souverain + recommendation-only design + ethics safeguards**. Justification : (a) Element differenciateur vs competitors MA (delays 5-15j -> 30s), (b) Cas usage proven industry (Tractable.ai + Solera + Lemonade), (c) Souverainete data Atlas Cloud strategique, (d) Recommendation-only design ACAPS compliant. Architecture : PyTorch 2.x + YOLOv8m + ResNet50 + LightGBM + Triton Inference Server + Kubernetes Atlas Cloud + MLflow + DVC + Grad-CAM + audit Sprint 7. Integration : Sprint 16 PoliceEstimationProvider implementation 5.1.11.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Dataset MA-specific bias.** Photos generales US/EU dataset bias vs vehicules MA (Dacia, Renault dominants). Solution : curation dataset 10000+ photos vehicules MA + augmentation + validation accuracy across makes.

**Piege 2 : Model accuracy variance critical claims.** Critical claims > 100000 MAD ont moins de samples training -> moins accurate. Solution : stratified sampling + claim amount range distribution + threshold strict 0.90 critical claims + human review obligatoire.

**Piege 3 : Triton Inference Server latency cold start.** GPU model loading initial > 30s -> first request slow. Solution : pre-load models startup + warm-up requests + persistent model cache.

**Piege 4 : Atlas Cloud GPUs availability MA.** NVIDIA T4 GPUs Atlas Cloud limited inventory MA. Solution : reserve capacity contract + fallback inference CPU temporary + autoscale prudent.

**Piege 5 : Photos download S3 timeout Sprint 10.** Customer mobile slow upload -> photos partial -> IA inference incomplete. Solution : timeout 30s + retry exponential + minimum 3 photos required (Sprint 16 5.1.4 ACAPS strict).

**Piege 6 : Confidence scoring miscalibration.** Models trained well + confidence scores not calibrated -> over-confidence false positives. Solution : Platt scaling calibration + Expected Calibration Error metrics + thresholds tuned validation set.

**Piege 7 : Ethics opt-out customer compliance.** Customer opt-out IA estimation -> need fallback human review Sprint 22+ mandatory. Solution : ai_estimation_opt_out flag Sprint 8 strict respect + bypass IA + direct human review.

**Piege 8 : Drift detection false positives.** Model accuracy weekly variance noise -> false alerts retraining. Solution : statistical significance tests + 4-week rolling window + ALERT only > 5% degradation sustained.

**Piege 9 : Grad-CAM heatmaps generation expensive.** Critical claims trigger Grad-CAM = additional inference 2-3s. Solution : async generation post-inference + store S3 + lazy load human reviewer.

**Piege 10 : Audit log persistence volume.** IA decisions audit log Sprint 7 -> 100k decisions/year * 5+ years = volume. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse archival + retention policy strict 5 years legal compliance.

---

## LISTE DES 12 TACHES -- SCOPE DETAILLE

### Tache 5.5.1 -- Architecture Base + Decision-021 + Design Doc IA

**Effort** : 5h | **P0** | **Densite** : 110-150 KB

Gateway architectural Sprint 20. Decision-021 ratifiee + design doc 20+ pages IA + peer-review obligatoire + clarification decision-007.

**Livrables** : `decisions/021-ai-vision-estimation-photos.md` 8-12 pages (clarification decision-007 + AI Vision stack + Atlas Cloud + ethics + recommendation-only + alternatives Tractable.ai SaaS / custom training / open-source models + consequences + revision Phase 7+), `docs/architecture/ai-estimation-pipeline-design.md` 20+ pages (pipeline + models + deployment + ethics + integration), kickoff report Phase 4 Sprint 5, peer-review Saad+Abla + ethics review Phase 7+ roadmap.

### Tache 5.5.2 -- Datasets Training Photos Vehicules MA

**Effort** : 8h | **P0 | **Densite** : 140-180 KB

Curation + labeling datasets training 10000+ photos vehicules MA.

**Livrables** : Datasets `repo/packages/ai-estimation/datasets/` (training 8000 photos + validation 1000 photos + test 1000 photos), sources (fictional brand AssurMaghreb synthetic data 3000 + Atlas Insurance 2000 + MediAssur 2000 + partenaires garage reels NDAs 2000 + photos publiques Flickr Creative Commons MA-relevant licensed 1000), annotations exhaustives (bbox dommages multi-classes hood/door/bumper/window/wheel/headlight/taillight/side_panel via tool CVAT.ai labels + severity grades 1-5 cosmetique/superficiel/modere/grave/total_loss + montants reference reels MAD + metadata vehicule make/model/year + conditions photo angle/distance/lighting + customer demographics anonymized ages/regions), augmentation (rotation 0-360 + luminosite brightness/contrast + cropping random + JPEG quality variations), DVC tracking + S3 storage Atlas Cloud, 8+ tests data quality checks.

### Tache 5.5.3 -- Models Training YOLOv8 + ResNet + LightGBM

**Effort** : 12h | **P0 CRITIQUE** | **Densite** : 200-260 KB

Training 3 modeles pipeline (detection + classification + regression).

**Livrables** : Notebooks Jupyter `notebooks/01-train-detection.ipynb` (Ultralytics YOLOv8m + 200 epochs + AdamW optimizer + lr scheduler cosine + mAP > 0.75 target validation set), `notebooks/02-train-classification.ipynb` (ResNet50 backbone + 5 classes severity + cross-entropy loss + accuracy > 0.85 target), `notebooks/03-train-regression.ipynb` (LightGBM features bbox_count + severity_grades + vehicule metadata one-hot encoded + montants_reference target + MAE < 20% target + Pearson correlation > 0.80), hyperparameter tuning Optuna 50 trials per model, training pipelines reproducibles via MLflow tracking, evaluation comprehensive (mAP + classification accuracy + regression MAE/RMSE + cross-validation 5-fold + confusion matrices + ROC curves + calibration plots Platt scaling), models export ONNX format pour Triton serving, 16+ tests evaluation reproducibility.

### Tache 5.5.4 -- Deployment Atlas Cloud Souverain + Triton

**Effort** : 9h | **P0 CRITIQUE SOUVERAINETE** | **Densite** : 170-220 KB

Deployment infrastructure souverain Atlas Cloud + Triton Inference Server.

**Livrables** : Terraform `repo/infrastructure/ai-estimation/terraform/` (Atlas Cloud region MA-1 Benguerir + Kubernetes cluster ai-estimation-cluster 3 nodes + GPUs NVIDIA T4 dedicated + load balancer + storage S3), Kubernetes manifests `k8s/triton-server.yaml` (Triton Inference Server multi-model serving YOLOv8 + ResNet + LightGBM ensemble + persistent volume models + readiness probes), `k8s/autoscaling.yaml` HPA based GPU/CPU utilization + min 1 max 5 replicas, `k8s/monitoring.yaml` Prometheus + Grafana dashboards (latency P50/P95/P99 + throughput + GPU usage + memory + errors), CI/CD pipeline build + deploy via GitOps ArgoCD, certificates mTLS strict + secrets Sprint 5 Vault, 12+ tests deployment validation.

### Tache 5.5.5 -- API IA Estimation REST Endpoint

**Effort** : 7h | **P0** | **Densite** : 140-180 KB

Endpoint REST consumable Sprint 16 + retours structured.

**Livrables** : Controller `ai-estimation.controller.ts` + endpoint `POST /api/v1/ai/estimation/sinistre` request body validation Zod `{sinistre_id, photo_urls[], vehicule_metadata?: {make, model, year}}`, service orchestrate (download photos S3 Sprint 10 via signed URLs + pre-process resize 1280x720 + invoke Triton inference + post-process aggregate + business rules confidence + return), response structured `{estimation_montant_mad, confidence_score, bbox_detections[], severity_grade, severity_breakdown, human_review_required, processing_time_ms, model_versions}`, **timeout 30s strict + circuit breaker** Sprint 14 pattern + idempotency via sinistre_id Sprint 15 + rate limit 100 req/min/tenant + permissions Sprint 7.5a `ai.estimation.request`, OpenAPI 3.1 schemas, 14+ tests E2E.

### Tache 5.5.6 -- PoliceEstimationProvider Sprint 16 Dependency

**Effort** : 7h | **P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION** | **Densite** : 140-180 KB

Implementation interface `PoliceEstimationProvider` Sprint 16 5.1.11 defined.

**Livrables** : Service `PoliceEstimationProviderImpl implements PoliceEstimationProvider` Sprint 16 interface, methode `estimateClaimAmount(sinistreId, photoUrls): EstimationResult` (orchestrate AIEstimationService.estimate + return canonical EstimationResult `{montant_estime_mad, confidence_score, human_review_required, model_versions, bbox_count, severity_grade}`), integration Sprint 16 SinistreService listener event `sinistre.received` -> async invoke estimateClaimAmount -> INSERT sinistre.metadata.ai_estimation JSONB + emit event `sinistre.ai_estimation_completed`, error handling exhaustive (timeout, photos invalid, model errors) avec fallback graceful (return human_review_required=true + reason explanatory), tests integration end-to-end Sprint 16 + Sprint 20 verify flow complete, 14+ tests.

**Effort** : 6h | **P0 CRITIQUE | **Densite** : 130-170 KB

Confidence scoring algorithm sophistique + human review fallback thresholds.

**Livrables** : Service `ConfidenceScoringService.calculate(detectionResult, classificationResult, regressionResult)` aggregate per-model confidence (detection IOU mean + classification softmax max prob + regression prediction interval narrowness via quantile predictions) -> overall confidence 0-1, helper `determineHumanReviewRequired(confidence, claim_amount_estimated)` thresholds configurable (default 0.80 + strict 0.90 si claim > 100000 MAD + permissive 0.70 si claim < 5000 MAD + critical claims > 500000 MAD always human review regardless confidence), Platt scaling calibration models trained validation set + Expected Calibration Error metrics monitored, audit log decisions IA + thresholds applied + reasons explanatory, 12+ tests scenarios.
```

```md
## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Sinistre Workflow Uber-Style** premier sprint Phase 5 Skalean Dispatch Platform. Le sprint construit le marketplace dispatch layer qui transforme Sinistre Sprint 16 ponctuel "customer declare + reviewer review" en marketplace "customer declare -> matching garages geo+capacite+ratings -> notification fanout multi-channel + acceptance timeout fallback + real-time tracking ETA + assignment automatic". Pattern type Uber/Doordash adapte assurance : sinistre = demand, garage = supply, dispatch platform Skalean = orchestrator. Le sprint introduit **decision-022 ratification** dispatch matching algorithm + real-time tracking PostGIS + Redis Geo + multi-channel notifications + acceptance workflow.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un **dispatch platform operational** avec entites domain `Garage` (id + tenant_id + name + location PostGIS POINT + radius_service_km + capacity_concurrent_sinistres + rating_average + verified + active), `DispatchOffer` (id + sinistre_id + garage_id + status proposed/accepted/rejected/timeout/expired + proposed_at + responded_at + score_matching + reason_assignment), `Assignment` (id + sinistre_id + garage_id + assigned_at + estimated_arrival_at + actual_arrival_at + completion_at + status), `GarageRating` (id + sinistre_id + garage_id + customer_id + rating_1_5 + comment + timestamps), un **matching algorithm** sophistique combinant proximity (haversine distance PostGIS) + capacity (current sinistres count < capacity_concurrent) + ratings (>= 4.0 stars priority) + customer preferences (favorites override) + score weighted (proximity 40% + capacity 20% + ratings 30% + preferences 10%) configurable, un **real-time geolocation tracking** GPS garage live position via Redis Geo SET + ETA calculation distance/speed_avg, un **notification fanout multi-channel** fanout garages eligibles top 5 nearest via Sprint 9 NotificationRouter (push native Sprint 19 + SMS Sprint 9 + email Sprint 9 simultanement), un **acceptance workflow** garages 60s timeout + premier accept wins + fallback automatic next batch garages si tous reject/timeout + max 3 fallback rounds avant escalade reviewer Sprint 22, un **integration Sprint 16 sinistre lifecycle** transition `review -> assigned` automatic post acceptance + emit event `sinistre.assigned_to_garage` consume Sprint 9 customer notification + Sprint 18+19 real-time UI updates, un **garage onboarding workflow** signup self-service + KYC verification + location verification + capacity declaration + activation admin Sprint 22+, des **customer preferences** favorites garages + ratings history + algorithm preference matching, des **endpoints REST dispatch** consumables Sprint 18/19, des **real-time updates SSE/WebSocket** Sprint 18 web + Sprint 19 mobile (offer received, accepted, ETA updates), et la suite de tests E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant matching algorithm + multi-channel notifications + acceptance timeout + fallback rounds + integration Sprint 16 + real-time tracking + customer preferences.

Le sprint est **premier proof point marketplace Skalean** : transition assurance vertical -> marketplace vertical = vision Skalean platform multi-vertical (assurance Phase 4 + dispatch Phase 5 + autres futurs).

---

customer-garage favorites mapping), le **package `@insurtech/dispatch`** v1.0.0 NestJS module integration monorepo, le **matching algorithm** service `MatchingService.findEligibleGarages(sinistre, options): GarageMatch[]` (query PostGIS ST_DWithin radius 50km default + filter capacity available + filter active+verified + apply customer preferences priority + scoring weighted + sort score desc + limit top 5 batch initial) avec algorithm configurable poids (proximity 40% + capacity 20% + ratings 30% + preferences 10%) tunable per tenant, un **real-time geolocation tracking** service `GarageTrackingService.updateLocation(garageId, lat, lng)` Redis Geo SET `garage:locations` + service `GarageTrackingService.getNearestGarages(lat, lng, radius_km, limit)` Redis GEORADIUSBYMEMBER pour queries performance (10000 garages query < 50ms target), ETA calculation `EtaCalculatorService.calculateEta(garage_location, sinistre_location): number_minutes` distance haversine PostGIS + speed average 40 km/h urbain MA + traffic multiplier configurable, un **notification fanout multi-channel** service `DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, garage_matches)` (fanout top 5 garages parallel via Sprint 9 NotificationRouter + push native Sprint 19 device tokens + SMS Sprint 9 (Maroc Telecom + Orange + INWI) + email Sprint 9 (Brevo backend) + WhatsApp Business status='proposed' + timeout 60s strict + lock optimistic Postgres SELECT FOR UPDATE + first wins update status='accepted' others auto-status='superseded' + emit event `dispatch.offer_accepted`) + service `expireOffers()` cron daily 60s window enforce + service `triggerFallback(sinistre_id)` next batch 5 garages si tous initial reject/timeout + max 3 fallback rounds avant escalade Sprint 22 - Listener event `dispatch.offer_accepted` -> Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition(sinistre_id, 'assigned') + emit event `sinistre.assigned_to_garage` consume Sprint 9 + Sprint 18/19 real-time UI

legal upload Sprint 10) + KYC verification process (verification documents legal RC + IF + ICE + signature numerique Sprint 10) + admin approval Sprint 22+ workflow (super-admin valide + activate garage), des **customer preferences** Sprint 8 customer profile extension (favorite_garages_ids[] + preferred_radius_km + min_rating_threshold + customer_segment) + service `CustomerPreferenceService.applyToMatching(customer_id, garage_matches): GarageMatch[]` priority customer favorites + filter min_rating + adjust scoring weights, un **rating system** post-assignment customer rates garage 1-5 stars + comment optional via Sprint 18+19 UI + service `GarageRatingService.submitRating(sinistre_id, customer_id, garage_id, rating, comment)` + update garage.rating_average rolling average, des **endpoints REST dispatch** controller `dispatch.controller.ts` avec 15 endpoints (POST `/api/v1/dispatch/sinistre/{id}/match-garages` trigger matching manual override + GET `/api/v1/dispatch/sinistre/{id}/offers` list offers + POST `/api/v1/dispatch/offers/{id}/accept` garage accept + POST `/api/v1/dispatch/offers/{id}/reject` garage reject + GET `/api/v1/dispatch/garages` list garages filterable + POST `/api/v1/garages` onboarding signup + PUT `/api/v1/garages/{id}/location` real-time location update mobile + GET `/api/v1/garages/nearest?lat&lng&radius_km` proximity query + GET `/api/v1/dispatch/assignments/{id}` details + POST `/api/v1/dispatch/assignments/{id}/rate` customer rate + GET `/api/v1/garages/{id}/ratings` history + autres admin endpoints), des **real-time updates SSE/WebSocket** extension Sprint 18 web `/api/sse/dispatch` consume Kafka topics dispatch events + Sprint 19 mobile push native via Sprint 9 NotificationRouter dispatch categories (offer_received + offer_accepted + eta_updated + assignment_status_change), et **30+ tests E2E** covering matching algorithm scenarios + multi-channel fanout + acceptance race conditions + fallback rounds + customer preferences override + ratings submission + integration Sprint 16 lifecycle + real-time tracking proximity.

### Exclus du Sprint 21 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas le **workflow expertise human validation** post-assignment (reviewer Sprint 22+ valide travail garage avant payout) defere Sprint 22 (Sprint 21 fait dispatch + assignment + ratings basiques, validation expertise reviewer Sprint 22), les **payouts garage automatic** post-completion sinistre defers Sprint 22+ (Sprint 21 fait assignment status workflow, payouts integration Sprint 11 differe Sprint 22), les **garage networks contractuels** (groupes garages contractuels insurer-specific) differes Sprint 23 (Sprint 21 fait dispatch open marketplace, contractual networks differs Sprint 23), les **multi-garage dispatch parallel** (1 sinistre dispatched plusieurs garages simultane comparison quotes) differes Phase 7+ (Sprint 21 single garage assignment), les **AI optimization matching** (ML model predicting best garage per sinistre type) differes Sprint 30+ (Sprint 21 algorithm deterministic weights), les **garage performance analytics** dashboards differes Sprint 28 admin reports (Sprint 21 ratings basiques, analytics differs Sprint 28), les **garage compensation negotiations** workflow defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 21 prix fixe insurer-defined), les **WhatsApp Business notifications** garage differs Phase 7+ (Sprint 21 push + SMS + email suffisant), les **dispute resolution** garage-customer conflicts defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 21 ratings + comments uniquement), et les **garage employee management** (multi-users per garage avec roles) differs Sprint 23 (Sprint 21 1 garage = 1 user owner).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 21 est l'**inflection point business model** Skalean. Quatre dimensions strategiques justifient l'investissement de 88 heures.

Premierement, Sprint 21 transforme **Skalean d'insurer-broker tool a marketplace platform**. Phase 4 (Sprints 16-20) construit infrastructure assurance vertical = produit insurer-side. Sprint 21+ construit marketplace dispatch = produit insurer + garage + customer (3-sided marketplace). Cette evolution est strategique car marketplaces valorisent 5-10x plus que tools verticaux (network effects + winner-takes-most dynamics).

Deuxiemement, le **pattern Uber/Doordash applique a assurance** est differentiateur fort. Marche MA reparation auto post-sinistre = 50000+ sinistres/an + 5000+ garages disponibles. Customers actuels appel garage manuel + attendre disponibilite + comparer devis = friction enorme. Skalean automatise : sinistre declare -> 5 garages eligibles notifies -> premier accept wins + ETA -> customer voir progression real-time. Friction reduction = adoption massive customers + value-add insurer (faster claim resolution).

Troisiemement, l'**integration Sprint 16 sinistre lifecycle** ferme proof point Phase 4 complete. Phase 4 livre pile verticale assurance. Sprint 21 demontre que cette pile s'integre seamlessly avec marketplace dispatch = architecture extensible. Pour Demo Day 30 juin 2026, demo end-to-end Customer declare sinistre app mobile -> IA estimation Sprint 20 -> dispatch matching Sprint 21 -> garage accept push notification -> customer voit ETA real-time -> assignment completed = wow factor investisseurs.

Quatriemement, le **dispatch platform est foundation Phase 5 + 6** : Sprint 21 (dispatch core) -> Sprint 22 (workflow expertise validation reviewer) -> Sprint 23 (garage networks contractual) = pile dispatch complete. Sans Sprint 21, Phase 5 ne peut pas avancer.

---
```

```
### Decision-022 -- Dispatch Matching + Real-Time Tracking + Multi-Channel

**Justification** :
- (a) Uber/Doordash patterns proven marketplace assurance
- (b) PostGIS spatial queries + Redis Geo performance combined
- (c) Multi-channel fanout maximize garage acceptance rate
- (d) Acceptance timeout 60s + fallback rounds proven retry pattern
- (e) Customer preferences override basique pour fidelite

**Architecture** :
- PostgreSQL + PostGIS spatial extension + Redis Geo
- Matching algorithm weighted score configurable
- Notification fanout Sprint 9 multi-channel (push + SMS + email)
- Acceptance optimistic locking SELECT FOR UPDATE
- Fallback 3 rounds max avant escalade reviewer
- Integration Sprint 16 lifecycle via Kafka listeners

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-021 + **decision-022** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-016 srf-schema-registry** : utilise pour events canonical `dispatch.*` Avro.

**Decision-017 sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine** : integration listener transitions.

**Decision-020 mobile-stack** : push notifications Sprint 19 garages mobile users.

**Decision-022 dispatch-matching-real-time-tracking** (NOUVEAU - ratification 6 juin 2026) : adoption **dispatch platform marketplace Uber-style + matching algorithm weighted score + real-time geolocation tracking PostGIS+Redis + multi-channel notifications + acceptance timeout 60s + fallback 3 rounds**. Justification : (a) Patterns Uber/Doordash proven, (b) PostGIS + Redis Geo performance, (c) Multi-channel fanout maximize acceptance rate, (d) Fallback escalation reviewer Sprint 22. Architecture : 5 entites domain + matching service weighted + tracking Redis Geo + fanout Sprint 9 + acceptance optimistic locking + integration Sprint 16 listener.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Matching algorithm performance 10000 garages.** Query PostGIS ST_DWithin radius 50km + scoring 10000 garages slow. Solution : Redis Geo GEORADIUSBYMEMBER pre-filter + PostGIS detailed scoring top 50 + index spatial GIST.

**Piege 2 : Notification fanout race conditions.** 5 garages notifies parallel + tous accept simultane = race condition multiple assignments. Solution : SELECT FOR UPDATE strict + first wins + others auto-superseded + atomic transaction.

**Piege 3 : Acceptance timeout 60s reset on partial response.** Garage opens notification but does not accept dans 60s -> next batch. Solution : timeout strict 60s start_notification + cron expire offers + fallback automatic next.

**Piege 4 : Customer preferences override conflicts.** Favorite garage offline/unavailable -> apply preferences vs matching score. Solution : preferences priorize but verify availability+capacity first + fallback algorithm scoring si favorite indisponible.

**Piege 5 : Real-time tracking GPS battery drain mobile.** Garage app updates location every 10s drains battery. Solution : adaptive frequency (10s active sinistre + 60s idle + 5min background) + opt-out garage settings.

**Piege 6 : Fallback rounds infinite loop.** Sinistre rejets cycle infini -> never assigned. Solution : max 3 fallback rounds strict + escalation reviewer Sprint 22 manual assignment.

**Piege 7 : Rating system spam/fraud.** Garage owners create fake customers rate themselves 5 stars. Solution : ratings only allowed post-assignment completion + customer_id verified Sprint 8 KYC + fraud detection patterns Phase 7+.

**Piege 8 : Multi-channel notification cost.** 5 garages x (push + SMS + email) = 15 notifications x cost. Solution : push priority (free) + SMS fallback si push fail (paid) + email always (free).

**Piege 9 : PostGIS migration complex existing data.** Adding PostGIS extension production database. Solution : staging environment test + migration script idempotent + rollback plan + downtime 0 strategy.

**Piege 10 : Garage onboarding fraud verification.** Fake garages signup self-service. Solution : KYC documents legal verification + admin Sprint 22+ approval before activation + verified flag.

---

### Tache 6.1.2 -- Domain Entities Garage + DispatchOffer + Assignment + Rating

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

5 entites domain dispatch + PostGIS spatial index + RLS multi-tenant.

**Livrables** : Package `@insurtech/dispatch` v1.0.0, migration `CreateDispatchEntities` (`garages` aggregate + PostGIS POINT geometry + GIST index spatial + radius_service_km + capacity_concurrent + rating_average + verified + active, `dispatch_offers` append-only audit + status enum proposed/accepted/rejected/timeout/superseded/expired + scoring + reasons, `assignments` lifecycle + estimated_arrival_at + actual_arrival_at + completion_at + status, `garage_ratings` history append-only + rating 1-5 + comment, `garage_preferences` customer-garage favorites mapping), RLS active 5 tables, indexes performance (PostGIS GIST garages.location + tenant_id+status partial + capacity_concurrent + customer_id+garage_id favorites + dispatch_offers.sinistre_id), enums TypeScript explicit, seeds initial 50 garages MA fictifs + 6 villes (Casablanca, Rabat, Marrakech, Fes, Tanger, Agadir) + ratings synthetiques, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 6.1.3 -- Matching Algorithm Weighted Score

**8h | P0 CRITIQUE | 150-200 KB**

Algorithm matching scoring weighted + tunable poids + customer preferences.

**Livrables** : Service `MatchingService.findEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, options?): GarageMatch[]` orchestrate (query PostGIS ST_DWithin radius 50km default configurable + filter capacity available current_sinistres_count < capacity_concurrent + filter active+verified + apply customer preferences favorites priority + scoring weighted + sort score desc + limit top 5 batch initial), algorithm scoring weighted poids configurable per tenant (`proximity_weight 0.40 + capacity_weight 0.20 + rating_weight 0.30 + preference_weight 0.10`), helper `calculateProximityScore(garage_location, sinistre_location)` haversine distance normalized 0-1 (closer = 1.0, 50km = 0.0), `calculateCapacityScore(garage)` (1 - current_count/capacity_max) si capacity disponible sinon 0, `calculateRatingScore(garage)` (rating_average / 5.0), `calculatePreferenceScore(customer_id, garage_id)` 1.0 si favorite sinon 0.5 default, telemetry `matching_eligible_garages_count` + `matching_top_score` + `matching_algorithm_duration_ms`, 16+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.1.4 -- Real-Time Geolocation Tracking PostGIS + Redis Geo

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE | 140-180 KB**

Tracking GPS garages + queries proximity performance + ETA calculation.

**Livrables** : Service `GarageTrackingService.updateLocation(garage_id, lat, lng, accuracy_meters?)` Redis Geo SET `garage:locations:{tenant_id}` GEOADD + Postgres UPDATE garage.location PostGIS POINT (denormalize cache write), service `getNearestGarages(lat, lng, radius_km, limit?)` Redis GEORADIUSBYMEMBER WITHCOORD WITHDIST (10000 garages query < 50ms target), service `EtaCalculatorService.calculateEta(garage_location, sinistre_location): number_minutes` (distance haversine PostGIS km + speed_avg 40 km/h urbain MA + traffic_multiplier configurable + buffer 5min minimum), endpoint `PUT /api/v1/garages/{id}/location` consume Sprint 19 mobile GPS updates (adaptive frequency 10s active + 60s idle + 5min background), cleanup stale locations > 24h via cron Sprint 17 pattern, 14+ tests integration.

### Tache 6.1.5 -- Notification Fanout Multi-Channel

**7h | P0 | 140-180 KB**

Notification fanout top 5 garages parallel push + SMS + email Sprint 9.

**Livrables** : Service `DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages(sinistre_id, garage_matches)` orchestrate fanout top 5 garages parallel via Promise.allSettled + integration Sprint 9 NotificationRouter (push native Sprint 19 garage device tokens registered + SMS Sprint 9 Maroc Telecom/Orange/INWI fallback si push fail + email Sprint 9 Brevo always sent), templates Sprint 9 dispatch-specific (FR + AR i18n) `dispatch-offer-received` (sinistre details + estimation IA Sprint 20 + customer location + ETA calculee), single attempt par garage par offer (no retry pour garage rejete/timeout), telemetry `dispatch_notifications_sent_total{channel,result}` + `dispatch_notification_fanout_duration_ms`, 12+ tests integration.

### Tache 6.1.6 -- Acceptance Workflow + Timeout Fallback

**8h | P0 CRITIQUE | 150-200 KB**

Acceptance optimistic locking + timeout 60s + fallback 3 rounds.

**Livrables** : Service `OfferAcceptanceService.acceptOffer(offer_id, garage_id, eta_estimated_minutes)` (verify offer status='proposed' + lock optimistic Postgres SELECT FOR UPDATE + first wins update status='accepted' + UPDATE other offers same sinistre status='superseded' + atomic transaction + emit event `dispatch.offer_accepted`), service `rejectOffer(offer_id, garage_id, reason)` (status='rejected' + emit `dispatch.offer_rejected`), service `expireOffers()` cron job every 30s window enforce 60s timeout (UPDATE status='timeout' WHERE proposed_at < now() - INTERVAL '60 seconds' AND status='proposed' + emit per garage `dispatch.offer_timeout`), service `FallbackOrchestrationService.handleAllOffersFailed(sinistre_id, current_round)` (verify current_round <= 3 max + find next batch 5 garages excluding previously offered + DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages new batch + increment round counter + emit `dispatch.fallback_round_triggered` + si round > 3 emit `dispatch.all_offers_failed` escalate Sprint 22 reviewer manual assignment), Sprint 16 listener `dispatch.all_offers_failed` -> SinistreService.escalateToManualReview + Sprint 22+ notification, 16+ tests integration scenarios race conditions concurrent acceptance.

### Tache 6.1.7 -- Integration Sprint 16 Sinistre Lifecycle

**6h | P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION | 130-170 KB**

Integration listener Sprint 16 lifecycle + transitions automatic.

**Livrables** : Listener `SinistreReviewListener` Sprint 16 Kafka topic `insurtech.events.sinistre.review` -> async invoke MatchingService.findEligibleGarages(sinistre_id) -> create DispatchOffers entries (one per garage top 5) -> DispatchNotificationFanoutService.notifyEligibleGarages -> wait acceptance, listener `OfferAcceptedListener` event `dispatch.offer_accepted` -> Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition(sinistre_id, 'assigned') + create Assignment entity + emit event `sinistre.assigned_to_garage` consume Sprint 9 NotificationRouter (customer notification + push native Sprint 19) + Sprint 18 real-time SSE update, listener `AllOffersFailedListener` event `dispatch.all_offers_failed` -> Sprint 16 SinistreService.escalateToManualReview + Sprint 22+ reviewer notification, error handling exhaustive + retry queues persistent, integration tests end-to-end Sprint 16 + Sprint 21 sinistre.review -> matching -> notifications -> acceptance -> assignment -> Sprint 16 transition assigned, 14+ tests.

### Tache 6.1.8 -- Garage Onboarding Workflow

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

Garage signup self-service + KYC + admin approval Sprint 22+.

**Livrables** : Endpoint `POST /api/v1/garages` onboarding signup (garage info + location pin map + radius_service_km + capacity_concurrent + photos local upload Sprint 10 + documents legal RC/IF/ICE upload Sprint 10 + signature digitale Sprint 10), screens Sprint 18 `/garages/signup` formulaire multi-step similar Sprint 5.3.8 sinistres declaration pattern, service `GarageOnboardingService.signup(garageData)` (ACAPS-like compliance check + validation + create Garage status='pending_verification' + audit log Sprint 7), screens Sprint 22+ admin approval `/admin/garages/pending` list + buttons "Verify documents" + "Activate" / "Reject" + audit + emit event `garage.verified` ou `garage.rejected` + email Sprint 9 garage owner, 12+ tests E2E.

### Tache 6.1.9 -- Customer Preferences + Ratings System

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Customer favorites garages + ratings post-assignment + rolling average.

**Livrables** : Service `CustomerPreferenceService.toggleFavorite(customer_id, garage_id)` Sprint 8 customer profile extension (favorite_garages_ids[] + preferred_radius_km + min_rating_threshold + customer_segment), service `applyToMatching(customer_id, garage_matches): GarageMatch[]` (priority customer favorites + filter min_rating + adjust scoring weights), service `GarageRatingService.submitRating(sinistre_id, customer_id, garage_id, rating_1_5, comment?)` (verify assignment completed + customer_id = sinistre.customer_id + INSERT garage_rating + UPDATE garage.rating_average rolling average via materialized view ou trigger), endpoints `POST /api/v1/dispatch/assignments/{id}/rate` + `GET /api/v1/garages/{id}/ratings`, UI Sprint 18+19 modal rating post-assignment completion, 10+ tests E2E.

### Tache 6.1.10 -- Endpoints REST Dispatch

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

15 endpoints REST consumables Sprint 18/19/Sprint 22+ admin.

**Livrables** : Controller `dispatch.controller.ts` 15 endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/dispatch/sinistre/{id}/match-garages` trigger matching manual
- `GET /api/v1/dispatch/sinistre/{id}/offers` list offers
- `POST /api/v1/dispatch/offers/{id}/accept` garage accept (Sprint 22+ garage mobile)
- `POST /api/v1/dispatch/offers/{id}/reject` garage reject
- `GET /api/v1/dispatch/garages` list garages filterable
- `POST /api/v1/garages` onboarding signup
- `PUT /api/v1/garages/{id}/location` real-time location update
- `GET /api/v1/garages/nearest?lat&lng&radius_km` proximity query
- `GET /api/v1/dispatch/assignments/{id}` details
- `POST /api/v1/dispatch/assignments/{id}/rate` customer rate
- `GET /api/v1/garages/{id}/ratings` history
- `POST /api/v1/admin/garages/{id}/verify` admin approval Sprint 22+
- `POST /api/v1/admin/garages/{id}/activate` admin activate
- `POST /api/v1/dispatch/sinistre/{id}/escalate` manual escalation reviewer
- Autres admin endpoints

OpenAPI 3.1 schemas, permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15, 16+ tests E2E.

### Tache 6.1.11 -- Real-Time Updates SSE/WebSocket

**6h | P0 | 120-160 KB**

Real-time updates Sprint 18 web SSE + Sprint 19 mobile push extension.

**Livrables** : Route `/api/sse/dispatch` extension Sprint 18 endpoint consume Kafka topics dispatch events `dispatch.*` (offer_received customer-side + offer_accepted + eta_updated + assignment_status_change + offer_timeout + fallback_round + all_offers_failed) + filter events per tenant + sinistre_id + customer_id scoped, Sprint 19 mobile push native categories extension via Sprint 9 (dispatch_offer_received garage-side + dispatch_assignment_accepted customer-side + dispatch_eta_updated customer-side + dispatch_assignment_completed both), hooks Sprint 18 + Sprint 19 `useDispatchEvents()` EventSource + reconnect + cache invalidation React Query, UI updates real-time (sinistre details page show "Recherche garages..." -> "Garage XYZ accepted, ETA 25 min" -> "Garage en route" -> "Travail en cours" -> "Termine"), 12+ tests integration.

### Tache 6.1.12 -- Tests E2E 30+ + Sprint 21 Closure

**8h | P0 | 180-240 KB**

Tests E2E exhaustifs + Phase 5 Sprint 1 closure.

**Livrables** : 30+ tests E2E organises :
- Suite matching algorithm 6 tests (proximity scoring, capacity filter, ratings filter, preferences override, top 5 batch, configurable poids tenant)
- Suite real-time tracking 4 tests (location update Redis, nearest query performance, ETA calculation, stale cleanup)
- Suite notifications fanout 4 tests (multi-channel parallel, push priority, SMS fallback, email always)
- Suite acceptance workflow 6 tests (single accept wins race condition, timeout 60s, fallback round 1, fallback round 2, fallback round 3 escalation, optimistic locking)
- Suite integration Sprint 16 4 tests (listener review -> matching, accept -> assignment, fallback -> escalation, real-time updates)
- Suite garage onboarding 3 tests (signup multi-step, KYC documents verification, admin approval)
- Suite ratings 3 tests (post-assignment rating, rolling average update, fraud prevention customer verification)

Benchmarks (P95 matching < 500ms 1000 garages + P95 nearest query < 50ms 10000 garages), coverage >= 85% lignes, rapport closure `sprint-21-closure-report.md` documenter premier dispatch operational + integration Sprint 16 verified + matching algorithm tuned + real-time tracking + notifications fanout + acceptance + fallback + ratings + pre-requis Sprint 22 (workflow expertise validation) valides + recommandation GO Sprint 22.
```

```md
# B-22 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 22 : WORKFLOW EXPERTISE VALIDATION

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 5 Sprint 2)
**Phase** : 5 -- Skalean Dispatch Platform (Sprints 21-23)
**Sprint** : 22 / 37 -- Phase 5 Sprint 2 (FERME CYCLE COMPLETE)
**Position** : Apres Dispatch Uber-Style (Sprint 21), avant Garage Network (Sprint 23)
**Numerotation taches** : 6.2.1 a 6.2.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~85 heures developpement
**Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE LIFECYCLE (ferme boucle complete sinistre declaration -> payment)
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)
```
