## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Workflow Expertise Validation** -- ferme le cycle complete lifecycle sinistre (declaration -> dispatch -> work -> validation -> payment). Le sprint construit le workflow human review post-completion travail garage : reviewer humain receive assignment completed -> compare photos before/after + verify montant claim vs estimation IA Sprint 20 + verify customer signature acceptance + decide approve/reject/dispute -> trigger payouts Sprint 11 (customer indemnisation + garage compensation tiered). Le sprint introduit **decision-023 ratification** workflow expertise + payment trigger automatic + dispute resolution workflow 3 niveaux escalation.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un workflow expertise complete avec entites domain `Expertise` (id + sinistre_id + reviewer_id + status assigned/in_review/approved/rejected/disputed + reviewed_at + decision + final_amount_mad + rationale_text + photos_before_count + photos_after_count + customer_signature_at + override_ia boolean + override_rationale), `ExpertiseValidation` (id + expertise_id + validation_type photos_quality/montant_coherence/customer_signature/garage_compliance + result pass/fail/warning + reviewer_notes), `PayoutTrigger` (id + sinistre_id + customer_payout_id Sprint 11 + garage_payout_id Sprint 11 + triggered_at + completed_at), `Dispute` (id + sinistre_id + expertise_id + initiator customer/garage/insurer + reason + status open/under_review/escalated/resolved + resolution_decision + escalation_level 1/2/3), un **workflow assignment reviewer** automatic via routing rules (load balancing reviewers actifs + expertise specialty matching + workload distribution + escalation senior reviewers > 100000 MAD claims), un **expertise review interface** Sprint 18 reviewer dashboard avec photos before/after comparison side-by-side via Sprint 10 + comparison IA estimation Sprint 20 vs garage final amount + customer signature display Sprint 10 + verification checklist 8 items + approve/reject/dispute buttons + override IA rationale textarea, un **IA estimation override authority** reviewer can override Sprint 20 estimation avec rationale obligatoire + audit log Sprint 7 + feedback loop dataset Sprint 20 retraining, un **customer signature acceptance** post-completion garage work customer signs acceptance via Sprint 19 mobile signature pad ou Sprint 18 e-signature Sprint 10, un **payout customer integration Sprint 11** post-validation approve trigger PayoutInstantService.processCustomerPayout(sinistre_id, customer_id, final_amount_mad, payment_method) via mobile wallet BAM ou bank transfer + integration Sprint 13 Analytics, un **garage compensation calculation** tiered rules service `GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation(garage_id, sinistre_id, work_type, final_amount_mad)` (commission percentage 70-85% selon tier garage + bonus on-time + penalty late + custom contracts insurer-specific Sprint 32+), trigger PayoutInstantService.processGaragePayout post-customer-payout, un **dispute resolution workflow** 3 niveaux escalation : niveau 1 reviewer second opinion (24h SLA), niveau 2 senior reviewer + manager review (48h SLA), niveau 3 ethics committee + legal review (1 week SLA), un **integration Sprint 16 sinistre lifecycle** transitions `assigned -> completed -> validated -> finalized` ou `disputed` ou `rejected` avec audit Sprint 7 + emit events `sinistre.work_completed_by_garage` + `sinistre.validated_by_reviewer` + `sinistre.payout_triggered` + `sinistre.finalized` + `sinistre.disputed`, un **reviewer dashboard** Sprint 18 admin section `/admin/reviewer/expertise` (list pending expertises sorted priority + filters status + assignment workload + statistics personal performance + leaderboard), des **endpoints REST workflow expertise** consumables Sprint 18 admin + Sprint 19 mobile reviewer + Sprint 22+ garage app future, et la suite de tests E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant assignment automatic + photos comparison + IA override + customer signature + payouts triggers + garage compensation tiers + dispute escalation 3 levels.

Le sprint **ferme boucle complete lifecycle sinistre** : declaration mobile Sprint 19 -> IA Sprint 20 -> review Sprint 16 -> dispatch Sprint 21 -> work garage -> validation Sprint 22 -> payment Sprint 11 = experience end-to-end customer + garage + insurer Demo Day complete.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 22

Le sprint construit workflow expertise + payouts triggers + dispute resolution + integrations. Concretement, il livre la **decision-023 ratification** documentant workflow expertise + payment trigger + dispute resolution, les **4 entites domain expertise** (`Expertise` aggregate + `ExpertiseValidation` checklist items + `PayoutTrigger` orchestration + `Dispute` resolution workflow) + RLS multi-tenant + indexes performance, le **package `@insurtech/expertise-workflow`** v1.0.0 NestJS module, un **service `ExpertiseAssignmentService`** automatic routing reviewers via algorithm (load balancing reviewers actifs avec capacity < 10 expertises in-review + expertise specialty matching auto_repair/dommages_corporels/RC_familiale + workload distribution round-robin weighted + escalation senior reviewers automatic si claim > 100000 MAD strict + manual override admin), un **service `ExpertiseReviewService`** orchestrate review flow (assignment notification reviewer Sprint 9 + load photos before/after Sprint 10 + load IA estimation Sprint 20 + load customer signature Sprint 10 + verification checklist 8 items + decision approve/reject/dispute + audit log Sprint 7), checklist verification 8 items :
1. Photos before quality acceptable (min 5 photos, eclairage, angles)
2. Photos after quality acceptable (min 5 photos, comparable angles)
3. Photos coherence (vehicule meme + dommages reparation evident)
4. Montant final coherent estimation IA Sprint 20 (deviation < 30% acceptable, > 30% override rationale obligatoire)
5. Customer signature presence + timestamp valide
6. Garage compliance documents (facture detaillee + breakdown pieces + main d'oeuvre)
7. Delais respect (assignment completed dans 14j defaut)
8. Quality reparation visuelle (no defaults evidents)

Un **service `IAEstimationOverrideService`** reviewer override Sprint 20 estimation avec rationale obligatoire (compare estimation_ia_montant_mad vs reviewer_final_amount_mad + deviation_percent calculated + rationale textarea min 50 chars + audit log Sprint 7 + feedback Sprint 20 dataset retraining via tag `human_override`), un **service `CustomerSignatureService`** post-completion garage work :
- Customer signs acceptance via Sprint 19 mobile signature pad react-native-signature-canvas (mobile flow declaration + acceptance same component)
- Customer signs acceptance via Sprint 18 e-signature Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow (web flow alternative)
- Signature timestamp + photos final + customer agreement statement signed audit Sprint 7
- Required avant trigger payouts

Un **service `PayoutCustomerService`** post-validation approve trigger payouts customer :
- Integration Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.processCustomerPayout(sinistre_id, customer_id, final_amount_mad, payment_method) selon customer preference (mWallet BAM via M-Wallet/Bank-Wallet + bank transfer SEPA-equivalent + CashPlus pickup-MAD-cash)
- Audit log Sprint 7
- Notification Sprint 9 customer "Indemnisation X MAD envoyee, methode Y, ref Z" via push native Sprint 19 + email Sprint 9
- Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline payouts

Un **service `GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation(garage_id, sinistre_id, work_type, final_amount_mad)`** tiered rules :
- Tier basic 70% commission post-completion verification
- Tier verified 75% commission (verified status garage Sprint 21)
- Tier premium 80% commission (rating > 4.5 + > 50 completed assignments)
- Tier partner 85% commission (contractual partner Sprint 23 future + insurer-specific)
- Bonus +5% on-time completion (within ETA estimated)
- Penalty -10% late completion (> 50% over ETA estimated)
- Custom contracts insurer-specific Sprint 32+ override defaults

Trigger PayoutInstantService.processGaragePayout post-customer-payout (sequence customer-first then garage), notification Sprint 9 garage "Compensation X MAD versee" + email + analytics.

Un **service `DisputeResolutionService`** workflow 3 niveaux escalation :
- Niveau 1 (24h SLA) : reviewer second opinion (different reviewer assigned + review original expertise + decision : confirme original / overrules)
- Niveau 2 (48h SLA) : senior reviewer + manager review (decision : confirme niveau 1 / overrules + can mediate customer-garage negotiation)
- Niveau 3 (1 week SLA) : ethics committee + legal review (final binding decision + ACAPS reporting if applicable)
- Audit log Sprint 7 each escalation
- Notifications Sprint 9 stakeholders each level

Un **integration Sprint 16 sinistre lifecycle** complete :
- Listener event `dispatch.assignment_completed` (Sprint 21 garage marks work done) -> trigger ExpertiseAssignmentService.assignReviewer
- Listener event `customer.signature_acceptance_received` -> trigger ExpertiseReviewService.startReview
- Sprint 16 SinistreService.transition orchestrate :
  - assigned -> completed (garage mark work done)
  - completed -> validated (reviewer approve)
  - validated -> finalized (payouts completed)
  - completed -> disputed (reviewer dispute or customer/garage)
  - disputed -> validated (resolution favor approve)
  - disputed -> rejected (resolution favor reject)
- Emit events finalization complete

Un **reviewer dashboard** Sprint 18 admin section `/admin/reviewer/expertise` :
- List pending expertises sorted priority (claim_amount DESC + age DESC)
- Filters status assigned/in_review/disputed
- Assignment workload personnal (max 10 in-review)
- Statistics personal performance (avg_review_time + approve_rate + dispute_rate)
- Leaderboard reviewers performance
- Detail expertise page comparison photos before/after + IA estimation + customer signature + checklist + decision

Des **endpoints REST workflow expertise** controller `expertise.controller.ts` 14 endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/assignments/{sinistre_id}/assign` manual assign reviewer
- `GET /api/v1/expertise/assignments/me` reviewer personal pending
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/review/start` start review
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/review/approve` decision approve + final_amount + rationale
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/review/reject` decision reject + reason
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/dispute/raise` raise dispute
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/dispute/escalate` escalate to next level
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/dispute/resolve` resolve dispute decision
- `POST /api/v1/expertise/{id}/ia-override` override IA estimation rationale
- `POST /api/v1/sinistre/{id}/customer/signature` customer signature acceptance
- `GET /api/v1/expertise/{id}` details
- `GET /api/v1/expertise/{id}/checklist` verification checklist results
- `GET /api/v1/admin/expertise/leaderboard` reviewers performance
- `POST /api/v1/admin/expertise/statistics` admin reports

OpenAPI 3.1 + permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15, 16+ tests E2E, et **30+ tests E2E** covering assignment automatic + photos comparison + checklist 8 items + IA override rationale + customer signature + customer payout Sprint 11 + garage compensation tiered + dispute escalation 3 levels + integration Sprint 16 lifecycle finalization + reviewer dashboard.

### Exclus du Sprint 22 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas les **garage networks contractual** (groupes garages contractuels insurer-specific avec custom contracts compensation) differes Sprint 23 (Sprint 22 fait tiered defaults + custom Sprint 23), les **legal automation disputes** (avocat AI + jurisprudence) differes Phase 7+, les **fraud detection sinistres** AI-powered patterns differes Sprint 30+ (Sprint 22 reviewer manual detection), les **customer satisfaction surveys** post-validation differes Phase 7+ (Sprint 22 ratings Sprint 21 suffisent), les **reviewer training certifications** workflow defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 22 admin manual approval), les **multi-language reviewer notes** AR/EN differs Phase 7+ (Sprint 22 FR uniquement), les **video conferencing** disputes resolution defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 22 email + comments uniquement), et les **AI-powered checklist** auto-validation defers Sprint 30+ (Sprint 22 manual checklist + IA estimation override).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Le sprint 22 **ferme cycle complete lifecycle sinistre** = experience end-to-end Demo Day. Quatre dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, sans Sprint 22 le workflow Skalean s'arrete a "garage en route" + "work in progress" sans validation finale = experience customer incomplete. Customer accident -> declare -> IA -> dispatch -> garage -> ... -> ? Sprint 22 ferme l'experience : work complete -> reviewer valide -> customer signe -> payouts effectues. Demo Day investisseurs voient cycle complete = produit fini, pas demo partielle.

Deuxiemement, **integration Sprint 11 payouts BAM** finalise proof point souverainete payments MA. Sprint 11 livre PayoutInstantService capability. Sprint 22 trigger cette capability dans workflow reel (customer indemnisation + garage compensation). Sans Sprint 22, Sprint 11 reste capability theorique. Avec Sprint 22, demonstration concrete BAM mobile wallet + bank transfers MAD live Demo Day.

Troisiemement, le **workflow expertise human-in-the-loop** complete decision-021 ethics safeguards Sprint 20. Sprint 20 IA estimation = recommendation only par design. Sprint 22 = reviewer human valide ou override IA recommendation. Sans Sprint 22, decision-021 reste theorique. Avec Sprint 22, demonstration concrete human-in-the-loop fonctionnel ACAPS ethics compliant.

Quatriemement, **dispute resolution workflow 3 niveaux** protege Skalean reputationally + legally. Marketplaces dispute = inevitable (customer-garage disagreements). Workflow structured escalation 3 levels (reviewer second opinion + senior + ethics committee) demontre Skalean responsible marketplace = trust customers + garages + insurers + regulators. Sans dispute workflow, Skalean reputation risk + ACAPS compliance risk.

---

## ARCHITECTURE CIBLE -- WORKFLOW EXPERTISE

### Package @insurtech/expertise-workflow

```
repo/packages/expertise-workflow/
├── package.json                       # @insurtech/expertise-workflow v1.0.0
├── src/
│   ├── entities/
│   │   ├── expertise.entity.ts                # Aggregate root
│   │   ├── expertise-validation.entity.ts     # Checklist items
│   │   ├── payout-trigger.entity.ts           # Orchestration
│   │   └── dispute.entity.ts                  # Resolution workflow
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── expertise-assignment.service.ts    # Routing reviewers
│   │   ├── expertise-review.service.ts        # Review flow
│   │   ├── ia-estimation-override.service.ts  # Sprint 20 override
│   │   ├── customer-signature.service.ts      # Sprint 10 + Sprint 19 mobile
│   │   ├── payout-customer.service.ts         # Sprint 11 trigger
│   │   ├── garage-compensation.service.ts     # Tiered rules
│   │   ├── dispute-resolution.service.ts      # 3 levels escalation
│   │   └── reviewer-dashboard.service.ts      # Statistics + leaderboard
│   ├── listeners/                             # Kafka consumers
│   │   ├── assignment-completed-listener.ts   # Sprint 21
│   │   ├── customer-signature-listener.ts
│   │   └── payout-completed-listener.ts       # Sprint 11
│   ├── controllers/
│   │   └── expertise.controller.ts            # 14 endpoints
│   └── public.ts
└── tests/
```

### Decision-023 -- Workflow Expertise + Payment + Dispute Resolution

**Justification** :
- (a) Human-in-the-loop validation complete decision-021 IA ethics
- (b) Payouts trigger Sprint 11 finalise proof point souverainete BAM
- (c) Dispute resolution 3 levels protege marketplace trust
- (d) Garage compensation tiered align incentives
- (e) Cycle complete declaration -> payment = experience Demo Day

**Architecture** :
- 4 entites domain expertise + RLS
- Assignment routing automatic + escalation senior > 100k MAD
- Review checklist 8 items + IA override rationale
- Customer signature Sprint 10 + Sprint 19 mobile
- Payouts Sprint 11 sequenced (customer-first then garage)
- Garage compensation tiered + bonus/penalty
- Dispute escalation 3 levels SLA strict

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-022 + **decision-023** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-011 pci-dss-no-card-storage** : strict (Sprint 11 payouts no card data).

**Decision-012 payout-instant-mwallet-bam** : utilise pour customer payouts.

**Decision-016 srf-schema-registry** : events canonical `expertise.*` + `dispute.*` Avro.

**Decision-017 sinistre-lifecycle-state-machine** : transitions assigned -> completed -> validated -> finalized.

**Decision-020 mobile-stack** : Sprint 19 mobile customer signature pad reuse.

**Decision-021 ai-vision-estimation-photos** : Sprint 20 IA override authority reviewer.

**Decision-022 dispatch-matching** : Sprint 21 assignment lifecycle precedes Sprint 22 workflow.

**Decision-023 workflow-expertise-validation-payment** (NOUVEAU - ratification 7 juin 2026) : adoption **workflow expertise human validation post-completion + IA estimation override authority + payouts Sprint 11 trigger automatic + garage compensation tiered + dispute resolution 3 niveaux escalation**. Justification : (a) Human-in-the-loop complete IA Sprint 20 ethics, (b) Payouts BAM Sprint 11 proof point souverainete, (c) Dispute trust marketplace, (d) Cycle complete Demo Day. Architecture : 4 entites + assignment routing + checklist 8 items + override rationale + signature + payouts sequenced + tiered compensation + dispute 3 levels.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Reviewer workload imbalance.** Routing automatic reviewers high load = burnout. Solution : load balancing strict max 10 in-review + escalation senior si surcharge + analytics reviewer wellbeing.

**Piege 2 : IA override rationale quality.** Reviewer override sans rationale = pas de feedback Sprint 20 dataset. Solution : rationale min 50 chars + structured fields (issue_type + correction + evidence) + audit Sprint 7 + Sprint 20 retraining feedback.

**Piege 3 : Customer signature dispute timing.** Customer signs but later disputes. Solution : signature timestamp + photos final + agreement statement + 14j window dispute strict + escalation Sprint 22 niveau 1 si applicable.

**Piege 4 : Sprint 11 payout failures.** BAM mobile wallet API down -> payout fail. Solution : retry queue persistent + circuit breaker Sprint 14 + fallback bank transfer + notification customer.

**Piege 5 : Garage compensation tier calculation race.** Tier upgrades pendant payout calculation. Solution : SELECT FOR UPDATE garage record + cache tier 1h TTL + audit any tier change.

**Piege 6 : Dispute escalation SLA breaches.** Niveau 1 reviewer slow > 24h SLA. Solution : SLA monitoring Sprint 9 alerts + auto-escalate next level + manager visibility.

**Piege 7 : Sequencing payouts customer-first vs garage-first.** Garage compensation depend customer payout success. Solution : sequence strict customer-first then garage + transaction atomic if possible + retry pattern.

**Piege 8 : Review checklist 8 items fatigue.** Reviewers skip items = quality risk. Solution : mandatory all 8 items + visual progress + estimated time + breaks reminders.

**Piege 9 : IA override frequency tracking.** > 30% override = Sprint 20 model quality issue. Solution : weekly override rate monitoring + Sprint 20 alerts si > threshold + retraining trigger.

**Piege 10 : Dispute resolution legal complexity.** Niveau 3 ethics committee + legal complex. Solution : structured workflow + legal review checklist + ACAPS reporting strict if applicable.

---

## LISTE DES 12 TACHES -- SCOPE DETAILLE

### Tache 6.2.1 -- Architecture + Decision-023 + Design Doc

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Gateway architectural Sprint 22 + decision-023 ratifiee + design doc 20+ pages + peer-review.

**Livrables** : `decisions/023-workflow-expertise-validation-payment.md` 8-12 pages, `docs/architecture/expertise-workflow-design.md` 20+ pages (architecture + checklist + assignment routing + IA override + signature + payouts sequenced + compensation tiered + dispute 3 levels), kickoff Phase 5 Sprint 2, peer-review Saad+Abla.

### Tache 6.2.2 -- Entites Expertise + Validation + PayoutTrigger + Dispute

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

4 entites domain expertise + RLS + indexes.

**Livrables** : Package `@insurtech/expertise-workflow` v1.0.0, migration `CreateExpertiseEntities` (`expertises` aggregate + `expertise_validations` checklist 8 items + `payout_triggers` orchestration sequenced + `disputes` resolution workflow), RLS active 4 tables, indexes performance (tenant_id+status partial + reviewer_id+status workload + sinistre_id + dispute escalation_level), enums TypeScript explicit, seeds initial 10 reviewers fictifs + specialties + workload demo, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 6.2.3 -- Expertise Assignment Service Automatic Routing

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE | 140-180 KB**

Routing automatic reviewers + load balancing + escalation senior.

**Livrables** : Service `ExpertiseAssignmentService.assignReviewer(sinistre_id)` algorithm (query reviewers active + workload < 10 in-review + specialty matching sinistre branche + round-robin weighted by historical accuracy + escalation senior automatic si claim > 100000 MAD strict + manual override admin), helper `getReviewerWorkload(reviewer_id)` (count expertises status='in_review' + 'assigned'), `escalateToSenior(reviewer_id, claim_amount)` (find senior reviewers + assign highest priority), endpoint `POST /api/v1/expertise/assignments/{sinistre_id}/assign` manual override admin, integration listener Sprint 21 `dispatch.assignment_completed` -> trigger assignReviewer + notification Sprint 9 reviewer "Nouvelle expertise assignee", 14+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.2.4 -- Expertise Review Service + Checklist 8 Items

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE | 140-180 KB**

Review flow + checklist verification + decision approve/reject/dispute.

**Livrables** : Service `ExpertiseReviewService.startReview(expertise_id, reviewer_id)` (transition status='in_review' + load photos before/after Sprint 10 + load IA estimation Sprint 20 + load customer signature Sprint 10 + initialize checklist 8 items), service `submitChecklistItem(expertise_id, item_index, result, notes?)` (result pass/fail/warning + reviewer_notes + audit log Sprint 7 + persist validation entity), service `approve(expertise_id, final_amount_mad, rationale_text)` (verify all 8 checklist items completed + decision='approved' + transition validated + emit event `expertise.approved`), service `reject(expertise_id, reason)` (decision='rejected' + transition + emit event), service `dispute(expertise_id, reason, initiator)` (create Dispute entity + escalation_level=1 + emit `dispute.raised`), checklist 8 items :
1. Photos before quality acceptable
2. Photos after quality acceptable
3. Photos coherence vehicule
4. Montant coherent IA (deviation < 30% sinon override required)
5. Customer signature presence + timestamp
6. Garage compliance documents (facture detaillee)
7. Delais respect (assignment completed < 14j)
8. Quality reparation visuelle

12+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.2.5 -- IA Estimation Override Service + Feedback Sprint 20

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Reviewer override Sprint 20 + rationale + feedback retraining.

**Livrables** : Service `IAEstimationOverrideService.override(expertise_id, reviewer_final_amount_mad, rationale_text, issue_type)` (compare ia_estimation_montant_mad vs reviewer_final + deviation_percent calculated + rationale min 50 chars + issue_type enum photos_quality/damage_underestimated/damage_overestimated/vehicle_mismatch/other + audit log Sprint 7 + Sprint 20 dataset retraining feedback via tag `human_override` + sinistre.metadata.ai_estimation_override JSONB), monitoring `ia_overrides_rate_weekly` alert > 30% Sprint 20 model quality issue, 10+ tests.

### Tache 6.2.6 -- Customer Signature Acceptance

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Customer signature post-completion garage work via Sprint 19 mobile + Sprint 18 e-signature.

**Livrables** : Service `CustomerSignatureService.captureSignature(sinistre_id, signature_data, source)` source enum 'mobile_pad'|'esign_sprint10', mobile flow via Sprint 19 react-native-signature-canvas (reuse component Sprint 5.4.8 declaration), web flow via Sprint 18 e-signature Sprint 10 SigningWorkflow, store signature + timestamp + photos final + customer agreement statement audit Sprint 7, validation strict (sinistre.status='completed' garage marked done + customer = sinistre.customer_id), required avant trigger payouts, emit event `customer.signature_acceptance_received`, 10+ tests.

### Tache 6.2.7 -- Payout Customer Integration Sprint 11

**6h | P0 CRITIQUE PAYMENTS | 130-170 KB**

Trigger payout customer Sprint 11 post-validation approve.

**Livrables** : Service `PayoutCustomerService.triggerPayout(expertise_id)` (verify expertise.status='approved' + verify customer signature acceptance + Sprint 11 integration PayoutInstantService.processCustomerPayout(sinistre_id, customer_id, final_amount_mad, payment_method) selon customer preference Sprint 8 customer_payout_preference enum mWallet_bam/bank_transfer/cashplus_pickup), audit log Sprint 7, notification Sprint 9 customer "Indemnisation X MAD envoyee" push native Sprint 19 + email Sprint 9, Sprint 13 Analytics ETL pipeline, error handling Sprint 11 failure (BAM down) + retry queue persistent + circuit breaker Sprint 14 + fallback bank transfer si mWallet fail, listener Sprint 11 `payout.completed` event -> mark PayoutTrigger.customer_payout_completed_at + trigger garage compensation next step, 14+ tests integration.

### Tache 6.2.8 -- Garage Compensation Tiered + Sprint 11

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

Garage compensation calculation tiered + Sprint 11 payout.

**Livrables** : Service `GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation(garage_id, sinistre_id, work_type, final_amount_mad)` tiered rules :
- Tier basic 70% commission default
- Tier verified 75% commission (verified status Sprint 21)
- Tier premium 80% commission (rating > 4.5 + > 50 completed)
- Tier partner 85% commission (contractual Sprint 23+ insurer-specific)
- Bonus +5% on-time (within ETA)
- Penalty -10% late (> 50% over ETA)
- Custom contracts Sprint 32+ override defaults

Service `triggerGaragePayout(payout_trigger_id)` post-customer-payout sequence strict, integration Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.processGaragePayout, notification Sprint 9 garage "Compensation X MAD versee" + email + analytics, 12+ tests scenarios tier calculations.

### Tache 6.2.9 -- Dispute Resolution Workflow 3 Niveaux

**8h | P0 CRITIQUE | 150-200 KB**

Dispute resolution 3 niveaux escalation + SLA monitoring + audit complete.

**Livrables** : Service `DisputeResolutionService.raiseDispute(expertise_id, reason, initiator)` (create Dispute entity + escalation_level=1 + assign second reviewer + SLA 24h + emit event), service `escalate(dispute_id)` automatic ou manual (verify SLA breached ou explicit escalate + escalation_level++ + assign next level reviewer + SLA 24h/48h/1week selon niveau), service `resolve(dispute_id, decision, rationale)` decision enum favor_original_approve/favor_dispute_reject/mediation/partial_payout + transition Sprint 16 sinistre status + Sprint 11 payout adjustments si applicable + emit event `dispute.resolved`, escalation niveaux :
- Niveau 1 (24h SLA) reviewer second opinion
- Niveau 2 (48h SLA) senior reviewer + manager review (mediation customer-garage possible)
- Niveau 3 (1 week SLA) ethics committee + legal review (ACAPS reporting si applicable)

SLA monitoring Sprint 9 alerts + auto-escalate next level si breach + manager visibility dashboard, 14+ tests scenarios.

Dashboard reviewer Sprint 18 admin section.

Service `ReviewerDashboardService.getPersonalStatistics(reviewer_id)` + `getLeaderboard(limit, period)`, 10+ tests E2E.

**10h | P0 | 180-240 KB**

Tests E2E exhaustifs + closure Phase 5 Sprint 2.

**Livrables** : 30+ tests E2E organises :

- Suite checklist 8 items 5 tests (all items + IA deviation > 30% override required + customer signature absence rejection + garage compliance fail + delais)
- Suite IA override 4 tests (rationale required + min 50 chars + audit Sprint 7 + feedback Sprint 20 tag)

Benchmarks performance (assignment routing P95 < 200ms + payout trigger P95 < 1s + dispute escalation P95 < 500ms), coverage >= 85%, documentation `tests/e2e/SCENARIOS-EXPERTISE.md`, rapport closure `sprint-22-closure-report.md` documenter cycle complete sinistre operational + Sprint 16 lifecycle finalization + payouts Sprint 11 trigger + dispute resolution + pre-requis Sprint 23 garage networks valides + recommandation GO Sprint 23.

## 25. B-23-sprint-23-garage-networks.md

```md
# B-23 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 23 : GARAGE NETWORK DISPATCH

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 5 Sprint 3 -- DERNIER PHASE 5)
**Phase** : 5 -- Skalean Dispatch Platform (Sprints 21-23)
**Sprint** : 23 / 37 -- Phase 5 Sprint 3 (CLOTURE PHASE 5)
**Position** : Apres Workflow Expertise (Sprint 22), avant Phase 6 Apps Sinistre (Sprint 24)
**Numerotation taches** : 6.3.1 a 6.3.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~80 heures developpement
**Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE NETWORKS (closed marketplace + insurer differentiation)
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Garage Network Dispatch** -- dernier sprint Phase 5 Skalean Dispatch Platform. Le sprint introduit la notion de **networks garages contractuels insurer-specific** : chaque insurer (AssurMaghreb, Atlas Insurance, MediAssur en brands fictifs) peut creer son propre network de garages partenaires avec **custom compensation contracts overriding Sprint 22 defaults**. Pattern type Amazon Flex / Doordash networks vs Sprint 21 open marketplace. Le sprint introduit **decision-024 ratification** garage networks + custom contracts + matching priority + tier auto-upgrade via network membership stages.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un network management complete avec entites domain `GarageNetwork` (id + tenant_id (insurer) + name + description + status active/suspended + created_at + admin_user_id + total_garages_count + metrics + tier_auto_upgrade_enabled boolean), `NetworkMember` (id + network_id + garage_id + tenant_id + status applied/approved/rejected/active/suspended + applied_at + reviewed_at + reviewed_by + custom_tier override basic/verified/premium/partner + custom_priority_score 0-100), `NetworkContract` (id + network_id + tenant_id + name + description + version + custom_compensation_rules JSONB override Sprint 22 defaults + effective_from + effective_to nullable + status draft/active/expired + audit_trail), un **service `NetworkManagementService`** insurer creates and manages own networks (validate uniqueness per tenant + admin permissions Sprint 22+ insurer admin role + audit Sprint 7), un **service `NetworkMembershipService`** garage onboarding flow (apply by garage -> insurer reviews via Sprint 18 admin dashboard -> approve/reject decision + audit + emit events), un **service `NetworkContractService`** custom compensation contracts override Sprint 22 defaults (insurer creates contracts with custom rules `compensation_rules: {base_percent, bonus_rules[], penalty_rules[], custom_clauses[]}` + override matching Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation logic via priority order Network > Tier > Defaults), un **integration Sprint 21 matching priority** service `NetworkAwareMatchingService` extends MatchingService Sprint 21 (priority order : 1. network garages for insurer's customer + 2. preferred garages customer Sprint 21 + 3. open marketplace garages), un **integration Sprint 22 compensation override** service `NetworkAwareCompensationService` extends GarageCompensationService Sprint 22 (lookup active NetworkContract for sinistre.tenant_id + garage_id + apply custom_compensation_rules JSONB OR fallback Sprint 22 tiered defaults), un **tier auto-upgrade workflow** via network membership stages (network member -> tier auto-upgrade by performance + reviews + completed_count + insurer override manual), des **network performance analytics** dashboards Sprint 18 (network total garages + active members + total sinistres processed + avg completion time + customer satisfaction + dispute rate per network), un **cross-network conflict resolution** (garage can be member of multiple networks + matching priority by sinistre.tenant_id strict + custom contracts compete by effective_from latest), un **network admin dashboard Sprint 18** insurer admin manages networks (members + contracts + analytics + invitations), des **endpoints REST networks** 14 endpoints consumables Sprint 18 insurer admin + Sprint 21 matching integration + Sprint 22 compensation integration, et la suite de tests E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant network creation + membership workflow + custom contracts + matching priority + compensation override + tier auto-upgrade + cross-network conflicts + analytics.

Le sprint **cloture Phase 5 SKALEAN DISPATCH PLATFORM complete** : Sprint 21 (Sinistre Workflow Uber-Style open marketplace) + Sprint 22 (Workflow Expertise Validation + Payment) + Sprint 23 (Garage Networks closed marketplace insurer-specific) = pile dispatch complete. Phase 6 Apps Sinistre (Sprints 24-28) constitue prochaine etape avec apps web/mobile/admin specifiques.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 23

Le sprint construit garage networks foundation + integrations Sprint 21/22 + admin dashboard insurer. Concretement, il livre la **decision-024 ratification** documentant garage networks + custom contracts + matching priority + tier auto-upgrade, les **3 entites domain networks** (`GarageNetwork` aggregate + `NetworkMember` membership lifecycle + `NetworkContract` custom rules versioned) + RLS multi-tenant strict (network belongs to insurer tenant) + indexes performance, le **package `@insurtech/garage-networks`** v1.0.0 NestJS module, un **service `NetworkManagementService`** :
- `createNetwork(name, description, admin_user_id)` insurer creates network (verify tenant_id = insurer + unique name per tenant + audit)
- `updateNetwork(network_id, updates)` insurer updates own network
- `suspendNetwork(network_id, reason)` admin suspend network (existing members continue but no new approvals)
- `getNetworkAnalytics(network_id, period)` performance metrics

Un **service `NetworkMembershipService`** :
- `applyForMembership(garage_id, network_id)` garage initiates application (verify garage status active + not already member same network + audit)
- `reviewApplication(membership_id, decision, reviewer_id, custom_tier?, custom_priority?)` insurer reviews via Sprint 18 dashboard (verify reviewer = insurer admin + decision approved/rejected + audit + emit events)
- `suspendMember(membership_id, reason)` insurer suspends member
- `leaveNetwork(membership_id)` garage initiates leave (cancellation_notice 30 days)
- `getMemberStatistics(membership_id)` garage performance in network

Un **service `NetworkContractService`** :
- `createContract(network_id, name, custom_compensation_rules, effective_from)` insurer creates contract (validate compensation_rules schema + version increment + status='draft')
- `activateContract(contract_id)` insurer activates contract (status='active' + previous active expires automatically)
- `lookupActiveContract(network_id, garage_id, sinistre_id)` core lookup for Sprint 22 integration
- Audit trail complete contract changes

Un **integration Sprint 21 matching priority** service `NetworkAwareMatchingService` extends MatchingService Sprint 21 modifies findEligibleGarages :
- Lookup sinistre.tenant_id (insurer)
- Priority order modifications : 
  1. Network members of insurer's networks (boost score +0.20 per network membership)
  2. Customer preferred garages Sprint 21
  3. Open marketplace garages remaining
- Configurable per-tenant `network_priority_weight` env var

Un **integration Sprint 22 compensation override** service `NetworkAwareCompensationService` :
- `calculateCompensation(garage_id, sinistre_id, work_type, final_amount_mad)` lookup priority :
  1. Check active NetworkContract for sinistre.tenant_id + garage_id active membership
  2. If contract -> apply custom_compensation_rules JSONB
  3. Else fallback Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation tiered defaults
- Audit log Sprint 7 explicit which rules applied

Un **tier auto-upgrade workflow** :
- `evaluateTierAutoUpgrade(membership_id)` cron quarterly (verify network.tier_auto_upgrade_enabled + lookup garage performance metrics last quarter + recommended tier based on : completed_count + rating + dispute_rate + on_time_percentage + audit + emit event `network.tier_auto_upgraded`)
- Notification Sprint 9 garage owner + insurer admin

Des **network performance analytics** dashboards Sprint 18 (network total garages + active members + total sinistres processed + avg completion time + customer satisfaction rating + dispute rate per network + revenue generated + comparison vs open marketplace baseline) via Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views,

Un **cross-network conflict resolution** :
- Garage can be member of multiple networks (different insurers) -> matching priority by sinistre.tenant_id strict (only same-tenant networks count)
- Multiple active contracts same network+garage same sinistre -> latest effective_from wins + audit trail
- Cross-tenant data isolation strict (network of insurer A invisible to insurer B)

Un **network admin dashboard Sprint 18** insurer admin route `/insurer/networks/[network_id]` :
- Section "Members" : list + filters status + bulk actions
- Section "Contracts" : list + active + history + create new + edit drafts
- Section "Analytics" : performance dashboards
- Section "Invitations" : send invitations to garages
- Section "Settings" : network config + tier_auto_upgrade toggle

Des **endpoints REST networks** controller `garage-networks.controller.ts` 14 endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/networks` insurer creates network
- `GET /api/v1/networks` insurer lists own networks
- `PUT /api/v1/networks/{id}` insurer updates
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/suspend` insurer suspends
- `GET /api/v1/networks/{id}/analytics` performance metrics
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/members/apply` garage applies (garage side)
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/members/{member_id}/review` insurer reviews application
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/members/{member_id}/suspend` insurer suspends member
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/members/{member_id}/leave` garage leaves
- `GET /api/v1/networks/{id}/members` list members + filters
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/contracts` insurer creates contract
- `POST /api/v1/networks/{id}/contracts/{contract_id}/activate` activate contract
- `GET /api/v1/networks/{id}/contracts` list contracts + history
- `GET /api/v1/networks/garage/me` garage list own memberships

OpenAPI 3.1 + permissions Sprint 7.5a granulaires + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15, et **30+ tests E2E** covering network creation + membership apply -> review -> active + custom contracts compensation override Sprint 22 + matching priority Sprint 21 + tier auto-upgrade quarterly + cross-network multi-insurer garage + analytics dashboards + leave network workflow.

### Exclus du Sprint 23 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas les **vraies compagnies marocaines integrations** (Wafa Assurance + Saham Assistance + AtlantaSanad reels API) differees Sprint 32 (Sprint 23 utilise fictional insurers AssurMaghreb + Atlas + MediAssur synthetic), les **legal contracts e-signature** workflow contracts insurer-garage differs Phase 7+ (Sprint 23 contracts internal definitions only), les **revenue sharing complex calculations** (insurer profit share + Skalean platform fee + network admin fee) differs Phase 7+ (Sprint 23 simple commission + bonus/penalty), les **garage exclusive networks** (garage can only be in 1 network) defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 23 multi-network allowed), les **insurer competition rules** (anti-competitive networks) defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 23 open multi-membership), les **network templates** (pre-built standard network configurations) differs Phase 7+ (Sprint 23 manual creation only), les **smart contract automation** custom contracts via DSL/scripting differs Phase 7+ (Sprint 23 JSONB rules basic), les **garage union/group membership** organisations garage federations defers Phase 7+, et les **AI optimization network composition** (ML recommendations which garages to include) differs Sprint 30+ (Sprint 23 manual curation insurer).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 23 est le **proof point platform multi-tenant** + **closure Phase 5 Dispatch Platform complete**. Quatre dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, Sprint 23 permet **differentiation insurer-side** sur platform Skalean. Open marketplace Sprint 21 traite tous insurers identique = pas de differentiation competitive. Networks Sprint 23 = chaque insurer (Wafa vs Saham vs AtlantaSanad real-world equivalents) peut creer son own network of preferred garages + custom contracts = differentiation strategique. Insurers signent Skalean parce qu'ils peuvent build their own ecosystem dessus.

Deuxiemement, **custom compensation contracts** Sprint 23 align incentives insurer-garage. Wafa Assurance veut compensation higher pour garages premium reliability + reduced delays. Saham Assistance veut compensation lower pour high volume + simple processes. Atlas wants custom KPIs. Sprint 22 defaults uniform = doesn't match insurer specifics. Sprint 23 custom contracts = adaptation per insurer = sales argument enterprise.

Troisiemement, **proof point multi-tenant isolation** strict. Phase 1-4 multi-tenant foundation (decision-002) supports multi-insurer = theory. Sprint 23 networks cross-tenant strict isolation = practice. Network AssurMaghreb invisible AtlantaSanad = data residency + competitive protection. ACAPS + government compliance enterprise insurers requires this.

Quatriemement, **closure Phase 5 Dispatch Platform** complete : Sprint 21 open marketplace + Sprint 22 workflow validation + Sprint 23 networks closed = pile dispatch fully featured. Phase 6 Apps Sinistre (24-28) consume dispatch capabilities. Sans Sprint 23 closure, Phase 5 reste "marketplace + validation" = incomplete commercial offer to insurers.

---

│   │   ├── network-aware-compensation.service.ts # Sprint 22 integration

### Decision-024 -- Garage Networks Insurer-Specific + Custom Contracts

**Justification** :
- (a) Differentiation insurer-side platform Skalean
- (b) Custom contracts align incentives per insurer
- (c) Multi-tenant isolation strict cross-network
- (d) Tier auto-upgrade via network performance
- (e) Closure Phase 5 Dispatch Platform complete

**Architecture** :
- 3 entites domain network + member + contract
- RLS multi-tenant strict (network belongs to insurer)
- Matching priority Sprint 21 boost network members
- Compensation override Sprint 22 via JSONB rules
- Tier auto-upgrade quarterly performance evaluation
- Cross-network garage multi-membership allowed

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-023 + **decision-024** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-002 multi-tenant-3-niveaux** : strict (networks belong to insurer tenant).

**Decision-022 dispatch-matching** : extension via network priority.

**Decision-023 workflow-expertise-validation** : extension via custom compensation override.

**Decision-024 garage-networks-contractual** (NOUVEAU - ratification 8 juin 2026) : adoption **garage networks insurer-specific + custom compensation contracts + matching priority boost + tier auto-upgrade quarterly + cross-network multi-membership**. Justification : (a) Differentiation insurer-side, (b) Custom contracts incentives, (c) Multi-tenant strict isolation, (d) Closure Phase 5 Dispatch Platform. Architecture : 3 entites + RLS strict + integration Sprint 21+22 services aware + tier auto-upgrade quarterly cron.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Cross-tenant network visibility leak.** Network AssurMaghreb seen by Atlas insurer = competitive risk. Solution : RLS strict tenant_id propagation + tests negative cross-tenant access.

**Piege 2 : Custom contracts JSONB schema validation.** Insurer creates invalid compensation_rules JSONB = runtime error. Solution : Zod schema validation strict + version migration if schema evolves.

**Piege 3 : Sprint 22 compensation override priority confusion.** Network contract + tier defaults conflicts = ambiguous calculation. Solution : strict priority order Network > Tier > Defaults documented + audit explicit which applied.

**Piege 4 : Tier auto-upgrade quarterly cron failure.** Cron fails silent = members not upgraded. Solution : Sprint 9 alerts cron failures + manual trigger admin endpoint.

**Piege 5 : Multi-network garage performance attribution.** Garage in 3 networks completes sinistre = stats counted for all 3? Solution : stats per network distinct + sinistre.tenant_id determines which network counts.

**Piege 6 : Network membership leave cancellation period.** Garage leaves abruptly mid-sinistre = customer impact. Solution : 30 days cancellation notice + complete current assignments + audit.

**Piege 7 : Contract version effective_from race.** Multiple contracts effective_from same time = ambiguous. Solution : timestamp microseconds precision + last-write-wins audit + warning UI.

**Piege 8 : Sprint 21 matching priority weight tuning.** Network boost +0.20 too high = always network members win. Solution : configurable env var per tenant + analytics monitoring.

**Piege 9 : Analytics performance materialized views Sprint 13.** Network analytics complex queries = slow. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views + refresh daily.

**Piege 10 : Garage multi-application same network.** Garage applies twice same network = duplicate. Solution : UNIQUE constraint (network_id, garage_id, status='applied'|'active') strict.

---

## LISTE DES 12 TACHES -- SCOPE DETAILLE

### Tache 6.3.1 -- Architecture + Decision-024 + Design Doc

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Gateway architectural Sprint 23 + decision-024 ratifiee + design doc 20+ pages + peer-review.

**Livrables** : `decisions/024-garage-networks-contractual.md` 8-12 pages (contexte differentiation insurer + decision networks + custom contracts + multi-tenant isolation + alternatives evaluees open marketplace only / exclusive networks / templates standardized + consequences + roadmap Phase 7+ smart contracts automation), `docs/architecture/garage-networks-design.md` 20+ pages, kickoff Phase 5 Sprint 3 + clarification Phase 5 closure, peer-review Saad+Abla.

### Tache 6.3.2 -- Entites Networks + Members + Contracts

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

3 entites domain + RLS strict + indexes performance.

**Livrables** : Package `@insurtech/garage-networks` v1.0.0, migration `CreateNetworkEntities` (`garage_networks` aggregate + `network_members` lifecycle + `network_contracts` versioned), RLS strict tenant_id (network belongs to insurer), indexes (tenant_id+status partial + network_id+status members + network_id+effective_from contracts + UNIQUE (network_id, garage_id) members + UNIQUE (network_id, name) networks), enums TypeScript explicit, seeds initial 3 fictional insurer networks (AssurMaghreb-network + Atlas-network + MediAssur-network) + 30 garage memberships (10 per network) + 3 active contracts demo, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 6.3.3 -- Network Management Service

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

Insurer creates and manages own networks + admin permissions + audit.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkManagementService` (createNetwork verify uniqueness tenant + admin role + audit, updateNetwork own network only + audit, suspendNetwork existing members continue no new approvals + audit, getNetworkAnalytics performance metrics via Sprint 13 ClickHouse), 12+ tests.

### Tache 6.3.4 -- Network Membership Workflow

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE | 140-180 KB**

Garage onboarding flow apply -> review -> active + insurer admin Sprint 18 dashboard + audit.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkMembershipService` (applyForMembership garage initiates + verify status + UNIQUE constraint + audit + emit `network.membership_applied`, reviewApplication insurer decides via Sprint 18 + audit + emit `network.membership_approved` ou `network.membership_rejected`, suspendMember reasons + audit, leaveNetwork 30 days cancellation + complete current assignments + audit + emit, getMemberStatistics performance metrics), emit events Kafka topic `garage-networks.events.membership.*`, listener Sprint 9 notifications garage owner + insurer admin, 14+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.3.5 -- Network Contract Service

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE | 140-180 KB**

Custom compensation contracts override Sprint 22 + versioning + audit trail.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkContractService` (createContract validate compensation_rules JSONB Zod schema strict + version increment + status='draft' + audit, activateContract status='active' + previous active expires automatic + emit `network.contract_activated`, lookupActiveContract(network_id, garage_id, sinistre_id) core lookup pour Sprint 22 integration retourne contract.compensation_rules JSONB OR null si pas de contract), audit trail complete contract changes Sprint 7, schema JSON example `{base_percent: 75, bonus_rules: [{condition: 'on_time', percent: 5}, {condition: 'rating > 4.7', percent: 3}], penalty_rules: [{condition: 'late_50_percent', percent: -10}], custom_clauses: ['exclusive_priority', 'quarterly_review']}`, 14+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.3.6 -- Network-Aware Matching Sprint 21 Integration

**6h | P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION | 130-170 KB**

Extension Sprint 21 MatchingService priority boost network members.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkAwareMatchingService` extends MatchingService Sprint 21 modifies findEligibleGarages :
- Lookup sinistre.tenant_id (insurer)
- For each eligible garage query NetworkMembership status='active' for sinistre.tenant_id's networks
- If member of any insurer network -> boost score +NETWORK_PRIORITY_WEIGHT=0.20 configurable
- Priority order final : network members > customer favorites > open marketplace
- Configurable per-tenant via env var + tenant_config

Integration Sprint 21 MatchingService :
- Replace MatchingService Sprint 21 import in dispatch.module.ts with NetworkAwareMatchingService
- Maintain interface compatibility for backward
- Audit log Sprint 7 explicit network boost applied

12+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.3.7 -- Network-Aware Compensation Sprint 22 Integration

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE INTEGRATION | 140-180 KB**

Extension Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService custom contracts override.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkAwareCompensationService` extends GarageCompensationService Sprint 22 modifies calculateCompensation :
1. Check active NetworkContract for sinistre.tenant_id + garage_id active membership via NetworkContractService.lookupActiveContract
2. If contract found -> apply custom_compensation_rules JSONB :
   - base_percent from contract.base_percent
   - bonus_rules iterate conditions matching (on_time, rating thresholds)
   - penalty_rules iterate conditions matching
   - custom_clauses applied as metadata
3. Else fallback Sprint 22 GarageCompensationService.calculateCompensation tiered defaults

Audit log Sprint 7 explicit which rules applied (network_contract_id OR tier_defaults), helper `evaluateBonusRule(rule, garage_metrics)` evaluate condition + return percent, helper `evaluatePenaltyRule(rule, garage_metrics)`, integration Sprint 22 dependency injection :
- Replace GarageCompensationService Sprint 22 import in expertise-workflow.module.ts with NetworkAwareCompensationService
- Maintain interface compatibility backward

14+ tests scenarios (contract found + bonus rules matched + penalty applied + fallback defaults + audit explicit).

### Tache 6.3.8 -- Tier Auto-Upgrade Workflow

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Tier auto-upgrade quarterly cron via network membership performance.

**Livrables** : Service `TierAutoUpgradeService.evaluateMember(membership_id): TierEvaluation` (verify network.tier_auto_upgrade_enabled + lookup garage performance Sprint 21+22 last quarter aggregated : completed_count + rating_average + dispute_rate + on_time_percentage + recommended_tier algorithm : basic if completed<10 OR rating<4.0, verified if completed>=10 AND rating>=4.0 AND no_disputes_serious, premium if completed>=50 AND rating>=4.5 AND dispute_rate<5%, partner if completed>=100 AND rating>=4.7 AND on_time>90% AND custom_clause partner_contract), `runQuarterlyEvaluation()` cron schedule first day of quarter at 04h00 MA (iterate active memberships + evaluate + update custom_tier + audit + emit `network.tier_auto_upgraded` if changed), notification Sprint 9 garage owner + insurer admin, manual trigger admin endpoint Sprint 18, 10+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 6.3.9 -- Network Performance Analytics

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Dashboards Sprint 18 + materialized views Sprint 13 ClickHouse.

**Livrables** : Service `NetworkAnalyticsService.getNetworkMetrics(network_id, period: 'week'|'month'|'quarter'|'year'): NetworkMetrics` aggregate (total_garages + active_members + total_sinistres_processed + avg_completion_time_hours + customer_satisfaction_rating + dispute_rate_percent + revenue_generated_mad + comparison_open_marketplace_baseline), Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views refreshed daily (refresh cron 02h00 MA), dashboard Sprint 18 `/insurer/networks/[network_id]/analytics` charts (line + bar + KPI cards) + period selector, exports CSV/PDF, 8+ tests integration.

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Garage App Foundation + Multi-App Architecture** -- premier sprint Phase 6 Apps Sinistre. Le sprint construit l'app dediee aux garages (web + mobile) et etablit l'architecture multi-app pour la suite de Phase 6. Pattern type Uber Driver app vs Sprint 18 customer portal (= Uber Rider equivalent). Le sprint introduit **decision-025 ratification** multi-app architecture monorepo turbo + shared UI library + design system tokens + garage app dedicated stack reusing Sprint 18/19 patterns.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'apps Skalean multi-cibles avec **monorepo restructure** turbo workspace patterns (apps `customer-portal` Sprint 18 + `customer-mobile-portal` Sprint 19 + new `garage-portal` web + new `garage-mobile-portal` mobile + future `admin-portal` Sprint 26 + future `reviewer-portal` Sprint 26), un **shared UI library** `@insurtech/ui-shared` package extracting reusable components from Sprint 18/19 (buttons + forms + cards + modals + tables + signatures + photo capture + maps + etc.) + Storybook documentation + comprehensive testing, un **design system tokens** package `@insurtech/design-tokens` (colors palette MA-inspired earthy + typography fonts Inter/Roboto + spacing scale 4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64 + shadows + border-radius + animation timings) consumable cross-apps + dark mode support, un **garage app web** Next.js 15 App Router similar Sprint 18 architecture (port 3001 dev) avec SSO Sprint 5 + role='garage_owner' + screens (dashboard + dispatch offers + assignments + ratings history + earnings + network memberships + profile), un **garage app mobile** React Native Expo SDK 50 similar Sprint 19 architecture avec react-native-signature-canvas + maps + push notifications + biometric auth + screens (dispatch offers list + offer detail + assignments + ratings + profile), un **garage app key flows** receive dispatch offer (Sprint 21 integration) + accept/reject within 60s + track assignment real-time (Sprint 21 SSE/push) + submit work completion + view customer signature + receive payouts notifications Sprint 22, des **dashboards garage** (current assignments + completed last 30 days + ratings rolling average + earnings cumulative + network memberships Sprint 23), un **garage profile** management (location + photos + radius_service_km + capacity_concurrent + work_hours + documents legal RC/IF/ICE) + KYC verification status Sprint 21, un **cross-app authentication SSO** Sprint 5 JWT tokens + role-based redirects (garage_owner -> garage-portal, customer -> customer-portal, admin -> admin-portal Sprint 26+, reviewer -> reviewer-portal Sprint 26+), des **multi-app deployment pipelines** CI/CD per-app independent builds + shared library versioning + Docker images optimized + Atlas Cloud Kubernetes deployments + blue-green strategy, des **endpoints REST garage-specific** controller `garage-app.controller.ts` 12 endpoints (dispatch offers received + assignments + ratings + earnings + profile + network memberships), des **real-time updates** SSE Sprint 18 pattern + push native Sprint 19 pattern for garage app, et la suite de tests E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant garage app web/mobile flows + cross-app SSO + shared library components + design tokens + dispatch offers acceptance + assignments tracking + ratings display.

Le sprint constitue **proof point platform multi-actor experience** : pas seulement customer-side (Sprint 18+19) mais aussi garage-side (Sprint 24) + future reviewer-side (Sprint 26+) + future admin-side (Sprint 26+). Demo Day investisseurs voient ecosysteme complete multi-actor = platform mature.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 24

Le sprint construit garage app web + mobile + shared infrastructure + integrations Phase 5. Concretement, il livre la **decision-025 ratification** documentant multi-app architecture + monorepo turbo patterns + shared UI library + design tokens + garage app stack, le **monorepo restructure** dossier `repo/apps/` (customer-portal Sprint 18 + customer-mobile-portal Sprint 19 + garage-portal NEW + garage-mobile-portal NEW + admin-portal Sprint 26+ placeholder + reviewer-portal Sprint 26+ placeholder) + dossier `repo/packages/ui-shared` NEW + `repo/packages/design-tokens` NEW + turbo.json workspace optimization (cache build outputs + dependency graph + parallel execution + pipeline tasks), le **shared UI library** package `@insurtech/ui-shared` v1.0.0 extracting reusable components from Sprint 18 customer-portal :
- Buttons (Primary, Secondary, Ghost, Destructive)
- Forms (Input, Select, Textarea, Checkbox, Radio, DatePicker, FileUpload)
- Cards (Card, CardHeader, CardBody, CardFooter)
- Modals (Modal, ConfirmModal, FormModal)
- Tables (Table, TablePagination, TableFilters)
- Specialized (SignatureCanvas wrap react-native-signature-canvas + PhotoCapture wrap camera + MapPicker wrap Mapbox/Leaflet + StarRating + Timeline + ProgressBar)
- Navigation (Sidebar, Topbar, BreadcrumbBar, BottomTabs mobile)
- Feedback (Toast, Alert, Skeleton, Spinner, EmptyState)

Tests Vitest unit + Storybook stories per component + accessibility tests Sprint 18 a11y patterns, le **design system tokens** package `@insurtech/design-tokens` v1.0.0 :
- Colors (primary palette MA-inspired earthy red-orange #C84B31 + secondary deep blue #1E3A5F + neutrals grays + semantic success/warning/error)
- Typography (Inter sans-serif primary + Roboto fallback + scale 12/14/16/18/20/24/30/36/48/64 + weights 400/500/600/700)
- Spacing (scale 0/4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64/96 px units consistent)
- Shadows (sm/md/lg/xl Tailwind-like)
- Border-radius (sm 4px + md 8px + lg 12px + xl 16px + full 9999px)
- Animation timings (fast 150ms + base 250ms + slow 500ms)
- Dark mode support via CSS variables
- Tailwind config preset shared

Un **garage app web** dans `repo/apps/garage-portal` Next.js 15 App Router :
- Port 3001 dev
- SSO Sprint 5 NextAuth + role='garage_owner' enforced strict
- Layout shared sidebar + topbar
- Routes :
  - `/dashboard` (KPIs overview)
  - `/dispatch/offers` list pending offers Sprint 21 + accept/reject within 60s
  - `/dispatch/offers/[id]` detail offer
  - `/assignments` current + completed list
  - `/assignments/[id]` detail (sinistre info + customer signature + IA estimation Sprint 20 + work submission)
  - `/ratings` history
  - `/earnings` cumulative + breakdown per assignment
  - `/networks` memberships Sprint 23
  - `/profile` garage info + edit
- i18n FR + AR via next-intl Sprint 18 pattern
- React Query for state management
- Real-time SSE `/api/sse/dispatch` filtered garage_id

Un **garage app mobile** dans `repo/apps/garage-mobile-portal` React Native Expo SDK 50 :
- App.tsx + Expo Router file-based navigation
- SSO Sprint 5 + biometric auth react-native-keychain
- Push notifications Sprint 9 garage-specific categories (dispatch_offer_received + dispatch_assignment_accepted + dispatch_eta_updated + payout_received)
- react-native-signature-canvas reuse Sprint 19 pattern (signature acceptance for work submission)
- Maps react-native-maps for navigation to sinistre location
- Screens :
  - `index.tsx` dashboard
  - `dispatch/offers/index.tsx` list pending
  - `dispatch/offers/[id].tsx` detail + accept/reject
  - `assignments/index.tsx` list
  - `assignments/[id].tsx` detail + work submission
  - `ratings.tsx` history
  - `earnings.tsx`
  - `profile.tsx`
- Battery-aware GPS location updates Sprint 21 pattern adaptive (5s active assignment + 60s idle)

Un **garage app key flows** :
- Receive dispatch offer push notification Sprint 21 -> open app -> view detail -> accept/reject within 60s countdown timer
- Accept assignment -> Sprint 21 OfferAcceptanceService -> Sprint 16 transition assigned -> real-time tracking GPS
- Navigate to sinistre location via react-native-maps integration external navigation app (Apple Maps + Google Maps + Waze deep links)
- Arrive at sinistre -> mark arrival via app
- Submit work completion (photos after via PhotoCapture + breakdown costs + work_description + signature on tablet customer-side)
- Receive customer signature Sprint 22 via SSE notification + customer-portal Sprint 18 e-signature OR mobile signature pad Sprint 19
- Receive payout notification Sprint 22 PayoutGarageService

Un **garage profile management** :
- Location pin map + address
- Photos local garage 5+ photos
- Radius_service_km slider 5-50
- Capacity_concurrent input 1-20
- Work_hours weekly schedule
- Documents legal RC + IF + ICE upload Sprint 10
- KYC verification status badge

Un **cross-app authentication SSO** :
- Sprint 5 NextAuth shared backend
- JWT tokens with role claim
- Login redirect by role :
  - role='garage_owner' -> garage-portal :3001
  - role='customer' -> customer-portal :3000 Sprint 18
  - role='admin' OR 'super_admin' -> admin-portal Sprint 26+ :3002
  - role='reviewer' OR 'senior_reviewer' -> reviewer-portal Sprint 26+ :3003
  - role='insurer_admin' -> insurer-portal embedded customer-portal Sprint 23
- Single sign-on across apps via shared session cookie + domain *.skalean.local dev / skalean.ma prod

Des **multi-app deployment pipelines** :
- CI/CD per-app independent builds via GitHub Actions Sprint 1 pattern extension
- turbo build cache shared across apps
- Docker images optimized per-app
- Atlas Cloud Kubernetes deployments :
  - customer-portal :3000 namespace `customer`
  - garage-portal :3001 namespace `garage`
  - admin-portal Sprint 26+ :3002 namespace `admin`
  - reviewer-portal Sprint 26+ :3003 namespace `reviewer`
- Blue-green deployment strategy per-app
- Shared library versioning semantic versioning + dependency lock workspace

Des **endpoints REST garage-specific** controller `garage-app.controller.ts` 12 endpoints (cf B-24 details), des **real-time updates** SSE Sprint 18 pattern garage-side + push native Sprint 19 garage categories, et **30+ tests E2E** covering garage app web/mobile flows + cross-app SSO + shared library components + design tokens + dispatch offers acceptance + assignments + ratings + earnings + profile management.

### Exclus du Sprint 24 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas le **cross-tenant framework** patterns reusable defers Sprint 25 (Sprint 24 utilise patterns Sprint 18+19 reuse, framework Sprint 25), les **admin portal** application dedicated defers Sprint 26 (Sprint 24 placeholder admin-portal directory), les **reviewer portal** application dedicated defers Sprint 26 (Sprint 24 reviewer dashboard inside Sprint 18 admin section), les **tenants management** features defers Sprint 27 (Sprint 24 single tenant focus per app session), les **admin reports compliance** defers Sprint 28, les **app store deployment** iOS App Store + Android Play Store mobile app defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 24 Expo Go dev + EAS Build internal distribution), les **app icons + splash screens** custom defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 24 default Expo placeholder), les **a/b testing infrastructure** defers Phase 7+, les **garage app analytics** advanced behavioral tracking defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 24 basic Sprint 13 events), et les **garage app offline mode** sync defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 24 online-only).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 24 est **inflection multi-actor experience** et **foundation Phase 6 Apps Sinistre**. Trois dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, sans **garage app dedicated**, garage owners experience Skalean only via push notifications + no UI = friction adoption. Sprints 18+19 servent customer-side only. Garages recoivent offers via SMS + push generic Sprint 9 = pas d'interface unifiee pour manage assignments + ratings + earnings. Sprint 24 livre garage-portal web + mobile = parity customer-side = adoption garage massive.

Deuxiemement, **multi-app architecture monorepo** scale future apps. Phase 6 Sprints 26+ ajoutent admin + reviewer apps. Sans architecture proper, chaque app duplique code Sprint 18/19 = maintenance nightmare. Sprint 24 shared UI library + design tokens + turbo workspace = scalable foundation. Decision-025 ratifie architecture pour reste Phase 6+.

Troisiemement, **proof point platform mature multi-actor** Demo Day investisseurs. Showing customer experience (Sprints 18+19) + garage experience (Sprint 24) + future admin/reviewer/insurer experiences (Sprints 23 + 26+) = ecosystem complete. Investisseurs valuent multi-sided platforms 5-10x plus que tools verticaux.

---

repo/

│   ├── customer-mobile-portal/       # Sprint 19 existing

│   ├── garage-mobile-portal/         # NEW Sprint 24

│   └── reviewer-portal/              # Sprint 26+ placeholder (port 3003)
├── packages/
│   ├── ui-shared/                    # NEW Sprint 24
│   ├── design-tokens/                # NEW Sprint 24

│   ├── expertise-workflow/           # Sprint 22
│   ├── garage-networks/              # Sprint 23
│   ├── ai-estimation/                # Sprint 20
│   └── ...
└── turbo.json                        # Workspace optimization

### Decision-025 -- Multi-App Architecture + Shared UI + Design Tokens

**Justification** :
- (a) Garage owners need dedicated app parity customer-side
- (b) Monorepo turbo scales future apps Sprint 26+
- (c) Shared UI library + design tokens avoid duplication
- (d) Cross-app SSO single user experience
- (e) Proof point platform multi-actor Demo Day

**Architecture** :
- Turbo workspace patterns
- @insurtech/ui-shared package
- @insurtech/design-tokens package
- Garage app web + mobile reusing Sprint 18+19 patterns
- Cross-app SSO Sprint 5 JWT role-based redirect

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-024 + **decision-025** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-019 frontend-stack-i18n-accessibility-realtime** : strict (extension Sprint 18 patterns to garage app).

**Decision-020 mobile-stack-react-native-expo** : strict (extension Sprint 19 patterns to garage mobile).

**Decision-022 dispatch-matching** : strict (garage app consumes dispatch offers Sprint 21).

**Decision-023 workflow-expertise-validation** : strict (garage app consume work submission + signature).

**Decision-025 multi-app-architecture-shared-ui** (NOUVEAU - ratification 9 juin 2026) : adoption **monorepo turbo multi-app + shared UI library + design tokens + garage app dedicated stack + cross-app SSO**. Justification : (a) Garage parity customer-side, (b) Monorepo scales Phase 6 + 7, (c) Shared library avoid duplication, (d) SSO single user experience, (e) Proof point platform mature. Architecture : 6 apps separated + 2 shared packages + turbo workspace + cross-app SSO Sprint 5.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Shared UI library breaking changes propagation.** Modifying component breaks all consumer apps. Solution : strict semantic versioning + workspace dependency lock + Storybook visual regression tests + deprecation notices 2 versions ahead.

**Piege 2 : Design tokens inconsistency cross-apps.** Apps use different token versions = visual inconsistency. Solution : single source of truth `@insurtech/design-tokens` + Tailwind preset shared + visual regression tests cross-apps.

**Piege 3 : Cross-app SSO session conflicts.** Multiple apps same browser session = role confusion. Solution : domain *.skalean.local dev / skalean.ma prod single domain + role redirect on login + clear session on logout.

**Piege 4 : Monorepo turbo cache invalidation.** Cache stale shared library changes = broken builds downstream. Solution : turbo dependency graph correct + cache key including package versions + clean cache strategy on lib bumps.

**Piege 5 : Mobile app bundle size growth.** Adding shared library to React Native = bundle 50% larger. Solution : tree-shaking + Metro bundler optimization + lazy load non-critical components.

**Piege 6 : Cross-app navigation deep linking.** Garage app -> customer-portal redirect = browser context loss. Solution : explicit deep link patterns + session preservation across apps.

**Piege 7 : Storybook setup performance.** Storybook hot reload slow with many components. Solution : Vite-based Storybook + selective story loading + composition mode for sub-libraries.

**Piege 8 : Multi-app deployment coordination.** Deploy customer-portal first, garage-portal later = breaking changes if both depend on shared lib new version. Solution : coordinated releases + feature flags + backward compat 2 versions.

**Piege 9 : Garage owner UX vs technical complexity.** Garage owners non-technical = UI must be simple. Solution : user testing 5 garage owners pilot Marrakech + iteration UX + tooltips contextual.

**Piege 10 : SSE/push garage real-time battery drain.** Mobile app constant SSE drains battery. Solution : adaptive frequency Sprint 21 pattern + foreground vs background different strategies.

---

```md
# B-25 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 25 : CROSS-TENANT FRAMEWORK

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 6 Sprint 2)
**Phase** : 6 -- Apps Sinistre (Sprints 24-28)
**Sprint** : 25 / 37 -- Phase 6 Sprint 2
**Position** : Apres Garage App (Sprint 24), avant Admin Foundation (Sprint 26)
**Numerotation taches** : 7.2.1 a 7.2.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~75 heures developpement
**Priorite** : P0 CRITIQUE SECURITY (framework cross-tenant + audit + isolation)
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)
