## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Cross-Tenant Framework** -- etablir patterns reusable pour features qui legitimement span multiple tenants tout en maintenant **isolation strict + audit comprehensive + anonymization quand applicable**. Le sprint introduit **decision-026 ratification** cross-tenant patterns framework + super-admin RBAC extension + allow-list explicit operations + anonymization data sharing + ACAPS compliance considerations.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un **cross-tenant framework** robust avec entites domain `CrossTenantOperation` (id + operation_type + initiator_user_id + initiator_tenant_id + target_tenant_ids[] + scope_data_fields[] + reason + approval_required + approved_by_user_id + approved_at + executed_at + audit_trail JSONB), `CrossTenantPolicy` (id + operation_type + roles_allowed[] + tenant_scope all/specific/anonymized + approval_required + max_records_per_query + audit_retention_years), `AnonymizationRule` (id + entity_type + field_name + strategy enum nullify/hash/mask/aggregate + epsilon_dp differential privacy parameter), un **cross-tenant query framework** decorator `@CrossTenant` patterns NestJS marking endpoints/services explicitly cross-tenant + guard `CrossTenantGuard` enforcing roles + policy + audit obligatoire + max records limits, un **super-admin RBAC extension** Sprint 5+7 roles (`super_admin` + `compliance_officer` + `data_analyst_anonymized` + `support_engineer`) avec permissions cross-tenant granulaires (`cross_tenant.read.aggregated`, `cross_tenant.read.anonymized`, `cross_tenant.read.specific_with_approval`, `cross_tenant.admin.policies`) + 2FA mandatory + audit Sprint 7 reinforced, un **cross-tenant analytics aggregations** Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views avec aggregations safe (counts + averages + distributions per industry + benchmarks anonymized + jamais identifiants individuels), un **industry benchmarks comparisons** dashboards Sprint 18 super-admin (insurer A vs B vs C anonymized rankings + dispute rate + customer satisfaction + ratio garages active + revenue range categories sans identifiants), un **anonymization framework** data sharing service `AnonymizationService.applyRules(data, ruleset)` strategies (nullify identifiants + hash MD5 IDs + mask partial customer phones/emails + aggregate ages/regions into buckets + differential privacy epsilon-DP for sensitive numerics), un **ACAPS compliance reports cross-tenant** super-admin generation aggregated reports for regulatory submissions (volume sinistres MA total + average resolution time + dispute statistics + payouts volume) + scheduled monthly + audit, un **cross-tenant search marketplace optional** customer can search garages across networks Sprint 23 (boundaries clear : open marketplace vs network member status visible only same-tenant), un **audit framework cross-tenant operations** Sprint 7 audit_logs extension dedicated category `cross_tenant_operations` + retention 7+ years legal compliance + monthly review reports, des **performance optimization cross-tenant queries** indexes optimal + materialized views Sprint 13 + query budget limits + circuit breaker si queries > threshold, des **endpoints REST super-admin** controller `cross-tenant.controller.ts` 12 endpoints (cross-tenant analytics + industry benchmarks + ACAPS reports + super-admin admin), et la suite de tests E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant cross-tenant queries authorized + denied attempts + audit complete + anonymization rules + super-admin RBAC + ACAPS reports + performance limits.

Le sprint constitue **proof point security platform mature** ACAPS-grade compliance multi-tenant. Sans Sprint 25, Skalean staff cannot legitimately access cross-tenant data = support limited + analytics impossible + ACAPS reports manual. Avec Sprint 25, framework structured + audited + compliant.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 25

Le sprint construit framework cross-tenant complete + extensions security + audit. Concretement, il livre la **decision-026 ratification** documentant cross-tenant patterns + super-admin RBAC + anonymization + ACAPS considerations, les **3 entites domain** (`CrossTenantOperation` audit log dedicated + `CrossTenantPolicy` allow-list policies + `AnonymizationRule` data sharing rules) + RLS adapted (super_admin role bypass RLS via dedicated SQL functions audited), le **package `@insurtech/cross-tenant-framework`** v1.0.0 NestJS module providing decorators + guards + services, un **decorator `@CrossTenant(operation_type, options?)`** NestJS metadata marking endpoints/services as cross-tenant operations :
```typescript
@CrossTenant('analytics_industry_benchmarks', {
  approval_required: false,
  anonymized: true,
  max_records: 10000
})
async getIndustryBenchmarks() { ... }
```

Un **guard `CrossTenantGuard`** NestJS execution context :
- Verify user role in policy.roles_allowed
- Verify approval if required (lookup CrossTenantOperation status='approved')
- Apply max records limit
- Audit Sprint 7 dedicated category `cross_tenant_operations`
- Reject 403 if not authorized + reason

Un **service `CrossTenantPolicyService`** policy management :
- `createPolicy(operation_type, roles_allowed, options)` super-admin creates policy
- `requestApproval(operation_type, target_tenant_ids, reason)` user requests cross-tenant operation requiring approval
- `approveRequest(operation_id, approver_user_id)` super-admin approves
- `executeCrossTenant(operation_id)` execute approved operation

Un **super-admin RBAC extension** Sprint 5+7 :
- New roles enum extension : `super_admin` (full access Skalean staff) + `compliance_officer` (ACAPS reports + audit reviews) + `data_analyst_anonymized` (analytics anonymized only) + `support_engineer` (specific tenant support with approval)
- Permissions matrix Sprint 7.5a granular (cf B-25 listing)
- 2FA mandatory super-admin + compliance_officer roles
- Audit Sprint 7 reinforced (all operations cross-tenant logged 7+ years retention)
- Session timeout 30 min super-admin (vs 8h regular users)

Un **cross-tenant analytics aggregations** Sprint 13 ClickHouse :
- Materialized view `industry_metrics_monthly_mv` (anonymized insurer A/B/C + sinistres count + avg resolution time + dispute rate + customer satisfaction)
- Materialized view `garage_industry_distribution_mv` (anonymized garage counts per region + tier distribution)
- Refresh daily + audit access
- Aggregation strategies safe (counts + averages + distributions + percentiles + jamais individual IDs)

Un **industry benchmarks comparisons** dashboards Sprint 18 super-admin section :
- Insurer A vs B vs C anonymized rankings (positions only, no names)
- Dispute rate comparison
- Customer satisfaction benchmark
- Ratio garages active per region
- Revenue range categories anonymized (S/M/L brackets)
- Audit access logged

Un **anonymization framework** service `AnonymizationService` :
- `applyRules(data: any[], ruleset: AnonymizationRule[]): any[]` apply rules per field
- Strategies :
  - `nullify` (set to null - identifiants)
  - `hash` (SHA-256 hash - IDs traceable but anonymized)
  - `mask` (partial mask - phone 06**89**12 + email a**@b.com)
  - `aggregate` (bucket - ages 18-25/26-35/...)
  - `differential_privacy` (epsilon-DP noise add - sensitive numerics)
- Validation rules schema Zod strict
- Audit Sprint 7

Un **ACAPS compliance reports cross-tenant** super-admin generation :
- Volume sinistres MA total monthly
- Average resolution time aggregated
- Dispute statistics aggregated
- Payouts volume aggregated mWallet/bank/cash distribution
- Scheduled monthly cron + email Sprint 9 ACAPS contacts
- Audit Sprint 7 7+ years retention

Un **cross-tenant search marketplace optional** :
- Customer searches garages across multiple insurer networks Sprint 23
- Visibility rules : open marketplace garages always visible + network member status visible only same-tenant customer
- Search query enriched per-tenant scope
- Audit search queries

Un **audit framework cross-tenant operations** :
- Sprint 7 audit_logs extension dedicated category `cross_tenant_operations`
- Retention 7+ years legal compliance ACAPS
- Monthly review reports auto-generated for compliance_officer
- Suspicious patterns detection (excessive queries + unusual hours + denied attempts)

Des **performance optimization cross-tenant queries** :
- Indexes Postgres optimal cross-tenant (tenant_id, ...) compounds
- Materialized views Sprint 13 ClickHouse pre-aggregated
- Query budget limits per super-admin (max 100 cross-tenant queries/hour)
- Circuit breaker if query exceeds threshold timeout 30s

Des **endpoints REST super-admin** controller `cross-tenant.controller.ts` 12 endpoints :
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/analytics/industry-benchmarks` aggregated metrics
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/analytics/tenants-comparison` anonymized rankings
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/cross-tenant/policies` create policy
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/cross-tenant/policies` list policies
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/cross-tenant/operations/request` request operation approval
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/cross-tenant/operations/{id}/approve` approve
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/cross-tenant/operations` list with audit
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/acaps/reports/generate` ACAPS report
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/acaps/reports` list reports
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/anonymization/rules` create rule
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/anonymization/rules` list
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/audit/cross-tenant` audit logs cross-tenant

OpenAPI 3.1 + permissions Sprint 7.5a strict + 2FA mandatory + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15, et **30+ tests E2E** covering authorized cross-tenant queries + denied attempts negative + audit complete + anonymization rules verified + super-admin RBAC + ACAPS reports + performance limits + 2FA mandatory.

### Exclus du Sprint 25 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas l'**admin foundation** super-admin app dedicated defers Sprint 26 (Sprint 25 endpoints + auth + framework, app dedicated Sprint 26), les **tenants management** features differs Sprint 27 (Sprint 25 read-only cross-tenant, write tenant management Sprint 27), les **admin reports compliance** non-cross-tenant defers Sprint 28 (Sprint 25 cross-tenant only, per-tenant reports Sprint 28), les **ML training anonymized data sharing** advanced features defers Sprint 30+ (Sprint 25 framework basique, ML pipelines Sprint 30+), les **federated learning** privacy-preserving ML defers Phase 7+, les **data residency cross-border** rules MA -> EU/US data flows defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 25 strict MA only), les **legal frameworks DPA contracts** Data Processing Agreements per-insurer defers Phase 7+, et les **smart contracts blockchain** audit immutable defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 25 Sprint 7 audit logs sufficient).

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 25 est **proof point security platform mature ACAPS-grade**. Trois dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, sans **cross-tenant framework structured**, Skalean staff sont obliges de bypass security via direct DB access for support tasks = security risk + compliance violation. Sprint 25 livre framework approve + audited + safe. ACAPS audits will verify cross-tenant access controls = Sprint 25 is mandatory for compliance.

Deuxiemement, **industry benchmarks anonymized** valuable insurers commercial. Insurers want to know "how do we compare to peers" but legitimately cannot see competitor data. Sprint 25 anonymized benchmarks = compelling value-add Skalean platform = retention + upsell.

Troisiemement, **ACAPS compliance reports cross-tenant** automated. Currently manual aggregation per insurer = error-prone + slow + non-scalable. Sprint 25 automated reports = scalable + auditable + compliant.

---

### Decision-026 -- Cross-Tenant Framework + Super-Admin RBAC + Anonymization

**Justification** :
- (a) ACAPS-grade security multi-tenant access controls
- (b) Skalean staff legitimate support + analytics needs
- (c) Industry benchmarks anonymized commercial value
- (d) ACAPS compliance reports automated
- (e) Audit trail 7+ years legal compliance

**Architecture** :
- 3 entites domain + framework
- @CrossTenant decorator + CrossTenantGuard
- Super-admin RBAC 4 roles + 2FA mandatory
- Anonymization 5 strategies + epsilon-DP
- Sprint 13 materialized views pre-aggregated
- Audit Sprint 7 dedicated category 7+ years retention

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-025 + **decision-026** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-002 multi-tenant-3-niveaux** : strict (cross-tenant framework respects isolation).

**Decision-007 Sprint 5 auth + Sprint 7 RBAC** : extension super-admin roles.

**Decision-026 cross-tenant-framework-super-admin** (NOUVEAU - ratification 10 juin 2026) : adoption **cross-tenant framework + super-admin RBAC extension + anonymization + audit reinforced + ACAPS compliance**. Justification : (a) ACAPS-grade security, (b) Skalean staff legitimate needs, (c) Industry benchmarks anonymized value, (d) Compliance reports automated. Architecture : 3 entites + decorator @CrossTenant + guard + super-admin 4 roles + 2FA + anonymization 5 strategies + audit 7+ years.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Cross-tenant query leak via JOIN.** Developer forgets tenant_id condition = data leak. Solution : @CrossTenant decorator + guard explicit + ESLint rule custom + tests negative obligatoires.

**Piege 2 : Super-admin role abuse risk.** Single super-admin = single point of compromise. Solution : 2FA mandatory + session timeout 30min + audit reinforced + monthly review compliance_officer.

**Piege 3 : Anonymization reverse engineering.** Hash + small dataset = identifiable via linkage attacks. Solution : k-anonymity verification + epsilon-DP for sensitive + minimum aggregation size 100 records.

**Piege 4 : Performance cross-tenant aggregations.** Queries aggregate millions records slow. Solution : Sprint 13 materialized views pre-aggregated + refresh daily + indexes optimal compounds.

**Piege 5 : Audit volume cross-tenant operations.** 10000 ops/day x 7 years = volume huge. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse archival + compression + retention policies strict.

**Piege 6 : Differential privacy epsilon choice.** Wrong epsilon = noise too high OR too low. Solution : research best practices + epsilon 0.1-1.0 typical + dataset-specific tuning + review compliance_officer.

**Piege 7 : ACAPS reports format requirements.** ACAPS requires specific format Excel/PDF. Solution : XLSX skill leverage + PDF generation library + template per report type.

**Piege 8 : Cross-tenant search visibility rules.** Customer searches see other insurers' networks. Solution : strict tenant_id filter + visibility rules documented + tests positive + negative.

**Piege 9 : Materialized views refresh failures.** Refresh fails silent = stale data. Solution : Sprint 9 alerts on failure + retry queue + manual trigger admin.

**Piege 10 : Allow-list policies static vs dynamic.** Policies hardcoded = inflexible. Solution : database-driven policies + super-admin admin UI + audit changes.

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer l'**Admin Foundation** -- migration placeholders Sprint 24 admin-portal + reviewer-portal vers production apps dedicated complete. Le sprint construit 2 apps Next.js separees : admin-portal (port 3002) pour Skalean staff super-admin + compliance_officer + data_analyst_anonymized + support_engineer + insurer_admin + reviewer-portal (port 3003) pour reviewers (reviewer + senior_reviewer) avec workflow expertise Sprint 22 migration depuis Sprint 18 admin section. Le sprint introduit **decision-027 ratification** admin apps architecture + 2FA enforcement Sprint 25 + workflows admin-side + reviewer dedicated experience + cross-tenant views integration Sprint 25.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'un **admin-portal Next.js 15** sur port 3002 production-grade avec **3 sections** principales (super-admin tools cross-tenant + insurer admin networks Sprint 23 + compliance_officer ACAPS reports + reports), un **reviewer-portal Next.js 15** sur port 3003 production-grade avec workflow expertise complete (assignments queue + review checklist 8 items + IA override + decisions approve/reject/dispute + customer signature verification + statistics personal + leaderboard), un **authentication SSO** Sprint 5 + 2FA Sprint 25 enforcement strict (super_admin + compliance_officer + senior_reviewer 2FA mandatory + session 30min reduced), un **super-admin dashboard** cross-tenant integration Sprint 25 (industry benchmarks anonymized + ACAPS reports generation manual trigger + cross-tenant operations audit review + suspicious patterns alerts + system health monitoring), un **insurer admin dashboard** (networks management Sprint 23 + members approval + contracts management + analytics anonymized peer comparison), un **reviewer workflow migration** complete Sprint 22 expertise workflow depuis Sprint 18 admin section to reviewer-portal dedicated (parity features + UX optimized for reviewers + keyboard shortcuts power-users + estimated time per review), un **audit logs admin viewer** dedicated dashboards (Sprint 7 audit logs browsable + filters + Sprint 25 cross_tenant_operations category + export reports + monthly summary), une **multi-app deployment update** Sprint 24 turbo workspace pipeline extended (4 apps total customer-portal + garage-portal + admin-portal + reviewer-portal + 2 mobile apps customer-mobile-portal + garage-mobile-portal), des **endpoints REST admin/reviewer-specific** controller `admin-app.controller.ts` + `reviewer-app.controller.ts` ~20 endpoints, et la suite de tests E2E avec 30+ scenarios couvrant admin-portal + reviewer-portal + 2FA enforcement + super-admin tools + insurer admin + reviewer workflow migration + audit viewer + multi-app deployment.

Le sprint constitue **completion multi-actor experience apps separated** : customer-side Sprint 18+19 + garage-side Sprint 24 + admin-side Sprint 26 + reviewer-side Sprint 26 = ecosystem mature complet apps dedicated per role. Demo Day showing 4 actor apps = wow factor investisseurs platform polish.

- `/admin/insurer/networks/[id]/contracts` contracts management + activate

- `/expertise/[id]/checklist` step-by-step checklist 8 items wizard

- `/statistics` personal performance dashboard (avg_review_time + approve_rate + dispute_rate + IA_override_rate + claims_value_processed_mad)
- `/leaderboard` top 10 reviewers performance ranking (period selector weekly/monthly/quarterly)
- `/profile` reviewer profile + specialties + work schedule

Reviewer-specific features :
- Keyboard shortcuts power-users (A = approve, R = reject, D = dispute, N = next, P = previous, 1-8 = checklist items)
- Estimated time per review displayed
- Auto-save draft decisions every 30s
- Concurrent review prevention (lock expertise during review)
- Mobile responsive but desktop-optimized

Un **authentication SSO** Sprint 5 + 2FA Sprint 25 enforcement :
- admin-portal SSO redirect role-based :
  - super_admin -> /admin/cross-tenant/dashboard
  - compliance_officer -> /admin/compliance/acaps-reports
  - insurer_admin -> /admin/insurer/networks (Sprint 23 visibility tenant_id own)
  - data_analyst_anonymized -> /admin/cross-tenant/analytics (read-only)
  - support_engineer -> /admin/cross-tenant/operations (with approval)
- reviewer-portal SSO redirect :
  - reviewer -> /dashboard
  - senior_reviewer -> /dashboard (full access)
- 2FA Sprint 25 enforcement strict super_admin + compliance_officer + senior_reviewer mandatory + reviewer optional
- Session 30 min reduced super_admin vs 8h regular
- Cross-app SSO Sprint 24 domain shared *.skalean.local/skalean.ma

- ACAPS reports generation manual trigger Sprint 25 AcapsReportsService
- Cross-tenant operations approval queue (CrossTenantOperation status='pending_approval')

- System health monitoring (Postgres + Kafka + Redis + Triton AI + ClickHouse + S3 + Atlas Cloud)

- Members approval queue + tier upgrades

### Exclus du Sprint 26 (defere a d'autres sprints)

Le sprint ne livre pas la **tenants management** features (create tenant + suspend tenant + delete tenant + billing tenant) differs Sprint 27 (Sprint 26 read-only admin Skalean staff per-tenant view via super_admin role + Sprint 27 dedicated tenants management workflows), les **admin reports compliance per-tenant** defers Sprint 28 (Sprint 26 cross-tenant only via Sprint 25, per-tenant compliance Sprint 28), les **billing + invoicing admin** workflows defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 26 admin tools sans billing), les **support tickets system** defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 26 super-admin manual support), les **white-label admin customization** per-tenant defers Phase 7+, les **mobile admin app** dedicated defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 26 web-only admin), les **multi-language reviewer notes AR/EN** defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 26 FR primary), et les **AI-powered admin insights** (predictions + anomaly detection) defers Sprint 30+ (Sprint 26 manual dashboards + alerts).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 26 est **completion multi-actor experience** + **production-grade admin operations**. Trois dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, sans admin + reviewer apps **dedicated**, Skalean staff and reviewers work via Sprint 18 customer-portal /admin section = cluttered UX + conflicts with customer-facing features + difficult role-based access. Sprint 26 separates concerns clean = better UX per role + better security + better scalability.

Deuxiemement, **reviewer workflow dedicated** Sprint 22 deserves dedicated experience optimized power-users (keyboard shortcuts + auto-save + concurrent prevention) vs Sprint 18 admin section generic. Reviewers process 50-100 expertises/day = UX efficiency critical. Sprint 26 reviewer-portal optimized = 2-3x reviewer productivity.

Troisiemement, **proof point platform mature 4 dedicated apps**. Demo Day investisseurs see customer-portal + garage-portal + admin-portal + reviewer-portal = platform polished + scalable + multi-actor experience complete. Vs single SaaS with role-based views = perception less mature. Sprint 26 finalizes multi-app vision Sprint 24.

---

├── customer-mobile-portal/       Sprint 19

├── garage-mobile-portal/         Sprint 24
├── admin-portal/                 Sprint 26 NEW production (port 3002)
└── reviewer-portal/              Sprint 26 NEW production (port 3003)
```

### Decision-027 -- Admin Apps Architecture + 2FA Enforcement

**Justification** :
- (a) Multi-actor UX separation clean
- (b) Reviewer productivity dedicated UX
- (c) Security 2FA enforcement super-admin strict
- (d) Production-grade apps separated K8s namespaces
- (e) Cross-tenant integration Sprint 25 dedicated views

**Architecture** :
- 2 apps Next.js 15 production-grade
- 3 sections admin-portal (super-admin + compliance + insurer-admin)
- 8 routes reviewer-portal workflow complete
- 2FA Sprint 25 enforcement strict
- Cross-app SSO Sprint 24 maintained
- K8s namespaces dedicated

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-026 + **decision-027** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-023 workflow-expertise-validation** : strict (reviewer workflow migration Sprint 22).

**Decision-024 garage-networks** : strict (insurer admin networks Sprint 23 management).

**Decision-025 multi-app-architecture** : extension Sprint 24 patterns to admin + reviewer apps.

**Decision-026 cross-tenant-framework** : strict (super-admin dashboard integration + 2FA enforcement).

**Decision-027 admin-foundation-apps-2fa-enforcement** (NOUVEAU - ratification 11 juin 2026) : adoption **admin-portal + reviewer-portal production-grade dedicated apps + 2FA Sprint 25 enforcement + reviewer workflow migration + cross-tenant integration**. Justification : (a) Multi-actor UX clean, (b) Reviewer productivity, (c) Security 2FA strict, (d) Production-grade dedicated, (e) Cross-tenant integration. Architecture : 2 apps Next.js + 3 sections admin + 8 routes reviewer + 2FA enforcement + cross-app SSO + K8s namespaces.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX DU SPRINT

**Piege 1 : Sprint 18 admin reviewer migration data loss.** Migration from Sprint 18 /admin/reviewer to reviewer-portal :3003 = potential reviewer state loss in-progress. Solution : zero downtime migration + redirect existing /admin/reviewer URLs + complete current reviews before redirect + grace period 14 days.

**Piege 2 : 2FA enforcement bypass attempts.** Super-admin without 2FA = security risk. Solution : middleware enforcement strict + cannot access without 2FA enrolled + recovery codes mandatory + audit any 2FA bypass attempt.

**Piege 3 : Cross-app SSO session conflicts.** User logged in customer-portal + admin-portal simultaneous = role confusion. Solution : single role per session + role-based redirect on login + cookie scope strict.

**Piege 4 : Reviewer concurrent review prevention.** 2 reviewers open same expertise = race condition. Solution : optimistic locking + WebSocket lock notification + auto-release after 1h inactivity.

**Piege 5 : Insurer_admin cross-tenant visibility leak.** Insurer admin sees other insurers' data. Solution : RLS strict tenant_id + tests negative obligatoires + super_admin only cross-tenant.

**Piege 6 : Audit viewer performance large datasets.** Sprint 7 audit logs millions rows = slow queries. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views + indexed columns + pagination + filters server-side.

**Piege 7 : Reviewer keyboard shortcuts UX conflicts.** Shortcut conflicts standard browser. Solution : modifier keys (Alt + ...) + customizable preferences + tooltips visible.

**Piege 8 : Multi-app deployment coordination 6 apps.** Coordinated deployment 6 apps + shared library bumps = complex. Solution : shared library versioning strict + apps deploy independent + feature flags for breaking changes.

**Piege 9 : Super-admin Skalean staff onboarding.** New staff need accounts + 2FA + permissions. Solution : super_admin role onboarding workflow + 2FA enrollment + permissions matrix documented.

**Piege 10 : Performance admin dashboards aggregation.** Cross-tenant analytics + audit reviews + system health = many queries. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views + caching + lazy loading dashboards components.

---

# B-27 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 27 : TENANTS MANAGEMENT

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 6 Sprint 4)
**Phase** : 6 -- Apps Sinistre (Sprints 24-28)
**Sprint** : 27 / 37 -- Phase 6 Sprint 4

**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Tenants Management** complete -- workflows super-admin pour gerer le cycle de vie des tenants insurers : creation onboarding + KYC verification ACAPS-grade + configuration overrides + branding customization optional + suspension safeguards + deletion cascade rules + billing foundation. Le sprint introduit **decision-028 ratification** tenants lifecycle management + KYC workflows + custom configuration framework + billing foundation Phase 7+.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'entites domain **`Tenant`** etendue (id + name + legal_name + registration_number + status enum active/pending_kyc/suspended/deleted_soft + tier enum basic/standard/premium + created_at + activated_at + suspended_at + deletion_scheduled_at + super_admin_owner_id + acaps_license_number nullable + compliance_status enum pending/verified/expired + audit_trail JSONB) + **`TenantConfiguration`** (id + tenant_id + key + value JSONB + category + override_default + audit_trail) + **`TenantBranding`** (id + tenant_id + logo_url + primary_color + secondary_color + font_family + email_signature + active) + **`TenantBilling`** (id + tenant_id + plan_id + billing_cycle monthly/yearly + payment_method + last_invoice_at + next_invoice_at + status active/past_due/suspended) + **`TenantKycDocument`** (id + tenant_id + document_type RC/IF/ICE/acaps_license/bank_certificate + url S3 + uploaded_at + verified_by_user_id nullable + verified_at nullable + status pending/verified/rejected + rejection_reason), un **package `@insurtech/tenants-management`** v1.0.0 NestJS module, un **service `TenantCreationService.createTenant(tenant_data, super_admin_id)`** orchestration (validate unique registration_number + ACAPS license valid format + create Tenant status='pending_kyc' + create initial admin user role='insurer_admin' + send invitation email + audit + emit `tenant.created`), un **service `TenantKycService`** verification workflow (upload documents Sprint 10 S3 + verification states + reject reasons + Sprint 9 notifications + audit + ACAPS-grade traceability), un **service `TenantConfigurationService`** custom configuration overrides per tenant (matching rules + compensation defaults + notifications templates + workflow timeouts + IA estimation thresholds Sprint 20 + override defaults via key-value JSONB + audit changes + Sprint 25 compliance), un **service `TenantBrandingService`** custom branding optional (logo upload + colors palette override design tokens Sprint 24 + email templates branding + activation/deactivation), un **service `TenantSuspensionService.suspendTenant(tenant_id, reason, super_admin_id)`** with safeguards (verify no active sinistres in_progress + grace period 30 days for pending operations + notify all tenant users + suspend access + audit + emit `tenant.suspended`), un **service `TenantDeletionService.deleteTenant(tenant_id, super_admin_id, confirm_token)`** cascade rules (soft-delete only Phase 6+ + retain data 7 years legal + anonymize identifiers PII + cascade soft-delete users + cascade soft-delete networks Sprint 23 + audit irreversible decision), un **service `TenantBillingService` foundation** (plan management + invoice generation foundation + payment recording + status tracking, integration Stripe/payment provider defers Phase 7+), des **admin-portal screens** Sprint 26 `/admin/tenants/*` (list tenants + filters status/tier + create new wizard + detail page sections + KYC documents review + configuration overrides + branding customization + suspension confirm + deletion confirm), des **endpoints REST tenants management** controller `tenants-management.controller.ts` 14 endpoints super-admin only + 2FA mandatory Sprint 26, et **30+ tests E2E** scenarios couvrant tenant creation + KYC workflow + configuration overrides + branding + suspension safeguards + deletion cascade + billing foundation + admin UI.

Le sprint constitue **commercial enablement insurer onboarding** Skalean. Sans Sprint 27, chaque nouvel insurer requires manual DB inserts + custom dev work = non-scalable + erreurs. Avec Sprint 27, super-admin self-service tenant creation + KYC verification structured + custom configurations safe = scalable B2B onboarding.

---

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 27

Decision-028 ratifiee, 5 entites domain (Tenant etendue + TenantConfiguration + TenantBranding + TenantBilling + TenantKycDocument) + RLS strict tenant_id own + indexes performance + audit, package `@insurtech/tenants-management` v1.0.0, services :

**TenantCreationService.createTenant(tenant_data, super_admin_id)** : validate unique registration_number + ACAPS license valid format (regex pattern) + create Tenant status='pending_kyc' + create initial admin user role='insurer_admin' + send invitation email Sprint 9 + audit Sprint 7 + emit `tenant.created` Kafka.

**TenantKycService** :
- `uploadDocument(tenant_id, document_type, file)` Sprint 10 S3 upload + status='pending'
- `verifyDocument(document_id, verifier_id, decision, rejection_reason?)` super_admin OR compliance_officer + audit + emit
- `getKycStatus(tenant_id)` aggregate verification states
- Transition tenant.compliance_status = 'verified' if all 5 documents verified (RC + IF + ICE + acaps_license + bank_certificate)
- Transition tenant.status = 'active' from 'pending_kyc' if compliance_status = 'verified'
- Notification Sprint 9 insurer_admin + super_admin

**TenantConfigurationService** :
- `setConfiguration(tenant_id, key, value, category)` super_admin only + audit + emit
- `getConfiguration(tenant_id, key)` lookup with fallback default value
- `listConfigurations(tenant_id, category?)` browse
- Categories : matching_rules + compensation_defaults + notification_templates + workflow_timeouts + ai_thresholds Sprint 20
- Override defaults via key-value JSONB
- Audit changes Sprint 7

**TenantBrandingService** :
- `setBranding(tenant_id, branding_data)` (logo S3 + colors + font_family + email_signature)
- Activation/deactivation branding
- Default Skalean branding fallback if not set

**TenantSuspensionService.suspendTenant(tenant_id, reason, super_admin_id)** :
- Verify no active sinistres status in_progress (block if > 0)
- Grace period 30 days for pending operations
- Status='suspended' + suspended_at + reason audit
- Notify all tenant users Sprint 9
- Block tenant access (login + API)
- Emit `tenant.suspended`
- Reversible via `reactivateTenant(tenant_id, super_admin_id)`

**TenantDeletionService.deleteTenant(tenant_id, super_admin_id, confirm_token)** :
- Confirm token required (irreversible decision)
- Soft-delete only (status='deleted_soft' + retain data 7 years legal)
- Anonymize PII identifiers (customer phones/emails + garage owners names) via Sprint 25 AnonymizationService
- Cascade soft-delete users + networks Sprint 23 + memberships
- Audit Sprint 7 reinforced
- Emit `tenant.deleted_soft`

**TenantBillingService foundation** :
- Plan management (basic + standard + premium tiers)
- Invoice generation foundation (no payment processing Sprint 27)
- Payment recording manual (Phase 7+ Stripe integration)
- Status tracking active/past_due/suspended

Admin-portal Sprint 26 screens `/admin/tenants/*` :
- List tenants + filters status (active/pending_kyc/suspended/deleted_soft) + tier + sort
- Create wizard 4 steps (legal info + admin user + initial KYC documents + confirmation)
- Detail page `/admin/tenants/[id]` sections (Overview + KYC Documents + Configuration + Branding + Users + Sinistres summary + Billing + Audit)
- KYC documents review workflow + verify/reject
- Configuration overrides editor JSONB
- Branding customization (logo upload + color picker + preview)
- Suspension confirm modal + safeguards check
- Deletion confirm wizard (multiple confirmations + token)

Endpoints REST `tenants-management.controller.ts` 14 endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants` create
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/tenants` list + filters
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}` detail
- `PUT /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}` update
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/kyc/upload` upload doc
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/kyc/verify/{doc_id}` verify
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/configuration` set config
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/configuration` list
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/branding` set branding
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/suspend` suspend
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/reactivate` reactivate
- `POST /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/delete` delete soft
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/billing` billing foundation
- `GET /api/v1/super-admin/tenants/{id}/audit` tenant-specific audit

Super_admin only + 2FA mandatory Sprint 26 + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15, 30+ tests E2E.

### Exclus du Sprint 27 (defere)

Payment processing Stripe integration defers Phase 7+, invoice PDF generation advanced defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 27 foundation only), tenant self-service signup defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 27 super-admin only), white-label admin per-tenant defers Phase 7+, tenant migration tools (data export/import) defers Phase 7+, tenant-specific SSO providers (SAML/OIDC) defers Phase 7+, automated KYC verification via APIs (gov databases) defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 27 manual review), AI-powered fraud detection tenant onboarding defers Sprint 30+, et tenant impersonation by super-admin (support purposes) defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 25 cross-tenant framework alternative).

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 27 est **commercial enablement scaling B2B**. Trois dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, sans **tenants management workflow**, chaque nouvel insurer requires manual DB inserts + custom Skalean dev work + 2-4 weeks setup = non-scalable. Avec Sprint 27, super-admin self-service onboarding 1-2 days = 10x scaling potential.

Deuxiemement, **KYC ACAPS-grade workflow** mandatory regulatory compliance. ACAPS verifies platform handles insurer onboarding with proper KYC = compliance proof. Without Sprint 27, Skalean cannot onboard regulated insurers legally.

Troisiemement, **commercial differentiation custom configurations + branding**. Insurers want platform feels like "their own" not generic Skalean. Custom configurations matching their workflows + branding optional = enterprise sales argument vs generic competitors.

---

### Decision-028 -- Tenants Lifecycle Management + KYC + Configuration + Branding + Billing Foundation

**Justification** :
- (a) Commercial enablement scaling B2B
- (b) KYC ACAPS-grade regulatory compliance
- (c) Custom configurations enterprise sales
- (d) Branding optional differentiation
- (e) Billing foundation Phase 7+

**Architecture** :
- 5 entites domain + RLS strict
- 7 services lifecycle complete
- Admin-portal Sprint 26 UI dedicated
- Super-admin only + 2FA mandatory
- Audit reinforced 7+ years Sprint 25

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-027 + **decision-028** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-002 multi-tenant-3-niveaux** : extension Tenant entity.

**Decision-025 multi-app-architecture** : strict (admin-portal Sprint 26).

**Decision-026 cross-tenant-framework** : strict (super-admin operations).

**Decision-027 admin-foundation-2fa** : strict (2FA mandatory super-admin).

**Decision-028 tenants-lifecycle-management** (NOUVEAU - ratification 12 juin 2026) : adoption **tenants lifecycle complete + KYC ACAPS-grade + configuration overrides + branding optional + suspension safeguards + deletion cascade + billing foundation**. Justification : (a) Commercial scaling, (b) KYC compliance, (c) Enterprise sales, (d) Differentiation, (e) Billing future. Architecture : 5 entites + 7 services + admin UI + super-admin + 2FA.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX

**Piege 1 : Tenant deletion cascade data loss.** Cascade hard-delete = data loss permanent. Solution : soft-delete only + retain 7 years + anonymize PII Sprint 25.

**Piege 2 : Suspension impact active sinistres.** Suspend tenant mid-sinistre = customer impact. Solution : block if > 0 active sinistres + grace period 30 days + notify users.

**Piege 3 : KYC documents PII storage.** Sensitive PII insurer documents = privacy risk. Solution : Sprint 10 S3 encryption + access logged audit + retention policies.

**Piege 4 : Configuration overrides conflicts.** Override + default conflicts = ambiguous behavior. Solution : explicit priority order documented + fallback chain + audit explicit which applied.

**Piege 5 : Branding default fallback missing.** No branding = blank UI. Solution : default Skalean branding fallback always available.

**Piege 6 : ACAPS license format validation.** Wrong regex = reject valid licenses. Solution : ACAPS documentation reference + validation tests positive + negative.

**Piege 7 : Concurrent tenant operations.** Super-admin + insurer_admin same tenant = race. Solution : optimistic locking + audit who acted first.

**Piege 8 : Tenant impersonation risk.** Super-admin acts as insurer_admin = audit unclear. Solution : audit always actor_user_id + impersonation context + Sprint 25 cross-tenant framework.

**Piege 9 : Billing foundation premature optimization.** Implementing payment processing Sprint 27 = scope creep. Solution : foundation only + Stripe Phase 7+ documented.

**Piege 10 : Deletion confirm token security.** Token reuse = accidental deletion. Solution : single-use token + expires 5 min + audit token usage.

---

## LISTE DES 12 TACHES -- SCOPE DETAILLE

### Tache 7.4.1 -- Architecture + Decision-028 + Design Doc

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Decision-028 ratifiee + design doc 20+ pages + peer-review.

**Livrables** : `decisions/028-tenants-lifecycle-management.md`, design doc, peer-review, kickoff Phase 6 Sprint 4.

### Tache 7.4.2 -- Entites Tenant + Configurations + Branding + Billing + KYC

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE | 140-180 KB**

5 entites domain + migrations + RLS + indexes + seeds.

**Livrables** : Package `@insurtech/tenants-management` v1.0.0, migrations 5 tables (tenants etendue + tenant_configurations + tenant_brandings + tenant_billings + tenant_kyc_documents) + RLS strict tenant_id + indexes (status partial + tier + compliance_status + UNIQUE registration_number), enums TypeScript, seeds initial 3 fictional insurer tenants (AssurMaghreb + Atlas + MediAssur) + KYC documents demo + configurations defaults, 16+ tests unitaires.

### Tache 7.4.3 -- Tenant Creation Workflow

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

Service creation + onboarding orchestration + initial admin user + invitation.

**Livrables** : Service `TenantCreationService.createTenant(tenant_data, super_admin_id)` orchestration (validate unique registration_number + ACAPS license format regex + create Tenant status='pending_kyc' + create initial admin user role='insurer_admin' Sprint 5 + send invitation email Sprint 9 + audit + emit), 12+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 7.4.4 -- KYC Verification Workflow

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE | 140-180 KB**

Service KYC + workflow upload/verify/reject + ACAPS-grade traceability.

**Livrables** : Service `TenantKycService` (uploadDocument Sprint 10 S3 + verifyDocument super_admin OR compliance_officer + getKycStatus + transition compliance_status + tenant status active + audit + notifications Sprint 9), 14+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 7.4.5 -- Tenant Configuration Overrides

**6h | P0 | 130-170 KB**

Service configuration custom overrides per tenant + categories + audit.

**Livrables** : Service `TenantConfigurationService` (setConfiguration + getConfiguration with fallback + listConfigurations by category), categories (matching_rules + compensation_defaults + notification_templates + workflow_timeouts + ai_thresholds), JSONB Zod validation, priority order documented, audit, 12+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 7.4.6 -- Tenant Branding Customization

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Service branding optional custom + design tokens override + default fallback.

**Livrables** : Service `TenantBrandingService` (setBranding logo S3 + colors + font_family + email_signature + active toggle + default Skalean fallback), integration design-tokens Sprint 24 dynamic override CSS variables, email templates Sprint 9 branded, 10+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 7.4.7 -- Tenant Suspension Workflow

**6h | P0 CRITIQUE SAFEGUARDS | 130-170 KB**

Service suspension + safeguards verify active sinistres + grace period + notifications + reactivation.

**Livrables** : Service `TenantSuspensionService.suspendTenant(tenant_id, reason, super_admin_id)` (verify no active sinistres > 0 block, grace period 30 days TENANT_SUSPENSION_GRACE_DAYS, status='suspended' + suspended_at + reason audit, notify all users Sprint 9, block access login + API, emit, reversible reactivateTenant), 12+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 7.4.8 -- Tenant Deletion Cascade

**7h | P0 CRITIQUE COMPLIANCE | 140-180 KB**

Service deletion soft + cascade rules + anonymization PII + audit irreversible.

**Livrables** : Service `TenantDeletionService.deleteTenant(tenant_id, super_admin_id, confirm_token)` (confirm token single-use 5 min, soft-delete only status='deleted_soft' + retain 7 years legal compliance, anonymize PII Sprint 25 AnonymizationService customer phones/emails + garage owner names, cascade soft-delete users + networks Sprint 23 + memberships + billing + configurations + brandings, audit reinforced Sprint 7 + 7+ years Sprint 25, emit), 12+ tests scenarios + confirmation flow.

### Tache 7.4.9 -- Tenant Billing Foundation

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

Service billing foundation + plans + invoices generation foundation + status tracking (payment processing Phase 7+).

**Livrables** : Service `TenantBillingService` (planManagement plans basic/standard/premium tiers + billing_cycle monthly/yearly + invoiceGenerationFoundation no payment processing + payment_method recording manual + status tracking active/past_due/suspended), seeds plans defaults, 10+ tests scenarios.

### Tache 7.4.10 -- Admin Portal Tenants UI

**8h | P0 | 150-200 KB**

Screens admin-portal Sprint 26 `/admin/tenants/*` complete UI.

**Livrables** : Screens admin-portal :
- List `/admin/tenants` filters status/tier + sort

- Suspension confirm modal + safeguards check display
- Deletion confirm wizard multi-step + token

14+ tests E2E.

14 endpoints REST super-admin only + 2FA mandatory + permissions.

Implementer l'**Admin Reports + Compliance** -- dernier sprint Phase 6 Apps Sinistre + cloture Phase 6 complete. Le sprint construit le **reports framework** complete avec compliance reports per-tenant (ACAPS-aligned au-dela cross-tenant Sprint 25) + analytics dashboards per-tenant (sinistres + dispatch + workflow + payouts) + custom reports builder (super-admin + compliance_officer) + scheduled reports cron daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly + exports XLSX/PDF/CSV + access control granular + audit complete. Le sprint introduit **decision-029 ratification** admin reports framework + custom builder + scheduling + exports + access control. A la cloture du sprint, **Phase 6 Apps Sinistre COMPLETE** : Sprint 24 multi-app foundation + Sprint 25 cross-tenant framework + Sprint 26 admin foundation + Sprint 27 tenants management + Sprint 28 admin reports + compliance = ecosystem apps complete production-grade ready Phase 7+ (Garage vertical Sprints 29-33 + Integration stubs Sprints 34-35 + Deployment Sprints 36-37).

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'**entites domain reports** (`Report` aggregate id + tenant_id nullable cross-tenant + name + description + category + custom_query_definition JSONB + parameters JSONB + created_by_user_id + scheduled boolean + schedule_cron + last_run_at + status + audit_trail, `ReportRun` id + report_id + parameters_at_run + status pending/running/completed/failed + started_at + completed_at + output_url S3 + audit, `ReportSchedule` id + report_id + cron_expression + active + recipients[]) + RLS adapted (per-tenant scope strict + super_admin cross-tenant via Sprint 25 framework), un **package `@insurtech/reports-framework`** v1.0.0 NestJS module providing report definitions + execution engine + scheduling + exports, un **service `ReportDefinitionService`** CRUD reports (system reports pre-built + custom reports super_admin/compliance_officer + audit Sprint 7), un **service `ReportExecutionService.execute(report_id, parameters?)`** running reports via Sprint 13 ClickHouse aggregations + Postgres pour transactional data + Apply anonymization Sprint 25 if cross-tenant + materialized views reuse + circuit breaker timeout 60s + caching results 1h TTL, un **service `ReportSchedulingService`** cron jobs (daily 02h00 MA + weekly Monday 03h00 + monthly 1st 04h00 + quarterly 1st 05h00) + recipients email Sprint 9 delivery + Sprint 7 audit, un **catalog system reports** pre-built per-tenant (compliance ACAPS per-tenant alignment + sinistres summary + dispatch performance + reviewer performance + garage performance + payouts breakdown + customer satisfaction + financial summary) cross-tenant (industry benchmarks Sprint 25 reuse + multi-tenant aggregated + ACAPS regulator), un **custom reports builder UI** Sprint 26 admin-portal (visual builder OR SQL-like query language safe sandboxed + parameters definition + preview + save + schedule), un **exports framework** XLSX skill leverage formatted + PDF generation via Puppeteer + CSV raw data + S3 storage Sprint 10 + access logged, un **access control granular** Sprint 7.5a permissions (`reports.system.read`, `reports.custom.create`, `reports.custom.execute`, `reports.cross_tenant.create` super_admin only via Sprint 25, `reports.schedule.manage`, `reports.export.download`), des **scheduled reports cron** daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly + recipients lists configurables + email Sprint 9 delivery + retry on failure + Sprint 7 audit, des **performance optimizations** Sprint 13 materialized views + caching + circuit breaker + async execution long reports queue Sprint 14 patterns, un **admin portal reports UI** Sprint 26 `/admin/reports/*` (list system reports + custom reports + scheduled + history + run manual + download + builder visual), des **endpoints REST reports** controller `reports.controller.ts` 12 endpoints + permissions + 2FA pour cross-tenant, et **30+ tests E2E** couvrant system reports + custom reports builder + scheduling cron + exports formats + access control + performance limits.

Le sprint **cloture Phase 6 Apps Sinistre COMPLETE** declaration milestone formelle : 5 sprints (24+25+26+27+28) livres = ecosystem apps complete + multi-actor experience + admin operations + tenants lifecycle + reports & compliance = platform production-grade ready Demo Day investisseurs.

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 28

Decision-029 ratifiee, **3 entites domain reports** + RLS adapted, package `@insurtech/reports-framework` v1.0.0, **services** :

**ReportDefinitionService** :
- `createReport(name, description, category, query_definition, parameters)` super_admin/compliance_officer + audit + emit
- `updateReport(report_id, updates)` + audit
- `deleteReport(report_id)` super_admin + soft-delete + audit
- `listReports(tenant_id?, category?)` browse
- `getReportPreview(report_id, parameters_sample)` dry-run preview

**ReportExecutionService.execute(report_id, parameters?)** :
- Running reports via Sprint 13 ClickHouse aggregations + Postgres transactional
- Apply anonymization Sprint 25 if cross-tenant scope
- Materialized views Sprint 13 reuse
- Circuit breaker timeout 60s
- Caching results 1h TTL per (report_id + parameters_hash)
- Async queue Sprint 14 for long reports > 30s
- Audit Sprint 7

**ReportSchedulingService** :
- `scheduleReport(report_id, cron_expression, recipients)` activate scheduled
- `runScheduledReports()` cron job runner
- Cron jobs predefined :
  - Daily 02h00 MA `0 2 * * *`
  - Weekly Monday 03h00 `0 3 * * 1`
  - Monthly 1st day 04h00 `0 4 1 * *`
  - Quarterly 1st day quarter 05h00 `0 5 1 */3 *`
- Recipients email Sprint 9 delivery + retry 3x on failure + Sprint 7 audit

**Catalog system reports pre-built** (8 reports core) :
1. `compliance_acaps_per_tenant` ACAPS-aligned per-tenant (volume sinistres + resolution time + disputes + payouts) for insurer regulatory submissions
2. `sinistres_summary` per-tenant (counts by status + amount totals + trends)
3. `dispatch_performance` per-tenant (match rate + accept rate + offer-to-arrival time)
4. `reviewer_performance` per-tenant (avg review time + approve rate + IA override rate + dispute rate)
5. `garage_performance` per-tenant (ratings + completion rate + earnings)
6. `payouts_breakdown` per-tenant (mWallet + bank + cash + total amounts)
7. `customer_satisfaction` per-tenant (ratings + NPS + churn indicators)
8. `financial_summary` per-tenant (revenue + costs + margins foundation)

Cross-tenant via Sprint 25 framework :
- Industry benchmarks (reuse Sprint 25 IndustryBenchmarksService)
- Multi-tenant aggregated (super_admin only)
- ACAPS regulator submissions (compliance_officer)

**Custom reports builder UI** Sprint 26 admin-portal :
- Visual builder (drag entities + filters + aggregations + group by + having) OR
- SQL-like sandboxed query language safe (whitelist tables + columns + functions; no DELETE/UPDATE/INSERT)
- Parameters definition (typed inputs)
- Preview dry-run + sample data
- Save + schedule optional

**Exports framework** :
- XLSX skill leverage formatted (multiple sheets + branding optional Sprint 27)
- PDF generation Puppeteer (header + footer + branding optional + charts)
- CSV raw data
- S3 storage Sprint 10 with retention 90 days + access logged Sprint 7

**Access control granular** Sprint 7.5a permissions :
- `reports.system.read` (compliance_officer + super_admin + data_analyst + insurer_admin own tenant)
- `reports.custom.create` (super_admin + compliance_officer)
- `reports.custom.execute` (creators + super_admin)
- `reports.cross_tenant.create` (super_admin only via Sprint 25 framework + 2FA mandatory)
- `reports.schedule.manage` (super_admin + compliance_officer)
- `reports.export.download` (per-report ACL)

**Admin portal reports UI** Sprint 26 `/admin/reports/*` :
- `/admin/reports` list system + custom + scheduled + filters + sort
- `/admin/reports/[id]` detail + run manual + history runs + download exports
- `/admin/reports/builder` visual builder OR SQL editor (compliance_officer/super_admin)
- `/admin/reports/scheduled` scheduled reports management + cron expressions

**Endpoints REST reports** controller `reports.controller.ts` 12 endpoints :
- `POST /api/v1/admin/reports` create report
- `GET /api/v1/admin/reports` list + filters
- `GET /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}` detail
- `PUT /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}` update
- `DELETE /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}` soft-delete
- `POST /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/execute` run manual
- `GET /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/runs` history
- `GET /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/runs/{run_id}/download` download export
- `POST /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/schedule` schedule
- `DELETE /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/schedule` unschedule
- `POST /api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/preview` dry-run
- `GET /api/v1/admin/reports/catalog` system reports list

Permissions granulaires + 2FA Sprint 26 mandatory cross-tenant + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15, 30+ tests E2E,

**Rapport closure Sprint 28** + **CLOTURE PHASE 6 APPS SINISTRE COMPLETE** declaration milestone formelle :
- Sprint 24 Multi-App Foundation + Garage Apps
- Sprint 25 Cross-Tenant Framework + ACAPS-grade
- Sprint 26 Admin Foundation + 2FA
- Sprint 27 Tenants Management + KYC
- Sprint 28 Admin Reports + Compliance
= **Phase 6 Apps Sinistre complete production-grade**, pre-requis Phase 7 Garage Vertical (Sprints 29-33) valides, recommandation GO Phase 7.

### Exclus du Sprint 28 (defere)

Reports advanced analytics ML (predictions + anomaly detection) defers Sprint 30+ AI advanced, real-time streaming dashboards defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 28 batch refresh max 5min latency), business intelligence integration external (Tableau + PowerBI) defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 28 native only), reports white-labeling per-tenant custom branding advanced defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 28 basic branding Sprint 27), automated insights AI-powered defers Sprint 30+, data lake export bulk defers Phase 7+ (Sprint 28 reports level only), benchmarking vs external industry data sources defers Phase 7+, et reports versioning + drafts collaborative defers Phase 7+.

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 28 est **cloture Phase 6 + compliance per-tenant**. Trois dimensions strategiques.

Premierement, **compliance per-tenant ACAPS-aligned** insurers need their own reports for regulatory submissions. Sprint 25 cross-tenant covers Skalean platform-level ACAPS. Sprint 28 covers per-insurer regulator reports = enterprise commercial need.

Deuxiemement, **custom reports builder** valuable enterprise differentiation. Insurers want flexible reporting beyond pre-built. Custom builder = enterprise sales argument vs competitors fixed reports.

Troisiemement, **CLOTURE PHASE 6 COMPLETE** milestone declaration : ecosystem apps complete production-grade Demo Day. Phase 7+ becomes garage vertical + integration stubs + deployment, focused on hardening + Marrakech pilot.

### Decision-029 -- Admin Reports Framework + Custom Builder + Scheduling + Exports

**Justification** :
- (a) Compliance per-tenant ACAPS regulatory needs
- (b) Custom builder enterprise differentiation
- (c) Scheduled reports automation
- (d) Phase 6 closure milestone declaration

**Architecture** :
- 3 entites + RLS adapted
- 5 services + 8 system reports catalog
- Custom builder visual + SQL sandbox
- Exports XLSX/PDF/CSV + S3 retention 90 days
- Permissions granulaires + 2FA cross-tenant

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-028 + **decision-029** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-025 multi-app-architecture** : strict (admin-portal Sprint 26 UI).

**Decision-026 cross-tenant-framework** : strict (cross-tenant reports via Sprint 25).

**Decision-027 admin-foundation-2fa** : strict (2FA mandatory cross-tenant reports).

**Decision-028 tenants-lifecycle** : strict (per-tenant scope).

**Decision-029 admin-reports-framework** (NOUVEAU - ratification 13 juin 2026) : adoption **reports framework + custom builder + scheduling + exports + access control granular + Phase 6 closure**. Justification : (a) Per-tenant compliance, (b) Custom builder enterprise, (c) Scheduling automation, (d) Phase 6 closure milestone. Architecture : 3 entites + 5 services + 8 catalog + custom builder + exports.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX

**Piege 1 : Custom builder SQL injection.** SQL-like query allows malicious queries. Solution : whitelist tables + columns + functions strict + no DELETE/UPDATE/INSERT + sandbox execution + audit.

**Piege 2 : Reports performance large datasets.** Aggregations millions records slow. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse materialized views + caching + async queue Sprint 14.

**Piege 3 : Cross-tenant reports leak.** Reports scope wrong = data leak. Solution : @CrossTenant Sprint 25 decorator + anonymization + audit.

**Piege 4 : Exports XLSX/PDF size limits.** Large datasets > email attachments. Solution : S3 storage + signed URLs + access logged.

**Piege 5 : Scheduled reports cron failures.** Cron fails silent. Solution : Sprint 9 alerts + retry 3x + manual trigger fallback.

**Piege 6 : Reports caching stale data.** Cache TTL too long. Solution : 1h TTL + invalidation on data updates + opt-in real-time refresh.

**Piege 7 : Custom builder UX complexity.** Visual builder vs SQL learning curve. Solution : both modes + tutorials + templates pre-built.

**Piege 8 : Reports retention vs growth.** S3 retention 90 days = volume large. Solution : lifecycle policies + archival glacier > 1 year.

**Piege 9 : Permissions matrix complex.** 6 permissions x 4 roles = matrix complex. Solution : Sprint 7.5a documented + tests negative.

**Piege 10 : Phase 6 closure declaration timing.** Closure premature if Sprint 28 incomplete. Solution : V-28 >= 95% obligatoire avant declaration.

---

## 31. B-29-sprint-29-garage-vertical-foundation.md

```md
# B-29 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 29 : GARAGE VERTICAL FOUNDATION + DIRECT CUSTOMER BOOKING

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 7 Sprint 1)
**Phase** : 7 -- Garage Vertical (Sprints 29-33)
**Sprint** : 29 / 37 -- Phase 7 Sprint 1 (PREMIER PHASE 7)
**Position** : Apres Phase 6 Apps Sinistre complete (Sprint 28), avant Sprint 30 Garage Inventory + Mechanic Tools
**Numerotation taches** : 8.1.1 a 8.1.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~80 heures developpement

**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer la **Garage Vertical Foundation + Direct Customer Booking** -- pivot strategique extending garage business model au-dela sinistres insurance vers direct customers operations. Le sprint construit la foundation complete Phase 7 pour garages servant **directly customers** (non-insurance work : entretien periodique + reparation hors sinistre + customisation + diagnostic + accessoires), avec **public garage profiles SEO-optimized** (slug per-garage + Schema.org LocalBusiness structured data + meta tags + sitemap dynamic), **direct booking workflow** customers reserve garages directly + paiement own pocket (mWallet customer -> garage + bank transfer + cashplus + cash_on_delivery option), **garage CRM** track direct customers per-garage avec segmentation (new/returning/VIP) + statistics aggregees + outreach foundation pour Sprint 31 marketing campaigns, **services catalog** managed by garage (CRUD services + 4 categories enum entretien/reparation/diagnostic/customisation + pricing transparency + photos S3 + duration estimated + active toggle), **direct reviews** post-booking verified_purchase only avec moderation + window submission 1-30 days post-completion + aggregate rating average update, **payment direct foundation** Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService reuse pour direct customer -> garage payment flow (pre-authorization au booking_confirmed + capture au booking_completed + refund au cancellation). Le sprint introduit **decision-030 ratification** garage vertical scope expansion + direct customer business model + 2-sided platform commercial differentiation + Skalean revenue commission 5% direct bookings + TAM expansion 4x.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'**entites domain Garage Vertical** :
- **`GarageService`** (id + garage_id + name + description + category enum entretien/reparation/diagnostic/customisation + price_base_mad NUMERIC(10,2) + price_variable BOOLEAN + duration_estimated_minutes INTEGER + active BOOLEAN + photos_urls TEXT[] + created_at + audit_trail JSONB)
- **`DirectBooking`** (id + garage_id + customer_id (DirectCustomer) + service_id + scheduled_at TIMESTAMP + status enum requested/confirmed/in_progress/completed/cancelled + amount_quoted_mad NUMERIC(10,2) + amount_final_mad NUMERIC(10,2) nullable + payment_method enum mwallet/bank_transfer/cashplus/cash_on_delivery + payment_status enum pending/pre_authorized/captured/refunded/failed + payment_reference VARCHAR nullable + customer_notes TEXT + garage_notes TEXT nullable + opened_at + completed_at nullable + cancelled_at nullable + cancellation_reason TEXT nullable + audit_trail JSONB)
- **`DirectCustomer`** (id + garage_id + name + phone VARCHAR + email VARCHAR nullable + city VARCHAR + first_booking_at + total_bookings INTEGER + total_spent_mad NUMERIC(10,2) + last_booking_at + segment computed enum new/returning/vip + opt_in_marketing BOOLEAN + audit_trail JSONB)
- **`GaragePublicProfile`** (garage_id + slug_seo VARCHAR UNIQUE global + about_text TEXT + photos_gallery TEXT[] + opening_hours JSONB schema {monday: {open: "09:00", close: "18:00"} ...} + services_displayed UUID[] + verified_badge BOOLEAN + rating_average NUMERIC(3,2) + reviews_count INTEGER + active BOOLEAN + audit_trail JSONB)
- **`DirectReview`** (id + booking_id FK + customer_id + garage_id + rating INTEGER CHECK 1-5 + comment TEXT nullable + photos TEXT[] + verified_purchase BOOLEAN true (enforced) + active BOOLEAN + moderation_reason TEXT nullable + submitted_at + audit_trail JSONB)

Un **package `@insurtech/garage-vertical`** v1.0.0 NestJS module, **5 services principaux** :

**GarageServiceService** :
- `createService(garage_id, service_data, photos)` validation + upload photos S3 Sprint 10 + active=true default + audit + emit
- `updateService(service_id, updates)` partial update + audit
- `deleteService(service_id)` soft-delete via active=false + audit
- `listServices(garage_id, filters)` browse + pagination + filters category + active + price_range
- `getServicesByCategory(garage_id, category)` filtered list per category
- `togglePriceVariable(service_id)` toggle price_variable (some services have variable pricing depending on diagnostic)

**DirectBookingService.bookDirectGarage(garage_id, customer_data, service_id, preferred_date, customer_notes, payment_method)** orchestration critique :
1. Validate availability slot (no conflict with other booking same garage same time + opening_hours respected)
2. Lookup OR create DirectCustomer (UNIQUE constraint garage_id+phone : if exists update last_seen, if not create + emit `direct_customer.created`)
3. Calculate amount_quoted_mad from GarageService.price_base_mad
4. Reserve slot status='confirmed'
5. Apply Sprint 31 promotion code if provided (PromotionService.validateAndApply called Sprint 31 stub for Sprint 29)
6. Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.preAuthorize amount on payment_method (sauf cash_on_delivery)
7. Status='confirmed' + audit Sprint 7 + emit `direct_booking.confirmed` Avro Sprint 14
8. Sprint 9 notify garage_owner (new booking + customer + service + date) + customer (confirmation + booking details + cancellation policy)
9. Update DirectCustomer statistics (total_bookings if first, last_booking_at)

**DirectBookingService.completeBooking(booking_id, amount_final_mad, garage_notes)** :
1. Verify status='in_progress' + garage_owner authorized
2. Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.capture pre_authorized amount (capture amount_final + adjust if different from amount_quoted)
3. UPDATE status='completed' + completed_at + amount_final_mad + garage_notes
4. Update DirectCustomer.total_spent_mad += amount_final + last_booking_at
5. Sprint 9 notify customer (work completed + amount + review request link)
6. Schedule review request reminder 1 day later if no review submitted
7. Audit + emit `direct_booking.completed` Avro

**DirectBookingService.cancelBooking(booking_id, cancelled_by, reason)** :
1. Verify status in ['requested', 'confirmed', 'in_progress'] not 'completed' or 'cancelled'
2. Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService.refund if pre_authorized + cancellation penalty 10% if cancelled by customer < 24h before scheduled_at (DIRECT_BOOKING_CANCELLATION_PENALTY_PERCENT)
3. UPDATE status='cancelled' + cancelled_at + cancellation_reason
4. Sprint 9 notify other party (garage_owner if cancelled by customer or vice-versa)
5. Audit + emit `direct_booking.cancelled`

**DirectCustomerService** :
- `lookupOrCreate(garage_id, phone, customer_data)` find OR create new DirectCustomer (UNIQUE garage_id+phone) + first_booking_at = now if creating
- `updateStatistics(customer_id, total_spent_delta, increment_bookings_count)` atomic transactional
- `getSegment(customer_id)` compute segment from stats (new: total_bookings = 1, returning: 2-5, vip: > 5 OR total_spent > 10000 MAD)
- `listCustomers(garage_id, filters)` segmentation filters + opt_in_marketing
- `getStatistics(garage_id)` aggregate stats (total customers + new this month + retention rate)

**GaragePublicProfileService** :
- `getProfileBySlug(slug)` lookup public (no auth) + active garage + Redis cache 1h `PUBLIC_PROFILE_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=3600` + SEO meta tags + Schema.org LocalBusiness structured data injection
- `updateProfile(garage_id, profile_data)` about_text + photos_gallery S3 + opening_hours + services_displayed selection + invalidate cache
- `verifyProfile(garage_id, super_admin_id)` set verified_badge=true (super_admin trusted garages program)
- `getProfileSitemap()` generate sitemap.xml dynamic listing all active profiles for SEO indexing
- `generateRobotsTxt()` allow public profile pages + disallow other routes

**DirectReviewService** :
- `submitReview(booking_id, customer_id, rating, comment, photos)` orchestration :
  1. Verify booking.status='completed' (verified_purchase enforced)
  2. Verify customer = booking.customer_id (only customer can review own booking)
  3. Verify window submission 1-30 days post-completion (DIRECT_REVIEW_MIN_DAYS_AFTER_BOOKING=1 + DIRECT_REVIEW_MAX_DAYS_AFTER_BOOKING=30)
  4. Verify no existing active review for this booking (1 review per booking)
  5. Create DirectReview verified_purchase=true active=true
  6. Aggregate update GaragePublicProfile.rating_average atomic + reviews_count + invalidate cache
  7. Audit + emit `direct_review.submitted`
  8. Sprint 9 notify garage_owner (new review + rating + comment)
- `moderateReview(review_id, moderator_id, decision, reason)` super_admin OR garage owner request moderation (if abusive/fake) + UPDATE active=false + moderation_reason + audit
- `getReviewsByGarage(garage_id, filters)` filters rating + active + period + sort

**Integration Sprint 11 payment direct** : PayoutInstantService.preAuthorize + capture + refund pour direct customer -> garage flow (mWallet + bank + cashplus) + cash_on_delivery option no Sprint 11 needed (garage records payment manually post-completion),

**Integration Sprint 24 garage-portal extensions** screens dedicated tabs nouveaux :
- `/direct-bookings/*` (dashboard + list + detail + create manual booking + complete + cancel + filters status/date/customer)
- `/services-catalog/*` (CRUD services + photos upload + categories + pricing + active toggle)
- `/direct-customers/*` (CRM list + segmentation + detail + outreach foundation)
- `/public-profile/*` (editor + preview live + photos gallery + opening hours + services displayed selection)
- `/reviews-direct/*` (list + moderation + reply foundation Phase 8+)
- KPIs dashboard direct vs insurance revenue split (Sprint 13)

**Integration Sprint 18 customer-portal extensions** screens nouveaux public-facing :
- `/garages` (search + map view + filters category/price/rating/location + grid view toggle + pagination)
- `/garages/[slug]` (public profile + photos + opening hours + services + reviews aggregated + favorites button)
- `/garages/[slug]/book` (booking workflow multi-step : select service + select date + select payment method + apply promotion code optional + confirm)
- `/garages/[slug]/reviews` (reviews display + filter rating + sort + pagination)
- `/bookings` (my bookings list customer-side)
- `/bookings/[id]` (booking detail + status + cancel option + leave review post-completion)
- `/favorites/garages` (saved garages favorites)
- Notifications Sprint 9 (booking confirmed + reminder 24h before + in_progress + completed + review_request)

**Integration Sprint 13 analytics vertical split** materialized views ClickHouse (direct_vs_insurance_revenue_mv + customer_acquisition_mv + average_ticket_mv + retention_mv + reviews_trend_mv + service_category_popularity_mv) + refresh daily 04h00 MA + dashboards garage analytics tab,

Des **endpoints REST garage vertical** controller `garage-vertical.controller.ts` 12 endpoints + permissions Sprint 7.5a (garage_owner full + customer access public + super_admin verifyProfile) + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15 + public endpoint `/api/v1/public/garages/{slug}` no-auth rate limited (cache 1h),

Et **30+ tests E2E** scenarios couvrant services catalog CRUD + direct booking workflow (Sprint 11 integration) + customer CRM + public profile SEO + Schema.org structured data + reviews verified_purchase + extensions Sprint 18+24 UI + analytics vertical split.

Le sprint constitue **commercial differentiation Skalean** vs platforms generiques insurtech competitors. Skalean = insurance vertical + garage vertical = 2-sided platform commercial expansion garage market beyond insurance claims. Sans Sprint 29, Skalean = insurtech-only = limited TAM MAD ~500M Morocco. Avec Sprint 29, garages on Skalean platform manage 70% insurance work + 30% direct work = increased garage retention + platform value-add + Skalean revenue commission 5% direct bookings + TAM expansion 4x to MAD ~2B combined insurance + garage vertical Morocco.

---

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 29 est **pivot strategique commercial expansion 2-sided platform** + **foundation Phase 7 Garage Vertical complete** + **PREMIER sprint Phase 7**. Quatre dimensions strategiques majeures.

Premierement, **garage business beyond insurance**. Garages on Skalean platform earn currently 70% revenue from insurance dispatch work (Sprint 21-23 dispatch + sinistre work_submission) but 30% from direct customers (entretien + reparation hors sinistre + customisation + diagnostic). Without Sprint 29, garages must use separate tools (Google My Business + Facebook page + WhatsApp customer messaging + word-of-mouth + paper booking) for direct customers operations = inefficient + fragmentation + lost opportunities. With Sprint 29, garages can manage both insurance AND direct customers via Skalean unified = retention boost + platform stickiness max + commercial differentiation enterprise.

Deuxiemement, **TAM expansion 2-sided platform 4x**. Skalean insurtech-only TAM = MAD ~500M market Morocco (insurance claims processing + insurer-broker workflows). Skalean insurtech + garage vertical TAM = MAD ~2B market combined (insurance + garage direct customers + garage operations + garage marketing). Sprint 29 unlocks larger addressable market with same platform investment foundation = improved unit economics + faster path to scale.

Troisiemement, **commercial differentiation enterprise vs platforms generiques**. Insurtech competitors Morocco focus only on claims processing automation. Skalean offers garages a complete business platform (insurance dispatch + direct customers + operations Sprint 30 + accounting Sprint 31 + marketing Sprint 31 + AI Sprint 33) = differentiated value proposition + premium pricing justified + harder to switch away (data lock-in + workflow lock-in + ecosystem lock-in). Sprint 29 establishes 2-sided platform positioning critical for fundraising + ACAPS regulatory positioning.

Quatriemement, **revenue diversification Skalean**. Skalean revenue Sprint 21-28 = insurer subscription fees only. Sprint 29 introduces commission model Skalean revenue 5% on direct booking transactions (DIRECT_BOOKING_COMMISSION_PERCENT=5) = transaction-based revenue + scaling with garage adoption + foundation Phase 8+ commercial models (premium garage tiers + featured listings + advertising garage promotions).

---

### Decision-030 -- Garage Vertical Direct Customer Business Model

**Justification** :
- (a) Garage business beyond insurance 30% revenue capture
- (b) TAM expansion 2-sided platform 4x (MAD 500M -> 2B Morocco)
- (c) Commercial differentiation enterprise vs competitors insurtech-only
- (d) Platform stickiness garages retention complete OS foundation
- (e) Skalean revenue commission 5% direct bookings + transaction-based model
- (f) Foundation Phase 7 Sprints 30-33 (operations + accounting + marketing + AI)

**Architecture** :
- 5 entites domain + RLS strict garage_id
- 5 services + Sprint 11 payment reuse direct flow
- Sprint 24 garage-portal extensions 5 tabs
- Sprint 18 customer-portal extensions public-facing discovery + booking
- Sprint 13 analytics vertical split materialized views
- Foundation Phase 7+ (operations Sprint 30 + accounting Sprint 31 + marketing Sprint 31 + loyalty Sprint 32 + AI Sprint 33)

---

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-029 + **decision-030** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-011 paiement-multi-canal** : strict (Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService reuse direct payments customer -> garage flow + 4 payment methods mwallet/bank/cashplus/cash_on_delivery).

**Decision-022 dispatch-matching** : non-applicable directement (direct bookings bypass insurance dispatch workflow Sprint 21 + bookings vont directement customer -> garage sans intermediation Skalean dispatch).

**Decision-025 multi-app-architecture** : strict (Sprint 24 garage-portal extensions 5 nouveaux tabs + Sprint 18 customer-portal extensions public discovery routes).

**Decision-029 admin-reports-framework** : Sprint 28 framework reuse pour Sprint 29 analytics vertical (super_admin can run cross-tenant reports incluant direct bookings).

**Decision-030 garage-vertical-direct-customer-business-model** (NOUVEAU - ratification 14 juin 2026) : adoption **garage vertical direct customer business model + services catalog + direct booking workflow Sprint 11 integration + CRM + public profile SEO + reviews verified_purchase + Sprint 18+24 extensions + analytics vertical split**. Justification : (a) Garage business beyond insurance, (b) TAM expansion 4x, (c) Commercial differentiation, (d) Stickiness, (e) Revenue commission 5%, (f) Foundation Phase 7+. Architecture : 5 entites + 5 services + Sprint 11 reuse + Sprint 18+24 extensions + Sprint 13 analytics.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX

**Piege 1 : Direct customers != insurance customers data model confusion.** Confusion entre DirectCustomer (Sprint 29) et Customer (Sprint 16 insurance customer entity). Solution : entites separees DirectCustomer Sprint 29 vs Customer Sprint 16 + clear documentation distinction + no cross-references + tests negative ensuring isolation.

**Piege 2 : Booking availability slot race conditions.** Two customers attempt to book same garage same time slot simultaneously = double-booking + customer dissatisfaction. Solution : optimistic locking on availability check + transactional booking creation (BEGIN + SELECT WHERE NOT EXISTS conflicting booking + INSERT + COMMIT + retry on conflict 3x) + tests stress concurrent 100 booking attempts same slot only 1 succeeds.

**Piege 3 : Sprint 11 payment reuse adaptation complexity.** Sprint 11 PayoutInstantService designed primarily for insurance payouts (insurer -> garage post-sinistre) not direct customer -> garage payment. Solution : abstraction interface PaymentDirectionAdapter + adapter pattern + verify Sprint 11 service works bidirectional + tests integration end-to-end + manual fallback admin if Sprint 11 errors.

**Piege 4 : SEO public profiles indexing risk.** Public profiles indexed by Google with bad content (fake garages + spam + outdated info) = SEO penalty + brand damage Skalean. Solution : robots.txt strict (allow only verified profiles + active=true) + verified_badge program super_admin manual review + content moderation foundation + monitor Google Search Console + suppress badly performing pages.

**Piege 5 : Reviews authenticity verified_purchase enforcement.** Fake reviews (garages reviewing themselves + competitors negative reviews) damage trust platform. Solution : verified_purchase=true strict enforce (require booking.status='completed' + customer_id = booking.customer_id + window 1-30 days post-completion) + flag suspicious patterns (multiple reviews same IP + same wording + abnormal rating distribution) + moderation request flow + reviews count requires multiple bookings.

**Piege 6 : Cross-data leak direct customers + insurance customers.** RLS misconfiguration = direct customer accidentally sees other tenant's data + customer's insurance data exposed in direct booking context. Solution : RLS strict entity_type Sprint 25 framework + tests negative (different tenant cannot access) + audit cross-vertical access patterns + permissions Sprint 7.5a granular.

**Piege 7 : Garage portal extensions UX cluttered.** Adding 5 new tabs Sprint 29 + Sprint 30 tabs + Sprint 31 tabs = UX overwhelm garage owner. Solution : tab separation visual clear "Direct" section vs "Insurance" section + collapsible sidebar groups + onboarding tour first-time + permissions hide unused tabs.

**Piege 8 : Customer-portal extensions search performance.** Public garages search large dataset (1000+ garages) slow query. Solution : Sprint 13 ClickHouse search index + cache 5min popular queries + indexed columns garage_id + slug + city + Schema.org structured data lazy-loaded + pagination cursor stable + search analytics monitor.

**Piege 9 : Cash_on_delivery accounting complications.** Garage records cash payments manually post-completion = errors + tax compliance issues + reconciliation difficult. Solution : audit cash payments separately + reconciliation report monthly per garage + Sprint 31 accounting integration mandatory + reminder garage to record + Sprint 33 AI anomaly detection foundation.

**Piege 10 : Cancellation refund Sprint 11 complications.** Refund logic complex (full refund vs penalty + pre-authorized vs captured + cash_on_delivery no refund needed). Solution : DirectBookingService.cancelBooking handle 4 payment_method cases + cancellation penalty 10% if < 24h (DIRECT_BOOKING_CANCELLATION_PENALTY_PERCENT) + tests integration Sprint 11 refund all 4 methods + manual fallback admin Sprint 26 if Sprint 11 errors.

---
