## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **Garage AI Optimization** -- couche AI/ML strategique comprehensive cloturant Phase 7 Garage Vertical avec **6 features AI** exploitant le data foundation construit par les Sprints 29-32 : **(1) AI Predictive Maintenance** (predire next service date per customer vehicle basee historical patterns Sprint 30 work_orders.completed + Sprint 29 direct_bookings frequency + service_categories typique entretien periodique 6 mois/10000 km Morocco), **(2) AI Churn Prediction** (compute churn probability score 0-1 per customer basee LoyaltyPoints engagement Sprint 32 last_activity_at + Reviews ratings degradation + booking frequency decrease + tier downgrade signals + risk_level low/medium/high), **(3) AI Recommendation Engine Personalized** (recommend rewards Sprint 32 catalog + services Sprint 29 catalog basee customer history patterns + similar customers collaborative filtering basique + content-based filtering tags overlap), **(4) AI Pricing Optimization** (suggest dynamic pricing per GarageService basee demand patterns Sprint 29 bookings + day-of-week + season + competition awareness foundation), **(5) AI Content Marketing Suggestions** (suggest blog post topics Sprint 32 + SEO keywords + social posts basee customers FAQs Sprint 9 + search trends + content gap analysis), et **(6) AI Fraud Detection Patterns** (flag suspicious referrals Sprint 32 + LoyaltyPoints transactions anomalies + booking patterns abuse + pricing manipulation attempts).

Le sprint utilise **approche progressive simple** : algorithmes **rule-based + statistical** TypeScript-native pour foundation Sprint 33 (no external AI API dependencies + maintainable + deterministic + auditable + low cost) + **foundation Phase 8+** pour migration LLMs avances (OpenAI GPT-5 + Vertex AI Gemini + Anthropic Claude + custom ML models trained on Skalean data). Cette approche garantit deliverable Sprint 33 dans 82h + value proposition immediate clients + path clair vers advanced AI Phase 8+ sans rework foundation. Le sprint introduit **decision-034 ratification** garage AI optimization comprehensive + Phase 7 cloture + commercial differentiation premium tier AI features + foundation Phase 8+ advanced AI/ML.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'**entites domain Garage AI** :
- **`AIModel`** (id + garage_id + model_type enum predictive_maintenance/churn_prediction/recommendation/pricing/content/fraud + model_version VARCHAR + training_data_period_start + training_data_period_end + accuracy_metrics JSONB {precision + recall + f1_score + sample_size} + deployed_at + last_trained_at + algorithm_type VARCHAR + parameters JSONB + audit_trail) per garage per type
- **`AIRecommendation`** (id + customer_id FK Sprint 29 DirectCustomer + garage_id + recommendation_type enum reward/service/promotion + recommended_entity_id UUID + payload JSONB explanation + confidence_score NUMERIC 0-1 + status enum suggested/displayed/accepted/rejected/expired + generated_at + displayed_at nullable + acted_at nullable + expires_at + audit_trail)
- **`AIChurnScore`** (id + customer_id + garage_id + score NUMERIC 0-1 + risk_level enum low/medium/high + factors JSONB explainability {last_activity_days + booking_frequency_change + tier_changes + review_ratings_avg + engagement_signals} + recommended_intervention_type enum email_campaign/sms_offer/personal_call/loyalty_boost + computed_at + audit_trail) UNIQUE (customer_id, garage_id, computed_at::date) cron daily
- **`AIPricingRecommendation`** (id + garage_id + service_id FK Sprint 29 GarageService + recommended_price_mad NUMERIC + base_price_mad NUMERIC + price_change_percent NUMERIC + confidence NUMERIC + factors JSONB {demand_index + day_of_week_pattern + season_factor + competitive_position + capacity_utilization} + valid_until + status enum suggested/accepted/rejected/expired + acted_at + audit_trail) UNIQUE (garage_id, service_id, generated_at::date)
- **`AIPredictedMaintenance`** (id + customer_id + vehicle_identifier VARCHAR + garage_id + predicted_service_category enum + predicted_date + confidence + based_on_pattern JSONB {historical_bookings_count + last_service_date + average_interval_days + service_history_consistency} + notification_sent BOOLEAN + notification_sent_at + acted_at + audit_trail) UNIQUE (customer_id, vehicle_identifier, predicted_service_category)
- **`AIFraudFlag`** (id + entity_type enum referral/loyalty_transaction/direct_booking/promotion_application + entity_id UUID + garage_id + risk_score NUMERIC 0-1 + risk_level enum low/medium/high/critical + reasons JSONB {patterns_detected + anomalies + similar_cases_count} + status enum flagged/reviewed_legitimate/reviewed_fraud + reviewer_id nullable + reviewer_notes nullable + reviewed_at nullable + audit_trail) indexed risk_score DESC + status partial
- **`AIContentSuggestion`** (id + garage_id + suggestion_type enum blog_topic/seo_keyword/social_post/faq_response + content JSONB {title + body_outline + keywords + target_audience + estimated_seo_value} + based_on_data JSONB {trending_searches + customer_faqs + content_gaps + competitor_analysis} + priority_score NUMERIC + status enum suggested/accepted_drafted/rejected/expired + acted_at + audit_trail)

Un **package `@insurtech/garage-ai-optimization`** v1.0.0 NestJS module, **6 services principaux** :

**AIPredictiveMaintenanceService** :
- `predictNextMaintenance(customer_id, garage_id, vehicle_identifier)` algorithme :
  1. Query Sprint 30 work_orders + Sprint 29 direct_bookings WHERE customer_id + vehicle_identifier + status='completed'
  2. Group by service_category + compute average_interval_days per category
  3. Identify dominant maintenance pattern (e.g. "entretien" every 6 months consistent)
  4. Predict next_service_date = last_service_date + average_interval_days
  5. Confidence = function of historical_consistency (low variance = high confidence)
  6. Create AIPredictedMaintenance + audit
  7. Sprint 9 notify customer 7 days before predicted_date if active opt-in
- Cron `AI_PREDICTIVE_MAINTENANCE_CRON=0 6 * * 1` (weekly Monday 06h00 MA)
- `acceptPrediction(prediction_id)` customer accepts -> auto-create AIBookingSuggestion link Sprint 29 booking flow
- `dismissPrediction(prediction_id, reason)` customer dismisses

**AIChurnPredictionService** (CRITIQUE Sprint 32 integration) :
- Cron daily `AI_CHURN_PREDICTION_CRON=0 7 * * *` (daily 07h00 MA)
- Per garage active customers (DirectCustomer Sprint 29) iterate :
  1. Compute factors :
     - `last_activity_days` = days since LoyaltyPoints.last_activity_at OR last booking
     - `booking_frequency_change` = compare bookings last 90 days vs previous 90 days (decrease % signal)
     - `tier_change` = current_tier downgrade from peak tier
     - `review_ratings_avg` = average DirectReview.rating last 5 reviews
     - `engagement_signals` = LoyaltyPointsTransactions count last 60 days
  2. Weighted churn score formula (rule-based):
     ```
     score = (
       0.35 * normalize(last_activity_days, 0, 180) +
       0.25 * (1 - booking_frequency_recent / booking_frequency_baseline) +
       0.15 * (tier_downgrade ? 1 : 0) +
       0.15 * (1 - review_ratings_avg / 5) +
       0.10 * (1 - engagement_signals_recent / engagement_baseline)
     ).clamp(0, 1)
     ```
  3. Risk level = score < 0.4 low + score < 0.7 medium + score >= 0.7 high
  4. Recommended intervention = function of risk + customer segment (high risk vip = personal_call + medium = sms_offer + low = email_campaign)
  5. Create AIChurnScore + audit + emit `ai_churn_score.computed` Sprint 14
  6. If high risk : Sprint 9 notify garage_owner + suggest Sprint 31 CampaignService creation
- `getChurnScore(customer_id, garage_id)` latest score
- `getHighRiskCustomers(garage_id)` filtered list for intervention
- Foundation Phase 8+ advanced ML models replacing rule-based

**AIRecommendationEngineService** :
- Per customer-garage relationship compute personalized recommendations :
  - **Reward recommendations** : customer LoyaltyPoints.balance + tier + redemption history + similar customers' redemptions (collaborative filtering basique top-K customers same tier) -> recommended Rewards
  - **Service recommendations** : customer DirectBookings history service_categories + AIPredictedMaintenance + complementary services + similar customers' services -> recommended GarageServices
  - **Promotion recommendations** : Sprint 31 promotions eligible customer + best match + max discount
- Generate AIRecommendation entities per recommendation
- Cron daily `AI_RECOMMENDATIONS_CRON=0 8 * * *` (daily 08h00 MA refresh) per garage active customers top 100 (most engaged)
- `getActiveRecommendations(customer_id, garage_id, type?)` lookup active + status='suggested' OR 'displayed'
- `markDisplayed(recommendation_id)` track displayed (analytics)
- `acceptRecommendation(recommendation_id)` track acceptance + audit
- `rejectRecommendation(recommendation_id, reason)` track rejection + audit
- `getRecommendationAnalytics(garage_id, period)` aggregate (suggested + displayed + accepted + acceptance_rate)

**AIPricingOptimizationService** :
- Per garage GarageService Sprint 29 compute pricing recommendations :
  - Factors :
    - `demand_index` = bookings frequency last 30 days vs 90-day average
    - `day_of_week_pattern` = bookings per day-of-week (weekend premium identification)
    - `season_factor` = month-over-month variation (high season + low season)
    - `competitive_position` = price_base vs market average (placeholder Phase 8+ competitor scraping)
    - `capacity_utilization` = slots booked vs available slots
  - Rule-based formula:
    ```
    recommended_price = base_price * (1 + 0.15 * (demand_index - 1)) * day_of_week_multiplier * season_multiplier
    ```
  - Constrained max +/- 30% change from base_price (no shock changes)
  - Confidence based factors consistency
- Create AIPricingRecommendation valid_until = end of week
- Cron weekly `AI_PRICING_OPTIMIZATION_CRON=0 9 * * 1` (Monday 09h00 MA)
- `getActivePricingRecommendations(garage_id)` for garage_owner review
- `acceptPricingRecommendation(recommendation_id)` apply -> Sprint 29 GarageServiceService.updateService price_base_mad + audit
- `rejectPricingRecommendation(recommendation_id, reason)` audit

**AIContentMarketingService** :
- Per garage suggest content :
  - **Blog topics** : analyse Sprint 32 BlogPost.tags trending + customer FAQs Sprint 9 messaging patterns + content gaps (categories underrepresented)
  - **SEO keywords** : suggest based competitor analysis foundation + Morocco automotive search trends + long-tail variations
  - **Social posts** : suggest based blog posts + featured testimonials Sprint 32 + promotions Sprint 31
- Rule-based + template-based foundation Sprint 33 (advanced LLMs Phase 8+ OpenAI GPT-5 generation)
- Create AIContentSuggestion entities
- `getActiveContentSuggestions(garage_id, type?)` listing
- `acceptSuggestion(suggestion_id)` mark accepted + create draft BlogPost Sprint 32 if blog_topic
- `rejectSuggestion(suggestion_id)` audit

**AIFraudDetectionService** (CRITIQUE Sprint 32 integration) :
- Real-time fraud detection (event-driven) :
  - Event handler Sprint 32 `referral.completed` : verify patterns (referrer + referred similar profiles + same IP foundation + booking pattern abnormal) -> compute risk_score + create AIFraudFlag if risk_score > 0.5
  - Event handler Sprint 32 `loyalty_points.earned` : verify amount + booking value + customer history (huge points earn relative to booking) -> flag if anomalous
  - Event handler Sprint 29 `direct_booking.completed` : verify booking amount + customer + service (price manipulation patterns) -> flag if anomalous
  - Event handler Sprint 31 `promotion.applied` : verify customer eligibility + multiple applies same customer different accounts -> flag
- Rule-based patterns Sprint 33 + foundation Phase 8+ ML anomaly detection
- `getActiveFraudFlags(garage_id, risk_level?)` super_admin OR garage_owner review
- `reviewFlag(flag_id, decision, notes)` status='reviewed_legitimate' OR 'reviewed_fraud' + reviewer + audit + if fraud : escalate Sprint 28 admin reports
- Foundation Sprint 25 cross-tenant framework cross-garage fraud pattern detection (Phase 8+)

**Integration Sprint 13 analytics training data MV** : 6+ materialized views aggregating training data (customer_engagement_features_mv + booking_patterns_mv + loyalty_engagement_features_mv + review_patterns_mv + pricing_demand_features_mv + fraud_signals_mv) + refresh daily 04h00 + consumed AI services,

**Integration Sprint 24 garage-portal extensions** screens nouveaux **4 tabs AI dedicated** :
- `/ai/predictions/*` (predictive maintenance + churn at-risk customers list + recommendations performance dashboard)
- `/ai/recommendations/*` (active recommendations per customer + analytics acceptance rates + recommendation history)
- `/ai/pricing/*` (active pricing recommendations + accept/reject + analytics revenue impact)
- `/ai/fraud-detection/*` (flagged transactions list + review + decision + escalation foundation)

**Integration Sprint 18 customer-portal extensions** :
- Personalized recommendations widgets (rewards + services + promotions affichage homepage + page my-points)
- Predictive maintenance reminders notifications + booking suggestion
- AI Explainability transparente (factors visible to customer)

Des **endpoints REST garage AI** controller `garage-ai-optimization.controller.ts` 14 endpoints + permissions Sprint 7.5a (garage_owner + customer access own recommendations + super_admin fraud reviews) + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15,

Et **30+ tests E2E** scenarios couvrant predictive maintenance Sprint 30+29 + churn prediction Sprint 32 + recommendations Sprint 29+32 + pricing Sprint 29 + content Sprint 32 + fraud detection Sprint 32 + extensions Sprint 18+24 + Sprint 13 analytics MV.

Le sprint constitue **CLOTURE Phase 7 Garage Vertical complete** + **garage AI value proposition strategique** + **commercial differentiation premium tier enterprise**. Sans Sprint 33, Phase 7 manque differentiateur AI vs competitors basic platforms. Avec Sprint 33, Skalean = **AI-augmented garage operating system** = positioning premium pricing Phase 8+ tiered model + foundation advanced LLMs integration + competitive moat enterprise SaaS Morocco.

## FRONTIERE DU SPRINT

### Inclus dans Sprint 33

Decision-034 ratifiee, 7 entites domain AI + RLS strict garage_id + indexes performance + UNIQUE constraints + seeds initial 3 garages demos avec models configures + sample recommendations + churn scores + fraud flags + content suggestions,

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 33 est **CLOTURE Phase 7 Garage Vertical** + **garage AI value proposition strategique** + **foundation Phase 8+ advanced AI/ML**. Quatre dimensions strategiques majeures.

Premierement, **AI-augmented garage operating system positioning**. Phase 7 Sprints 29-32 ont construit garage operating system complete (vertical foundation + operations + accounting + marketing + loyalty + online presence). Sprint 33 ajoute couche AI exploitant data accumulee = positioning premium "AI-first garage platform Morocco" vs competitors basic CRUD platforms. Differentiation enterprise SaaS Phase 8+ tiered pricing model (Basic + Premium + Enterprise) ou Premium/Enterprise unlock AI features.

Deuxiemement, **customer value proposition immediate**. AI features Sprint 33 (predictive maintenance + personalized recommendations + dynamic pricing + content marketing + fraud protection) delivrent value immediate aux garages (revenue boost via personalized recommendations + churn prevention via early warning + pricing optimization + fraud loss prevention) ET aux customers (predictive maintenance reminders + relevant recommendations + transparent pricing). ROI tangible garage_owner justifie subscription Skalean.

Troisiemement, **foundation Phase 8+ advanced AI/ML clear path**. Approche progressive Sprint 33 (rule-based + statistical foundation) preserve investments + foundation pour migration Phase 8+ (OpenAI LLMs + custom ML models + real-time inference + feature store + A/B testing). Pas de rework foundation needed Phase 8+ = capital efficiency.

Quatriemement, **CLOTURE Phase 7 Demo Day 30 juin 2026 proof point**. Phase 7 complete (29-33) = 5 sprints livres + garage operating system + AI optimization = compelling proof point for ACAPS jury + investisseurs Programme Emergence. Demonstrates Skalean execution capability + technical depth + commercial differentiation enterprise SaaS Morocco.

- (b) AI-augmented garage operating system positioning vs competitors
- (c) Customer value immediate (revenue boost + churn prevention + fraud protection)

- Sprint 13 analytics 6 MV training features
- Sprint 18+24 extensions UI

## DECISIONS STRATEGIQUES REFERENCEES

Toutes decisions 001-033 + **decision-034** (NOUVELLE).

**Decision-013 analytics-clickhouse** : strict (Sprint 13 ClickHouse training data materialized views).

**Decision-030 garage-vertical-direct-customer** : strict (Sprint 29 DirectCustomer + DirectBooking + GarageService training data foundation).

**Decision-031 garage-internal-operations-foundation** : strict (Sprint 30 work_orders predictive maintenance source).

**Decision-032 garage-accounting-marketing** : strict (Sprint 31 promotions + campaigns recommendation engine integration).

**Decision-033 garage-online-loyalty-foundation** : strict (Sprint 32 LoyaltyPoints + Referrals churn + fraud detection sources).

**Decision-034 garage-ai-optimization-foundation** (NOUVEAU - ratification 18 juin 2026) : adoption **garage AI optimization comprehensive foundation rule-based + statistical Sprint 33 + foundation Phase 8+ advanced ML/LLMs**. Justification : (a) CLOTURE Phase 7 + premium differentiation, (b) AI-augmented platform positioning, (c) Customer value immediate, (d) Foundation advanced AI capital efficient, (e) Demo Day proof point. Architecture : 7 entites + 6 services + 4 handlers + 4 workers + Sprint 13 MV.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX

**Piege 1 : AI algorithms scope creep vs Sprint 33 foundation.** Tentation implement advanced ML Sprint 33 (transformer models + deep learning) = delivery timeline blown. Solution : strict rule-based + statistical Sprint 33 + roadmap Phase 8+ documented clear + acceptance criteria foundation only.

**Piege 2 : Training data privacy + multi-tenant isolation.** AI models could leak cross-garage data if RLS misconfigured. Solution : RLS strict garage_id per AI entity + algorithms per-garage isolated + tests negative ensuring no cross-tenant data + audit comprehensive.

**Piege 3 : Churn prediction false positives intervention cost.** Aggressive churn intervention (frequent campaigns + calls) annoys customers + brand damage. Solution : risk thresholds tunable per garage + intervention frequency caps + customer feedback loop foundation + A/B testing Phase 8+.

**Piege 4 : Predictive maintenance accuracy customer trust.** Wrong predictions damage trust ("you predicted service in 6 months but car broke at 3 months"). Solution : explainability factors transparent + confidence threshold high before notify customer + foundation only Sprint 33 (advanced Phase 8+) + customer opt-in only notifications.

**Piege 5 : Pricing optimization customer perception.** Dynamic pricing seems unfair to customers ("price changed since I checked yesterday"). Solution : pricing recommendations to garage_owner only (not customer auto-applied) + garage_owner discretion accept/reject + history transparent + foundation pricing variability subtle (max +/-30% from base).

**Piege 6 : Recommendation engine cold start customers.** New customers no history = no recommendations possible. Solution : fallback popularity-based recommendations (most-redeemed rewards + most-booked services) + onboarding minimal data collection + tier-based defaults.

**Piege 7 : Fraud detection false positives.** Legitimate customers flagged = customer service burden + frustration. Solution : risk thresholds tunable + review workflow garage_owner OR super_admin + escalation framework + legitimate-marked feedback loop reduce future false positives + foundation Phase 8+ ML refinement.

**Piege 8 : AI worker performance large garages.** Daily cron workers on 1000+ customers per garage = slow + resource intensive. Solution : Sprint 13 materialized views precomputed + worker batching + queue priority + horizontal scaling foundation Phase 8+ Kubernetes workers.

**Piege 9 : Algorithm explainability auditability.** Customer or garage_owner asks "why this recommendation?" = AI black box unacceptable. Solution : factors JSONB explainability stored per entity + UI displays factors transparent + foundation Phase 8+ SHAP/LIME advanced explainability.

**Piege 10 : Cost monitoring AI compute resources.** AI workers consume significant compute + memory + DB queries = infrastructure cost increase. Solution : monitoring Prometheus + alerts cost spikes + algorithms efficiency reviews + cron scheduling off-peak hours (04h-08h MA) + foundation Phase 8+ cost optimization + cloud AI services pay-per-use evaluation.

---

## 36. B-34-sprint-34-integration-stubs-insurers.md

```md
# B-34 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 34 : INTEGRATION STUBS INSURERS CONNECTEURS

> ## CORRECTION v3.1 (2026-06-02) -- PRIORITAIRE SUR LE RESTE DU FICHIER
>
> Ce fichier a ete genere avant le pivot v3.1 (ecosysteme 7 acteurs, decision-012-ecosysteme-7-acteurs). En cas de divergence, ces regles PRIMENT sur toute mention residuelle plus bas (qui parle encore de 6 acteurs, 26 roles, 7 types cross-tenant, 130 permissions, PartsHub, ou d'un e-constat genere/signe par Assurflow) :
>
> - Ecosysteme a 7 acteurs : Broker, Garage, Carrier, Expert, Customer/Assure, Tow, et CONSTATEUR (NOUVEAU v3.1).
> - Tenancy 3 niveaux : N1 tenants dedies (Garage, Broker, Carrier, Expert) ; N2 rattache (Customer/Assure) ; N3 sans tenant, acteur reseau leger style Uber-driver (Tow, Constateur).
> - RBAC : 28 roles (retrait de garage_parts_manager car PartsHub abandonne ; ajout de constateur_admin, constateur_dispatcher, constateur_agent).
> - Cross-tenant : 8 types (ajout de claim_to_constateur_dispatch). La ressource parts_order est remplacee par constat.
> - Permissions : 131 (module parts retire ~7 ; module constateur ajoute ~8).
> - PartsHub (decision-014) est ABANDONNE : il ne doit plus apparaitre comme fonctionnalite active.
> - e-constat : Assurflow ne cree, ne remplit ni ne signe JAMAIS le constat. Le constat est gere par l'application TIERCE e-constat de l'ecosysteme AFMA (saisie, signatures conformes loi 43-20, emission d'un numero officiel) ; Assurflow recupere les donnees du constat via API a partir de ce numero.

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 8 Sprint 1)
**Phase** : 8 -- Integration Stubs Connecteurs Externes (Sprints 34-35)
**Sprint** : 34 / 37 -- Phase 8 Sprint 1
**Position** : Apres Sprint 33 Garage AI Optimization (CLOTURE Phase 7), avant Sprint 35 Phase 8 Sprint 2
**Numerotation taches** : 9.1.1 a 9.1.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~80 heures developpement
**Priorite** : P0 PHASE 8 FOUNDATION CONNECTEURS EXTERNES + DEMO DAY READINESS
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **framework Integration Stubs Connecteurs Externes** -- couche infrastructure strategique permettant connexion future Skalean Assurflow avec ecosysteme externe Morocco (insurers + banks + government + telecom). Le sprint construit foundation comprehensive avec **5 categories connecteurs stubs** : (1) **Insurers stubs** trois fictional credible names approche C ratifiee (decision-027) -- **AssurMaghreb** (compagnie generaliste), **Atlas Insurance** (specialiste auto premium), **MediAssur** (santé + auto) -- APIs simulees implementant pattern realiste compagnies assurance Morocco (souscription police + verification couverture + sinistre declaration + reglement claim + reporting ACAPS), (2) **Banks connecteurs stubs** payment gateways -- **CMI (Centre Monetique Interbancaire)** stub principal Morocco + **Maroc Telecommerce** stub + **CBM (Crédit du Maroc)** stub -- patterns transactions (validation carte + autorisation + capture + refund + reconciliation reporting), (3) **Government APIs stubs** -- **CNDP (Commission Nationale Donnees Personnelles)** stub compliance reporting + **ACAPS reporting** stub regulatory submissions + **DGI (Direction Generale Impots)** stub tax reporting, (4) **Telecom SMS providers stubs** -- **Maroc Telecom SMS gateway** + **Orange Maroc SMS gateway** + **Inwi SMS gateway** -- patterns envoi SMS notifications + delivery reports + cost tracking, et (5) **framework observability + retry/circuit breaker pattern** -- ExternalConnector entity + ConnectorRequest tracking + ConnectorEvent + ConnectorMapping + ConnectorWebhook + observability complete (latency + success rate + circuit breaker state) + retry exponential backoff BullMQ workers + health checks periodiques.

Le sprint introduit **decision-035 ratification** integration stubs framework + connecteurs externes architecture + foundation Phase 9 production deployment + observability standardisee + retry resilience pattern + circuit breaker pattern + webhook secure HMAC + correlation_id traceability cross-service. Cette approche stubs preserve developpement velocity Phase 8 sans dependences externes APIs non disponibles + permet Demo Day 30 juin 2026 demonstration end-to-end credible avec stubs realistes + foundation Phase 9 swap stubs vers real APIs production via configuration toggle.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'**entites domain Integration Connecteurs** :
- **`ExternalConnector`** (id + connector_type enum insurer/bank/government/telecom + provider_name VARCHAR + base_url + credentials_encrypted JSONB Sprint 10 encryption + active BOOLEAN + last_health_check_at + health_status enum healthy/degraded/down + circuit_breaker_state enum closed/open/half-open + circuit_breaker_failure_threshold INTEGER default 5 + circuit_breaker_recovery_timeout_seconds INTEGER default 60 + sandbox_mode BOOLEAN + audit_trail) avec UNIQUE (connector_type, provider_name)
- **`ConnectorRequest`** (id + connector_id FK + request_type VARCHAR + idempotency_key VARCHAR + payload_request JSONB encrypted Sprint 10 + response_status_code INTEGER + response_payload JSONB encrypted + latency_ms INTEGER + status enum pending/success/failed/timeout + retry_count INTEGER + correlation_id UUID + initiated_by_user_id + initiated_at + completed_at + error_message + audit_trail) avec UNIQUE idempotency_key + indexes performance (correlation_id + status partial + initiated_at)
- **`ConnectorEvent`** (id + connector_id + event_type VARCHAR + payload JSONB + sent_at + delivery_status enum pending/delivered/failed/expired + retry_count + last_retry_at + correlation_id + audit_trail) append-only outbound events log
- **`ConnectorMapping`** (id + connector_id + mapping_type enum field_mapping/value_transformation/enum_mapping + skalean_field VARCHAR + external_field VARCHAR + transformation_rule JSONB + active + audit_trail) field mappings Skalean fields -> external API fields
- **`ConnectorWebhook`** (id + connector_id + webhook_url + secret_hmac_encrypted Sprint 10 + active + last_received_at + total_received_count + failed_received_count + audit_trail) incoming webhooks endpoints management

Un **package `@insurtech/integration-connectors`** v1.0.0 NestJS module, **5 services principaux** :

**ExternalConnectorService** :
- `createConnector(config)` validate + encrypt credentials Sprint 10 + audit
- `updateConnector(id, updates)` validate + re-encrypt if credentials change + audit
- `getConnector(id)` lookup + decrypt credentials runtime + RLS check
- `listConnectors(filters)` filter type + status + active
- `healthCheck(connector_id)` HTTP ping + measure latency + update health_status + audit
- `circuitBreakerCheck(connector_id)` :
  - If state='closed' : allow request
  - If state='open' : reject immediately + return cached fallback if available
  - If state='half-open' : allow 1 test request + transition closed if success OR open if fail
- `recordFailure(connector_id)` increment failure_count + transition open if >= threshold + audit
- `recordSuccess(connector_id)` reset failure_count + transition closed if was half-open + audit
- Foundation pattern Hystrix-like resilience + observability Prometheus metrics

**InsurerConnectorService** stubs (3 fictional credible Morocco) :
- `assurMaghrebStub` :
  - Methods : checkPolicy(policy_number) + subscribePolicy(payload) + declareIncident(payload) + getPaymentStatus(claim_id)
  - Response patterns : 80% success + 15% latency 2-5s + 5% error 500 (realistic distribution)
  - Sandbox mode complete + deterministic responses based input patterns
  - Idempotency-Key support
- `atlasInsuranceStub` similar patterns specialiste auto premium
- `mediAssurStub` similar patterns sante + auto
- Foundation Phase 9 swap real APIs via configuration toggle INTEGRATION_CONNECTORS_MODE=stub/production
- Sprint 14 events Avro emit `external_insurer.request.completed` per call

**BankConnectorService** stubs :
- `cmiStub` (Centre Monetique Interbancaire) :
  - Methods : authorize(amount, card_token) + capture(transaction_id) + refund(transaction_id, amount) + getTransactionStatus(transaction_id)
  - Realistic patterns Morocco banking
- `marocTelecommerceStub` payment gateway
- `cbmStub` (Crédit du Maroc) basic
- PCI-DSS compliance foundation (no card data stored + tokenization patterns)

**GovernmentConnectorService** stubs :
- `cndpStub` :
  - Methods : submitDataProcessingDeclaration(payload) + verifyConsentStatus(customer_id) + reportDataBreach(payload)
  - GDPR/Loi 09-08 alignment
- `acapsReportingStub` :
  - Methods : submitDailyReport(date) + submitMonthlyReport(month) + submitClaimReport(claim_id)
  - Sprint 28 admin reports framework integration
- `dgiStub` (Direction Generale Impots) :
  - Methods : submitInvoice(invoice_id) + submitVATDeclaration(period) + verifyTaxId(tax_id)
  - Sprint 31 invoices integration

**TelecomConnectorService** stubs SMS providers Morocco :
- `marocTelecomSMSStub` :
  - Methods : sendSMS(phone, message) + getDeliveryReport(message_id) + getCostReport(period)
  - Phone numbers MA format +212 6/7/5 validation
- `orangeMarocSMSStub` similar patterns
- `inwiSMSStub` similar patterns
- Integration Sprint 9 notifications routing (active provider config per garage_id)

Des **2 workers BullMQ Repeatable** :

**ConnectorRetryWorker** :
- Cron-less queue worker processing failed ConnectorRequest status='failed' + retry_count < MAX_RETRIES
- Exponential backoff (1min, 5min, 15min, 1h, 6h, 24h then expire)
- Retry with same idempotency_key
- Audit retry attempts
- Emit `connector.request.retried` event Sprint 14

**ConnectorHealthCheckWorker** :
- Cron `CONNECTOR_HEALTH_CHECK_CRON=*/5 * * * *` (every 5 minutes)
- Iterate active connectors + healthCheck + update health_status
- Alert Prometheus if connector down > 15 min
- Audit health checks

**Integration Sprint 9 notifications SMS routing** : TelecomConnectorService.sendSMS replaces direct provider integration with abstracted routing per garage_id active provider configuration,

**Integration Sprint 14 events Avro Kafka** : emit events per connector request `connector.{type}.{action}.{outcome}` for downstream observability + analytics,

**Integration Sprint 13 analytics ClickHouse MV** : connector_observability_mv aggregating latency + success rate + circuit breaker transitions per connector per garage per day for analytics dashboards,

**Integration Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions** screens nouveaux **3 tabs** :
- `/integrations/connectors/*` (CRUD connectors + health status real-time + circuit breaker state + manual reset + sandbox/production toggle)
- `/integrations/requests/*` (ConnectorRequest browse + filter + correlation_id search + retry manual + drill-down details encrypted)
- `/integrations/webhooks/*` (ConnectorWebhook management + HMAC secret rotation + endpoint test)

Des **endpoints REST integration connectors** controller `integration-connectors.controller.ts` 16 endpoints + permissions Sprint 7.5a (super_admin only) + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15,

Et **35+ tests E2E** scenarios couvrant insurer subscription + claim + bank authorization + government reporting + telecom SMS + retry mechanism + circuit breaker transitions + webhooks + Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions + Sprint 13 observability MV.

Le sprint constitue **foundation Phase 8 Integration Stubs strategique** + **Demo Day 30 juin 2026 demonstration end-to-end credibility** + **foundation Phase 9 production swap stubs -> real APIs**. Sans Sprint 34, Skalean = silo isole sans connection ecosysteme Morocco = pas demo credible jury ACAPS. Avec Sprint 34, Skalean = platform integree avec stubs realistes + observability + resilience = compelling proof point execution capability + technical depth + production-ready architecture.

- ExternalConnectorService + circuit breaker + retry pattern

### Exclus du Sprint 34 (defere Sprint 35 et Phase 9)

Real APIs integration (production endpoints AssurMaghreb actual + CMI actual + etc) defers Phase 9 deployment + post-go-live agreements, partner agreements + contracts negotiation + integration testing real environments defers Phase 9 commercial, API specifications detailed per partner (OpenAPI spec real APIs) defers Sprint 35 Phase 8 Sprint 2, advanced webhook patterns (event sourcing + replay + dead-letter queue advanced) defers Sprint 35, multi-region failover + geographic redundancy defers Phase 9 production architecture, PCI-DSS certification real production compliance defers Phase 9, financial reconciliation real banks defers Phase 9, government regulatory real submissions defers Phase 9 post-licensing, SMS provider real contracts + pricing defers Phase 9 commercial, advanced caching strategies external responses defers Sprint 35, GraphQL federation foundation defers Phase 8+ advanced, et inter-connector orchestration workflows (insurer + bank coordinated flow) defers Sprint 35.

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 34 est **foundation Phase 8 + Demo Day readiness end-to-end** + **technical architecture credibility ACAPS jury investisseurs**. Quatre dimensions strategiques majeures.

Premierement, **Demo Day 30 juin 2026 demonstration end-to-end credible**. Sans connecteurs stubs realistes, demonstration Skalean limitee a flows internes seulement = pas convaincant pour ACAPS jury attendant proof connection ecosysteme assurance Morocco. Avec Sprint 34 stubs, demonstration end-to-end (customer subscribe police via AssurMaghreb stub + claim declared + bank reglement via CMI stub + ACAPS reporting automated) = compelling narrative production-ready architecture.

Deuxiemement, **foundation Phase 9 production swap stubs -> real APIs**. Architecture stubs preserve developpement velocity Phase 8 + permet swap configuration toggle Phase 9 (INTEGRATION_CONNECTORS_MODE=stub vs production) = pas rework code Phase 9 + capital efficient + risk minimized go-live.

Troisiemement, **resilience pattern circuit breaker + retry production-grade**. External APIs flaky par nature (network issues + downtime + rate limits). Sprint 34 implements Hystrix-like pattern circuit breaker + retry exponential backoff + observability complete = production-grade resilience foundation. Differentiation enterprise SaaS Morocco vs basic platforms ne gerant pas externe failures gracieusement.

Quatriemement, **observability + auditability comprehensive**. Sprint 34 tracks ConnectorRequest detailed + correlation_id traceability cross-service + Sprint 13 analytics MV + Prometheus metrics + Sprint 28 admin-portal dashboards = visibility complete activity externe + debugging capability + SLA monitoring foundation Phase 8+.

### Decision-035 -- Integration Stubs Framework + Connecteurs Externes Foundation

**Justification** :
- (a) Demo Day 30 juin 2026 demonstration end-to-end credible ACAPS jury
- (b) Foundation Phase 9 production swap stubs -> real APIs via configuration toggle
- (c) Resilience pattern circuit breaker + retry production-grade
- (d) Observability + auditability comprehensive ConnectorRequest tracking
- (e) Architecture stubs preserve velocity Phase 8 sans dependences externes APIs

**Architecture** :
- 5 entites + RLS strict ou)/ application
- 5 services + 12 stubs implementations + 2 workers + 1 webhook receiver
- Sprint 13 analytics MV + Sprint 14 events Avro + Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions
- Foundation Phase 9 production swap

**Decision-028 admin-portal-framework** : strict (Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions integrations).

**Decision-035 integration-stubs-framework-connecteurs** (NOUVEAU - ratification 19 juin 2026) : adoption **integration stubs framework comprehensive + connecteurs externes architecture stubs 12 implementations (3 insurers + 3 banks + 3 government + 3 telecom) + foundation Phase 9 production swap real APIs**. Justification : (a) Demo Day end-to-end credible, (b) Foundation Phase 9 swap, (c) Resilience pattern, (d) Observability comprehensive, (e) Velocity preserve. Architecture : 5 entites + 5 services + 12 stubs + 2 workers + Sprint 13/14/28 integration.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX

**Piege 1 : Brand names confusion entreprises reelles.** Tentation utiliser noms reels (Wafa Assurance + RMA + AXA Morocco) = risque legal + brand infringement + confusion Demo Day jury. Solution : decision-027 fictional credible brand names approche C strict (AssurMaghreb + Atlas Insurance + MediAssur insurers + CMI/CBM/Maroc Telecommerce banks real institutional names retained but stubs simulated + Maroc Telecom + Orange + Inwi telecom real provider names retained for SMS but stubs simulated + government CNDP + ACAPS + DGI real institutions but stubs only) + disclaimer "stubs implementation" clear UI.

**Piege 2 : Credentials leakage logs/errors.** External API credentials accidentally logged or returned in error messages = security breach. Solution : credentials_encrypted JSONB Sprint 10 + decrypt only runtime + scrub credentials patterns in error messages + Sprint 7 audit comprehensive without credentials + Sprint 27 sensitive data masking.

**Piege 3 : Idempotency key collisions external + internal.** Same idempotency_key used different connectors causes confusion. Solution : idempotency_key scoped per connector_id (UNIQUE composite) + generation UUID v4 + correlation_id separate for traceability cross-connector + tests UNIQUE constraint.

**Piege 4 : Circuit breaker false positives.** Aggressive failure threshold = breaker opens unnecessarily blocking legitimate requests. Solution : threshold tunable per connector (default 5 failures within 60s window) + recovery timeout 60s default + half-open test request validates recovery + monitoring transitions Prometheus alerts.

**Piege 5 : Retry exponential backoff queue overflow.** Many failed requests retry simultaneously causes BullMQ queue overflow + downstream connector overload. Solution : retry queue rate limited per connector + max retry count (6 attempts then expire) + jitter random 0-30% on backoff to prevent thundering herd + dead-letter queue foundation Phase 8+.

**Piege 6 : Webhook HMAC verification bypass.** Incoming webhooks not properly verified = security breach allowing fake events injection. Solution : HMAC SHA-256 strict verification + secret_hmac_encrypted Sprint 10 + reject non-verified requests 401 + audit verification failures + rotate secrets foundation Sprint 28 admin-portal.

**Piege 7 : Stubs response patterns not realistic.** Stubs return always success = production code untested for failure paths. Solution : realistic distribution 80% success + 15% latency variability + 5% errors (500 + 503 + timeout) + deterministic responses based input patterns for testability + foundation Phase 9 swap real APIs encounters real failures gracefully.

**Piege 8 : Correlation ID propagation gaps.** Cross-service correlation_id not consistently propagated = debugging nightmare. Solution : correlation_id middleware automatic + extract from X-Correlation-Id header OR generate + propagate to all external connector calls + Sprint 14 events Avro include correlation_id + Sprint 13 analytics indexed.

**Piege 9 : Sprint 9 SMS routing migration risk.** Changing SMS provider integration from Sprint 9 direct to Sprint 34 abstracted breaks existing notifications. Solution : Sprint 34 TelecomConnectorService backward-compatible interface + Sprint 9 NotificationService refactor minor + tests E2E both Sprint 9 + Sprint 34 + feature flag rollout.

**Piege 10 : Observability data explosion.** ConnectorRequest table grows fast (every external call logged) = storage cost + query slow. Solution : retention policy 90 days hot + 1 year cold storage Sprint 28 archival + partitioning by month + indexes performance optimized + Sprint 13 MV aggregations + Prometheus metrics aggregated separate.

---

### Tache 9.1.8 -- Workers Retry + HealthCheck

**5h | P0 | 110-150 KB**

ConnectorRetryWorker BullMQ + ConnectorHealthCheckWorker cron.

**Livrables** :
- Worker `connector-retry.worker.ts` BullMQ :
  - Process failed ConnectorRequest status='failed' + retry_count < 6
  - Exponential backoff (1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 6h, 24h) + jitter random 0-30%
  - Rate limited per connector
  - Same idempotency_key reuse
  - Sprint 14 emit `connector.request.retried`
  - Dead-letter queue foundation if all retries exhausted
- Worker `connector-health-check.worker.ts` cron every 5 minutes :
  - Iterate active connectors
  - HTTP ping + measure latency
  - Update health_status + circuit breaker if degraded
  - Prometheus metrics
  - Alerts if connector down > 15 min (foundation Sprint 28)
- 12+ tests scenarios dont stress retry

## 37. B-35-sprint-35-pentest-security-hardening.md

```md
# B-35 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 35 : PENTEST + SECURITY HARDENING

> ## CORRECTION v3.1 (2026-06-02) -- PRIORITAIRE SUR LE RESTE DU FICHIER
>
> Ce fichier a ete genere avant le pivot v3.1 (ecosysteme 7 acteurs, decision-012-ecosysteme-7-acteurs). En cas de divergence, ces regles PRIMENT sur toute mention residuelle plus bas (qui parle encore de 6 acteurs, 26 roles, 7 types cross-tenant, 130 permissions, PartsHub, ou d'un e-constat genere/signe par Assurflow) :
>
> - Ecosysteme a 7 acteurs : Broker, Garage, Carrier, Expert, Customer/Assure, Tow, et CONSTATEUR (NOUVEAU v3.1).
> - Tenancy 3 niveaux : N1 tenants dedies (Garage, Broker, Carrier, Expert) ; N2 rattache (Customer/Assure) ; N3 sans tenant, acteur reseau leger style Uber-driver (Tow, Constateur).
> - RBAC : 28 roles (retrait de garage_parts_manager car PartsHub abandonne ; ajout de constateur_admin, constateur_dispatcher, constateur_agent).
> - Cross-tenant : 8 types (ajout de claim_to_constateur_dispatch). La ressource parts_order est remplacee par constat.
> - Permissions : 131 (module parts retire ~7 ; module constateur ajoute ~8).
> - PartsHub (decision-014) est ABANDONNE : il ne doit plus apparaitre comme fonctionnalite active.
> - e-constat : Assurflow ne cree, ne remplit ni ne signe JAMAIS le constat. Le constat est gere par l'application TIERCE e-constat de l'ecosysteme AFMA (saisie, signatures conformes loi 43-20, emission d'un numero officiel) ; Assurflow recupere les donnees du constat via API a partir de ce numero.

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 8 Sprint 2 -- DERNIER Phase 8 CLOTURE)
**Phase** : 8 -- Integration Stubs + Security (Sprints 34-35)
**Sprint** : 35 / 37 -- Phase 8 Sprint 2 (CLOTURE Phase 8)
**Position** : Apres Sprint 34 Integration Stubs Connecteurs Externes, avant Sprint 36 Phase 9 Production Deployment
**Numerotation taches** : 9.2.1 a 9.2.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~85 heures developpement
**Priorite** : P0 CLOTURE PHASE 8 + PRODUCTION READINESS PHASE 9
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **framework Pentest + Security Hardening comprehensive** -- couche securite strategique cloturant Phase 8 + preparation production Phase 9 deployment. Le sprint construit **8 categories security comprehensive** : (1) **Pentest internal + external** suivant **OWASP Top 10 2021** + **OWASP ASVS Level 2 verification** + tests SAST (Static Application Security Testing) + DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing) + manual penetration testing sur tous endpoints exposes + reporting comprehensive avec CVSS scoring, (2) **Vulnerability scanning automated** -- **Snyk** integration CI/CD pour code + dependencies + **Trivy** containers + Kubernetes manifests + **Gitleaks** secrets in code + **OWASP Dependency-Check** CVE database + reporting Sprint 28 admin-portal dashboards, (3) **Secrets management** -- **HashiCorp Vault foundation** ou **AWS Secrets Manager** integration + automated rotation policies (database credentials 90 days + API tokens 30 days + JWT signing keys 180 days) + audit access logs + emergency rotation procedures, (4) **Network security** -- **Web Application Firewall (WAF)** foundation (AWS WAF ou Cloudflare WAF) + **rate limiting** advanced per endpoint + **DDoS protection** foundation + **CORS** strict + **CSP (Content Security Policy)** headers strict + **HSTS** + **X-Frame-Options** + **Referrer-Policy**, (5) **Data protection** -- **encryption at rest** PostgreSQL TDE foundation + ClickHouse encryption + S3 server-side encryption KMS (Sprint 10 extension) + **encryption in transit** TLS 1.3 strict + mTLS inter-services foundation + **key rotation** automated 90 days + **PII data masking** Sprint 27 cross-tenant integration, (6) **Compliance audits** -- **CNDP (Commission Nationale Donnees Personnelles)** Loi 09-08 alignment audit + **ACAPS regulatory** compliance verification + **PCI-DSS readiness assessment** Phase 9 si payments + **GDPR** alignment EU customers foundation + reporting Sprint 28, (7) **Disaster recovery + backups** -- **PostgreSQL automated backups** pg_dump + pg_basebackup + WAL archiving + **ClickHouse backups** ALTER TABLE FREEZE + **Redis backups** + **S3 cross-region replication** + **RTO target 4 heures** + **RPO target 1 heure** + **DR testing trimestriel** automatisee + recovery procedures documented, et (8) **Security incident response** -- **incident management framework** (detect + contain + eradicate + recover + lessons-learned) + **Sprint 9 notifications critical alerts security_admin** + **post-mortem template** + **runbooks operations** + foundation SIEM Phase 9+.

Le sprint produit **rapport pentest comprehensive** (executive summary + findings detailed + CVSS scoring + remediation roadmap + cost estimates) + **security policy document** + **compliance attestations** (CNDP self-assessment + ACAPS pre-audit + PCI-DSS gap analysis) + **disaster recovery plan documented** + **runbooks operations security**. Le sprint introduit **decision-036 ratification** security hardening framework + production readiness comprehensive + foundation Phase 9 production deployment SLA enterprise-grade + ISO 27001 alignment Phase 9+.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'**entites domain Security** :
- **`SecurityScan`** (id + scan_type enum sast/dast/dependency/container/secrets/manual_pentest + tool VARCHAR + scope JSONB + status enum scheduled/running/completed/failed + findings_count_critical INTEGER + findings_count_high INTEGER + findings_count_medium INTEGER + findings_count_low INTEGER + report_url + report_s3_key + scan_started_at + scan_completed_at + executor enum automated_ci/automated_cron/manual + audit_trail) UNIQUE (scan_type, scan_started_at::date)
- **`VulnerabilityFinding`** (id + scan_id FK + cve_id VARCHAR nullable + severity enum critical/high/medium/low/info + cvss_score NUMERIC nullable + cvss_vector VARCHAR nullable + component VARCHAR + affected_files TEXT[] + description TEXT + remediation_recommendation TEXT + remediation_effort_hours INTEGER + status enum open/in_progress/resolved/false_positive/risk_accepted + assigned_to_user_id FK nullable + due_date + resolved_at nullable + resolution_notes + audit_trail) indexes (severity + status partial + assigned_to + due_date)
- **`SecretRotation`** (id + secret_identifier + secret_type enum database_password/api_token/jwt_signing_key/oauth_secret/encryption_key + last_rotated_at + next_rotation_due + rotation_interval_days + rotation_strategy enum automatic_zero_downtime/manual_scheduled/manual_emergency + last_rotation_status + audit_trail)
- **`BackupSnapshot`** (id + backup_type enum postgres_full/postgres_incremental/postgres_wal/clickhouse_freeze/redis_rdb/s3_replication + status enum scheduled/running/completed/failed + size_bytes BIGINT + duration_seconds INTEGER + retention_until + storage_location TEXT + verified_at nullable + verification_status enum pending/verified/corrupted + audit_trail) indexes (backup_type + verified_at + retention_until)
- **`DisasterRecoveryTest`** (id + scenario enum database_full_restore/clickhouse_restore/region_failover/key_rotation_emergency + executed_at + rto_target_seconds INTEGER + rto_actual_seconds INTEGER + rpo_target_seconds INTEGER + rpo_actual_seconds INTEGER + status enum pass/fail/partial + findings JSONB + improvements_identified TEXT + audit_trail)
- **`ComplianceAudit`** (id + framework enum cndp_loi_09_08/acaps_regulatory/pci_dss/gdpr/iso_27001 + audit_date + auditor VARCHAR + audit_type enum self_assessment/external_audit/pre_audit/certification + status enum compliant/partial/non_compliant + findings_count_critical INTEGER + findings_count_high INTEGER + report_url + report_s3_key + remediation_due_date + remediation_status enum not_started/in_progress/completed + audit_trail)
- **`SecurityIncident`** (id + incident_type enum unauthorized_access/data_breach/ddos_attack/credentials_compromise/malware/insider_threat/other + severity enum p0_critical/p1_high/p2_medium/p3_low + status enum open/investigating/contained/resolved/closed + reported_at + reported_by_user_id FK + resolved_at nullable + closed_at nullable + affected_systems TEXT[] + affected_users_count INTEGER + timeline JSONB append-only entries + root_cause_analysis TEXT + post_mortem_url + lessons_learned TEXT + audit_trail) indexes (severity + status partial + reported_at)

Un **package `@insurtech/security-hardening`** v1.0.0 NestJS module + **7 services principaux** :

**SecurityScanningService** :
- Integration **Snyk** API for code + dependencies scanning (npm + composer + pip foundation)
- Integration **Trivy** scanner for Docker images + Kubernetes manifests (CVE detection)
- Integration **Gitleaks** for secrets in code (foundation pre-commit hooks + CI/CD)
- Integration **OWASP Dependency-Check** for CVE database scanning
- `scheduleScan(scan_type, scope)` create SecurityScan + queue
- `processScan(scan_id)` worker execute scanner + parse results + create VulnerabilityFindings
- `getLatestScanResults(scan_type)` lookup latest + aggregations
- Cron `SECURITY_SCAN_DEPENDENCY_CRON=0 2 * * *` daily 02h00 MA dependencies
- Cron `SECURITY_SCAN_CONTAINER_CRON=0 3 * * *` daily 03h00 MA containers
- Cron `SECURITY_SCAN_SECRETS_CRON=0 4 * * 1` weekly Monday 04h00 secrets
- Foundation Sprint 28 admin-portal security dashboards

**VulnerabilityManagementService** :
- `createFinding(scan_id, finding_data)` create VulnerabilityFinding + auto-assign based severity + Sprint 9 notify security_admin if critical
- `assignFinding(finding_id, user_id, due_date)` workflow assignment + Sprint 9 notify
- `updateFindingStatus(finding_id, status, notes)` transitions in_progress -> resolved + audit
- `markFalsePositive(finding_id, justification)` audit + risk acceptance
- `getOpenFindings(filters)` filter severity + assigned_to + due_date overdue
- `getRemediationDashboard()` aggregations + KPIs
- SLA tracking : Critical 7 days + High 30 days + Medium 90 days + Low 180 days
- Sprint 9 notifications overdue critical findings escalation

**SecretsManagementService** :
- **HashiCorp Vault** foundation integration (OR AWS Secrets Manager fallback) :
  - `getSecret(secret_identifier)` runtime decrypt + audit access
  - `rotateSecret(secret_identifier, new_value)` atomic rotation + zero downtime (write new + verify + delete old)
  - `scheduleRotation(secret_identifier, interval_days)` configure rotation policy
- Automated rotation cron `SECRETS_ROTATION_CRON=0 5 * * *` daily 05h00 :
  - Iterate SecretRotations next_rotation_due <= now
  - Trigger rotation per strategy (automatic_zero_downtime executes + manual_scheduled notifies)
  - Update SecretRotation last_rotated_at + next_rotation_due
  - Audit comprehensive
- Emergency rotation procedure `emergencyRotateSecret(secret_identifier, reason)` immediate + escalation
- Foundation Phase 9 advanced rotation (database password rotation with connection pool refresh + JWT keys rotation with grace period)

**NetworkSecurityService** :
- WAF integration foundation (AWS WAF rules + Cloudflare WAF rules)
- Rate limiting advanced per endpoint Sprint 7.5a integration extension :
  - Public endpoints 100 req/min/IP
  - Authenticated endpoints 1000 req/min/user
  - Admin endpoints 50 req/min/user
  - Webhooks 10 req/sec/source
- DDoS protection foundation (rate limits aggressive + IP reputation + geo-blocking optional)
- CORS strict configuration per origin
- Security headers middleware :
  ```typescript
  app.use((req, res, next) => {
    res.setHeader('Content-Security-Policy', "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; ...");
    res.setHeader('Strict-Transport-Security', 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload');
    res.setHeader('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY');
    res.setHeader('X-Content-Type-Options', 'nosniff');
    res.setHeader('Referrer-Policy', 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin');
    res.setHeader('Permissions-Policy', 'geolocation=(self), microphone=()');
    next();
  });
  ```
- Foundation Sprint 28 monitoring WAF events

**DataProtectionService** :
- Encryption at rest verification :
  - PostgreSQL TDE foundation (configuration ALTER SYSTEM SET data_directory ...) Phase 9
  - ClickHouse encryption foundation
  - S3 server-side encryption KMS strict (Sprint 10 extension)
  - Redis at-rest encryption foundation
- Encryption in transit :
  - TLS 1.3 minimum enforced
  - Certificate management Let's Encrypt + renewal automated
  - mTLS inter-services foundation Phase 9
- Key rotation orchestration with SecretsManagementService integration
- PII masking Sprint 27 cross-tenant extension
- DLP (Data Loss Prevention) foundation Phase 9+

**BackupManagementService** :
- PostgreSQL backups :
  - Full backup daily pg_basebackup
  - Incremental backups WAL archiving continuous
  - Verification weekly automated restore test isolated environment
- ClickHouse backups ALTER TABLE FREEZE daily
- Redis backups RDB snapshots hourly + AOF append-only
- S3 cross-region replication (eu-west-1 -> eu-west-3 foundation Phase 9)
- Retention policies (daily 30 days + weekly 12 weeks + monthly 12 months + yearly forever foundation)
- Cron `BACKUP_POSTGRES_FULL_CRON=0 1 * * *` daily 01h00 MA
- Cron `BACKUP_CLICKHOUSE_FREEZE_CRON=0 2 * * *` daily 02h00 MA
- Cron `BACKUP_VERIFICATION_CRON=0 6 * * 0` weekly Sunday 06h00 MA
- BackupSnapshot tracking comprehensive

**DisasterRecoveryService** :
- DR scenarios documented + executable :
  - Database full restore RTO 4h RPO 1h
  - ClickHouse restore RTO 8h RPO 24h
  - Region failover foundation Phase 9
  - Key rotation emergency RTO 1h RPO 0
- DR testing quarterly automated :
  - Restore latest backup to isolated environment
  - Measure RTO actual vs target
  - Measure RPO actual vs target (data loss)
  - Pass/Fail + findings + improvements
- Runbooks operations documented + tested
- Sprint 9 notifications DR test results to security_admin

**ComplianceAuditService** :
- CNDP Loi 09-08 self-assessment :
  - Data processing declaration verification
  - Consent management Sprint 25 verification
  - DPO (Data Protection Officer) foundation
  - Data subject rights workflows (access + deletion + portability) Sprint 27 cross-tenant extension
- ACAPS regulatory pre-audit :
  - Sprint 28 reporting framework
  - Sprint 34 ACAPS reporting stub integration
  - Documentation alignment ACAPS guidelines
- PCI-DSS gap analysis Phase 9 readiness :
  - Card data flow analysis
  - Tokenization patterns Sprint 34
  - Network segmentation foundation
  - Logging + monitoring SAQ-A vs SAQ-D scope determination
- GDPR alignment foundation (EU customers)
- Foundation ISO 27001 Phase 9+

**SecurityIncidentService** :
- Incident lifecycle (open -> investigating -> contained -> resolved -> closed)
- `reportIncident(incident_data)` create + Sprint 9 immediate alert security_admin + page on-call
- `updateIncident(incident_id, update)` timeline append-only entries
- `closeIncident(incident_id, post_mortem)` post-mortem + lessons learned + audit
- Runbooks per incident_type (data_breach + ddos + credentials_compromise + etc)
- Foundation SIEM (Security Information Event Management) Phase 9+
- Sprint 9 notifications critical alerts + escalation chain

**Integration Sprint 9 notifications** : security_admin role notifications critical findings overdue + DR test results + security incidents,

**Integration Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions** **4 nouveaux tabs security** :
- `/security/scans/*` (security scans browse + findings drill-down + remediation tracking)
- `/security/findings/*` (vulnerability findings list + filter severity/status + assign + update + SLA tracking)
- `/security/incidents/*` (security incidents browse + report new + lifecycle management + post-mortem)
- `/security/compliance/*` (compliance audits + readiness assessments + remediation roadmap)

**Integration Sprint 13 analytics ClickHouse MV** : security_metrics_mv (findings trend + scan results aggregated + incident response times + SLA compliance) refresh daily 04h00,

Et **30+ tests E2E** scenarios couvrant pentest reports + vulnerability management + secrets rotation + WAF rules + backup restore + DR test + compliance audit + security incident response.

Le sprint constitue **CLOTURE Phase 8** + **production readiness Phase 9 deployment** + **foundation security operations enterprise-grade** + **compliance attestations Demo Day**. Sans Sprint 35, Skalean = production deployment Phase 9 sans security hardening = risk inacceptable. Avec Sprint 35, Skalean = production-ready security posture + compliance attestations + incident response framework = enterprise SaaS Morocco credibility complete.

External pentest by certified third-party (e.g. Cobalt + HackerOne + Synack) defers Phase 9 commercial post-launch, ISO 27001 certification full audit defers Phase 9+ post-go-live commercial, PCI-DSS certification full QSA assessment defers Phase 9 commercial post-payment-launch, SIEM (Security Information Event Management) full implementation (Splunk + ELK + Datadog) defers Phase 9+, SOC 2 Type II certification defers Phase 9+ enterprise customers, advanced WAF rules tuning post-go-live analysis defers Phase 9, bug bounty program defers Phase 9+ post-public-launch, zero-trust network architecture full defers Phase 9+ advanced, eBPF-based runtime security monitoring defers Phase 9+, container runtime security (Falco) defers Phase 9+, secrets injection at runtime (Vault Agent sidecar pattern) defers Phase 9 deployment, advanced threat detection ML-based defers Phase 9+, et chaos engineering (Chaos Monkey + Litmus) regular practice defers Phase 9+ post-stability.

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 35 est **CLOTURE Phase 8** + **production readiness Phase 9 deployment foundation absolute critical**. Quatre dimensions strategiques majeures.

Premierement, **production deployment Phase 9 sans security hardening = inacceptable risk**. Deployer InsurTech platform Morocco sans pentest + compliance audits + backup strategy + DR plan = data breach inevitable + regulatory non-compliance + brand damage catastrophique. Sprint 35 mitige risques production de maniere comprehensive.

Deuxiemement, **regulatory compliance Morocco (CNDP + ACAPS) mandatory**. CNDP Loi 09-08 stricte sur traitement donnees personnelles. ACAPS regulatory framework insurance. Sprint 35 produces self-assessment + pre-audit reports = preparation Phase 9 official audits + ACAPS Programme Emergence sign-off.

Troisiemement, **Demo Day 30 juin 2026 enterprise credibility**. Investisseurs + jury ACAPS attendent demonstration production-readiness + security posture comprehensive. Sprint 35 provides tangible evidence (pentest report + compliance attestations + DR plan) = enterprise SaaS Morocco credibility complete.

Quatriemement, **foundation Phase 9+ ISO 27001 + SOC 2 + scaling enterprise**. Architecture security Sprint 35 = foundation pour certifications enterprise Phase 9+ (ISO 27001 + SOC 2 Type II) requises grands clients corporate Morocco. Capital efficient sans rework Phase 9+.

### Decision-036 -- Security Hardening Framework + Pentest + Production Readiness

**Justification** :
- (a) Production deployment Phase 9 sans security hardening = unacceptable risk
- (b) Regulatory compliance Morocco CNDP + ACAPS mandatory
- (c) Demo Day enterprise credibility proof point
- (d) Foundation Phase 9+ ISO 27001 + SOC 2 enterprise certifications
- (e) Risk mitigation comprehensive (data breach + regulatory + brand damage)

**Architecture** :
- 7 entites domain
- 9 services + 8 cron workers
- Integration Snyk + Trivy + Gitleaks + OWASP Dependency-Check + Vault
- Sprint 9 critical alerts + Sprint 28 admin-portal 4 tabs + Sprint 13 MV
- Pentest report + compliance attestations + DR plan + runbooks

**Decision-036 security-hardening-pentest-production-readiness** (NOUVEAU - ratification 20 juin 2026) : adoption **security hardening framework comprehensive + pentest internal + external OWASP Top 10 + ASVS Level 2 + secrets management Vault foundation + WAF + DDoS protection + encryption at rest + in transit + key rotation + backup strategies + DR plan + compliance audits CNDP + ACAPS + PCI-DSS gap analysis + GDPR alignment + security incident response framework + foundation Phase 9+ ISO 27001 + SOC 2**. Justification : (a) Production readiness risk mitigation, (b) Regulatory compliance Morocco mandatory, (c) Demo Day enterprise credibility, (d) Foundation Phase 9+ certifications, (e) Capital efficient comprehensive Sprint 35. Architecture : 7 entites + 9 services + 8 workers + Sprint 9/13/28 integration + pentest reports + compliance docs + runbooks.

---

## PIEGES TECHNIQUES GENERAUX

**Piege 1 : Scope creep advanced certifications Sprint 35.** Tentation implement ISO 27001 + SOC 2 complete Sprint 35 = timeline blown. Solution : foundation Sprint 35 + roadmap Phase 9+ documente clair + scope acceptance criteria pentest + compliance gap analysis only.

**Piege 2 : Secrets exposure during rotation.** Rotating database password breaks active connections + service downtime. Solution : zero-downtime rotation pattern (write new secret + verify connections + remove old + grace period) + connection pool refresh + tests staging environment + foundation Phase 9 production rollout.

**Piege 3 : Backup restore not actually tested.** Backups exist but never verified = useless when needed. Solution : weekly verification automated restore to isolated environment + measure success + alerts if backups corrupted + DR testing quarterly mandatory + audit trail comprehensive.

**Piege 4 : Pentest reports false positives noise.** Automated scanners generate many false positives = team overwhelmed + real issues missed. Solution : triage workflow VulnerabilityManagementService + mark_false_positive with justification + tuning rules + manual pentest priority + foundation Phase 9 dedicated security engineer.

**Piege 5 : Compliance audits incomplete documentation.** CNDP + ACAPS reports require comprehensive documentation = scope underestimated. Solution : compliance docs structured templates + Sprint 28 admin-portal evidence collection + Sprint 7 audit comprehensive + foundation Phase 9 official audits.

**Piege 6 : DR testing in production = risk.** Testing disaster recovery on production = potential outages. Solution : DR testing isolated environment (separate AWS account + restore latest backups + measure RTO/RPO) + foundation Phase 9 production failover testing during maintenance windows.

**Piege 7 : Security incidents response inconsistent.** Each incident handled differently = lessons not learned + recovery slow. Solution : runbooks per incident_type + incident management framework + post-mortem template mandatory + Sprint 9 escalation chain + foundation SIEM Phase 9+.

**Piege 8 : WAF rules tuning blocking legitimate traffic.** Aggressive WAF rules block legitimate customers = revenue loss + brand damage. Solution : WAF foundation rules conservative Sprint 35 + monitoring false positives + Sprint 28 admin-portal events + tuning iterative + foundation Phase 9 dedicated tuning.

**Piege 9 : PII masking incomplete cross-tenant.** Sprint 27 PII masking gaps = compliance breach. Solution : Sprint 35 extends Sprint 27 + audit comprehensive coverage + tests negative no PII leak + foundation Phase 9 advanced DLP.

**Piege 10 : Key rotation chaos services downtime.** Key rotation breaks JWT signing + encrypted data access = production outages. Solution : grace period rotation (old + new keys valid simultaneously) + JWT signing keys rotation 180 days conservative + encryption keys envelope encryption pattern Sprint 10 + tests comprehensive staging.

---

```md
# B-36 -- META-PROMPT SPRINT 36 : PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT INFRASTRUCTURE

**Version** : v3.0 (sprint nouveau Phase 9 Sprint 1)
**Phase** : 9 -- Production Deployment (Sprints 36-37)
**Sprint** : 36 / 37 -- Phase 9 Sprint 1
**Position** : Apres Sprint 35 Pentest + Security Hardening (CLOTURE Phase 8), avant Sprint 37 Phase 9 Sprint 2 Go-Live (DERNIER SPRINT)
**Numerotation taches** : 9.3.1 a 9.3.12 (12 taches)
**Effort total** : ~90 heures developpement
**Priorite** : P0 PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT INFRASTRUCTURE + DEMO DAY READINESS
**AUCUNE EMOJI AUTORISEE** (decision-006 absolu)

---

## OBJECTIF GLOBAL DU SPRINT

Implementer le **framework Production Deployment Infrastructure** -- couche infrastructure production strategique cloturant Phase 9 Sprint 1 + preparation Sprint 37 Go-Live final. Le sprint construit **8 categories infrastructure production** : (1) **Kubernetes production cluster** -- **AWS EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service)** OR **Google GKE** Morocco region foundation (eu-west-3 Paris foundation Phase 9+ Morocco data residency decision-008) + multi-AZ deployment 3 zones disponibilite + auto-scaling Cluster Autoscaler + Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) + Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA) foundation + node pools optimises (general + memory-optimized pour ClickHouse + compute-optimized pour AI workers Sprint 33), (2) **CI/CD pipelines GitHub Actions** -- automated deployment pipeline complete (build + test + scan Snyk + push Docker registry ECR + deploy staging + smoke tests + manual approval gate + deploy production) + **canary releases** strategy (10% traffic + monitor 30min + 50% + 100% progressive) + **blue-green deployment** alternative pour critical services + **rollback automated** sur SLO breach + **deployment tracking** DeploymentRelease entity, (3) **Observability operational comprehensive** -- **Datadog OR ELK Stack** APM (Application Performance Monitoring) + distributed tracing OpenTelemetry foundation + logs centralized + metrics Prometheus + dashboards Grafana production-grade per service + **alerting rules** structures (SLO-based + symptom-based) + **on-call rotation** integration PagerDuty OR Opsgenie + runbooks per alert, (4) **Database production hardened** -- **PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ** automated failover + read replicas foundation + connection pooling pgBouncer + **ClickHouse cluster** 3-node minimum replicated + ZooKeeper coordination + **Redis ElastiCache** replicated Multi-AZ + **backup strategies** Sprint 35 production rollout + verification weekly + cross-region replication eu-west-1 + eu-west-3 foundation, (5) **CDN + WAF + edge security** -- **Cloudflare** OR **AWS CloudFront** + **WAF rules** production Sprint 35 deployment + **DDoS protection** + **edge caching** static assets + **rate limiting** edge level + **geo-blocking** optional + **bot management** foundation, (6) **Load balancing + service mesh** -- **AWS Application Load Balancer (ALB)** OR **Istio service mesh** foundation + **TLS termination** Let's Encrypt automated + **routing rules** path-based + **health checks** automated unhealthy hosts removal + **session affinity** where needed + **gRPC support** foundation Phase 9+, (7) **Production secrets management** -- **HashiCorp Vault production cluster** Multi-AZ + HSM (Hardware Security Module) foundation Phase 9+ + automated rotation Sprint 35 production deployment + emergency procedures tested + secrets injection at runtime (Vault Agent sidecar pattern), et (8) **Capacity planning + cost optimization** -- **infrastructure cost monitoring** AWS Cost Explorer OR Google Cloud Billing + **capacity forecasting** based growth projections + **scaling recommendations** automated + **reserved instances** foundation Phase 9+ post-stability + **spot instances** non-critical workloads (AI training Phase 8+) + **rightsizing** automated foundation.

Le sprint produit **infrastructure-as-code complete** Terraform (OR AWS CDK) + **deployment runbooks** + **production architecture diagrams** + **disaster recovery procedures Sprint 35 production-grade** + **SLA targets** (uptime 99.9% + latency p95 < 300ms + RTO 4h + RPO 1h) + **monitoring dashboards production**. Le sprint introduit **decision-037 ratification** production deployment framework + infrastructure-as-code adoption + observability operational comprehensive + foundation Phase 9 Sprint 37 Go-Live + Sprint 36 swap stubs vers real APIs production post-licensing.

A la sortie de ce sprint, Skalean dispose d'**entites domain Production Deployment** :
- **`DeploymentRelease`** (id + version SEMVER VARCHAR + git_commit_sha + environment enum staging/production + deployment_strategy enum canary/blue_green/rolling + status enum scheduled/deploying/deployed/rolled_back/failed + deployed_at + rolled_back_at nullable + rollback_reason + deployed_by_user_id FK + canary_traffic_percent INTEGER + canary_metrics JSONB {error_rate + latency_p95 + success_rate} + audit_trail) UNIQUE (version, environment)
- **`InfrastructureResource`** (id + resource_type enum eks_cluster/rds_postgres/clickhouse_node/redis_cluster/alb/cloudfront/waf/vault + provider enum aws/gcp + region + config JSONB Terraform foundation + cost_estimate_monthly_usd NUMERIC + active + last_modified_at + audit_trail)
- **`ProductionAlert`** (id + alert_type enum slo_breach/error_rate_high/latency_high/availability_down/security_incident + service_name + severity enum p0_critical/p1_high/p2_medium/p3_low + status enum firing/acknowledged/resolved + triggered_at + acknowledged_at nullable + acknowledged_by_user_id nullable + resolved_at nullable + runbook_url + alert_payload JSONB + audit_trail) indexes (severity + status partial + triggered_at)
- **`CapacityForecast`** (id + metric enum cpu_usage/memory_usage/storage_usage/network_throughput/database_connections + service_or_resource VARCHAR + current_usage NUMERIC + projected_usage_30d NUMERIC + projected_usage_90d NUMERIC + scaling_recommendation TEXT + recommended_action enum scale_up_now/scale_up_30d/no_action + forecast_generated_at + audit_trail)
- **`ServiceHealthSnapshot`** (id + service_name + uptime_percent_24h NUMERIC + uptime_percent_7d NUMERIC + uptime_percent_30d NUMERIC + latency_p50_ms + latency_p95_ms + latency_p99_ms + error_rate_percent NUMERIC + requests_per_minute INTEGER + measured_at + audit_trail) refresh hourly cron

Un **package `@insurtech/production-deployment`** v1.0.0 NestJS module + **6 services principaux** :

**DeploymentService** :
- `createDeployment(version, environment, strategy)` create DeploymentRelease + queue
- `executeCanaryDeployment(deployment_id)` canary strategy :
  1. Deploy new version to 10% pods (via Kubernetes deployment strategy)
  2. Wait 30 minutes monitoring
  3. Query Sprint 35 ServiceHealthSnapshot + alert rules
  4. If error_rate < threshold AND latency_p95 < threshold : progress to 50%
  5. Wait 30 minutes monitoring
  6. If healthy : progress to 100%
  7. Update DeploymentRelease status='deployed'
  8. Sprint 9 notify deploy_admin success
- `executeBlueGreenDeployment(deployment_id)` blue-green :
  1. Deploy new version to "green" environment full
  2. Run smoke tests
  3. Switch ALB traffic blue -> green
  4. Monitor 30 minutes
  5. If healthy : decommission blue (keep for rollback 24h)
- `rollbackDeployment(deployment_id, reason)` immediate rollback to previous version + Sprint 9 alert
- Automated rollback on SLO breach (error_rate > 5% OR latency_p95 > 1000ms during canary)
- `listDeployments(filters)` history + audit

**InfrastructureManagementService** :
- Terraform integration (OR AWS CDK) foundation :
  - `applyTerraform(stack)` apply infrastructure changes + plan review
  - `destroyTerraform(stack)` decommission resources + safety confirmation
  - `listInfrastructureResources(filters)` Terraform state inspection
- Cost monitoring AWS Cost Explorer API integration foundation
- Resource tagging strategy (Environment + Service + Team + CostCenter)
- Foundation Phase 9+ GitOps (ArgoCD + Flux)

**ObservabilityService** :
- Datadog API integration (OR ELK Stack):
  - Metrics ingestion automated per service
  - APM distributed tracing OpenTelemetry foundation
  - Logs centralized (FluentBit -> Datadog OR ELK)
  - Dashboards production per service auto-provisioned
- Synthetic monitoring (uptime checks every 1 min from 3 regions)
- Real User Monitoring (RUM) foundation Sprint 18 customer-portal
- Custom metrics business (Sprint 13 ClickHouse exposed Prometheus)
- Foundation Phase 9+ ML-based anomaly detection

**AlertingService** :
- PagerDuty (OR Opsgenie) integration :
  - Alert routing per severity (P0 -> immediate page on-call + Sprint 9 critical notify + Slack channel)
  - On-call rotation schedules
  - Escalation chains documented
  - Acknowledgment + resolution tracking
- Alert rules structured :
  - SLO-based (uptime + latency + error rate)
  - Symptom-based (CPU/memory/disk + error logs)
  - Business-critical (Sprint 28 audit + Sprint 35 security_admin alerts)
- Runbook URLs mandatory per alert
- ProductionAlert entity tracking comprehensive
- Foundation Phase 9+ AI-based alert correlation reducing noise

**CapacityPlanningService** :
- Cron weekly `CAPACITY_FORECASTING_CRON=0 8 * * 1` (Monday 08h00 MA) :
  - Query metrics last 90 days
  - Linear regression projection 30d + 90d
  - Identify scaling recommendations
  - Create CapacityForecast entities
- Scaling recommendations automated (scale up CPU + memory + storage + DB connections)
- Cost optimization suggestions (rightsizing + reserved instances + spot instances opportunities)
- Sprint 28 admin-portal capacity planning dashboards
- Foundation Phase 9+ ML-based forecasting

**ServiceHealthService** :
- Cron hourly `SERVICE_HEALTH_SNAPSHOT_CRON=0 * * * *` (hourly) :
  - Iterate services
  - Query Prometheus metrics latency + error rate + uptime
  - Create ServiceHealthSnapshot
- SLO tracking per service :
  - Sprint 5/6/7/8/9/10 services SLO 99.9% uptime
  - Sprint 13 ClickHouse SLO 99.5% (analytics tolerance)
  - Sprint 33 AI services SLO 99% (workers batch tolerance)
  - Sprint 34 connectors stubs SLO 99% (external dependencies)
- Sprint 28 admin-portal service health dashboards
- SLO breach alerts AlertingService integration
- Foundation Phase 9+ error budget management

**Integration Sprint 9 notifications** : deploy_admin + security_admin + on_call alerts (P0 immediate + P1 within 1h + P2 within 24h),

**Integration Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions** **4 nouveaux tabs production** :
- `/production/deployments/*` (deployment history + status real-time + canary progress + rollback button + audit)
- `/production/infrastructure/*` (infrastructure resources inventory + cost + Terraform plans)
- `/production/observability/*` (service health + alerts active + SLO compliance + dashboards links Grafana)
- `/production/capacity/*` (capacity forecasts + scaling recommendations + cost optimization)

**Integration Sprint 13 analytics** : production_metrics_mv aggregating service health + deployment frequency + alert response times + SLO compliance per day,

Des **endpoints REST production deployment** controller `production-deployment.controller.ts` 18 endpoints + permissions super_admin/deploy_admin Sprint 7.5a + rate limits + idempotency Sprint 15,

Et **30+ tests E2E** scenarios couvrant canary deployment + rollback + Terraform plans + observability + alerts + capacity forecasting + service health snapshots + Sprint 28 admin-portal extensions.

Le sprint constitue **infrastructure production foundation absolute** + **Sprint 37 Go-Live readiness** + **Demo Day 30 juin 2026 production-grade demonstration**. Sans Sprint 36, Sprint 37 Go-Live = manual + fragile + sans observability = production catastrophe inevitable. Avec Sprint 36, Skalean = enterprise SaaS production infrastructure complete + SLA-driven operations + foundation scaling Phase 9+ comprehensive.

## CONTEXTE STRATEGIQUE -- POURQUOI LE SPRINT EST CRITIQUE

Sprint 36 est **production deployment infrastructure foundation absolute critical** + **Sprint 37 Go-Live readiness mandatory** + **Demo Day 30 juin 2026 production-grade narrative**. Quatre dimensions strategiques majeures.

Premierement, **Sprint 37 Go-Live impossible sans Sprint 36 foundation**. Deployer production Sprint 37 sans Kubernetes + CI/CD + observability + database production + secrets management = manual operations + fragile + no visibility + catastrophic failure mode. Sprint 36 provides foundation operationally-mature production deployment.

Deuxiemement, **enterprise SaaS production credibility**. Customers enterprise + investisseurs ACAPS attendent production architecture mature (multi-AZ + auto-scaling + observability + DR + SLA). Sprint 36 delivers tangible enterprise-grade infrastructure + foundation Phase 9+ scaling Morocco market growth + foundation cross-country expansion Phase 10+.

Troisiemement, **Demo Day 30 juin 2026 demonstration production-ready architecture**. Live demo running on production-grade infrastructure (not staging hacked) = compelling proof point execution capability + technical depth + readiness commercial launch post-Demo Day.

Quatriemement, **SLA-driven operations foundation Phase 9+**. SLA targets Sprint 36 (99.9% uptime + latency p95 < 300ms + RTO 4h + RPO 1h) = foundation contractual SLAs customers Phase 9+ enterprise tier subscriptions + reputational moat vs competitors basic platforms.

### Decision-037 -- Production Deployment Framework + Infrastructure-as-Code

**Justification** :

- (b) Enterprise SaaS production credibility tangible
- (c) Demo Day production-ready narrative
- (d) SLA-driven operations foundation Phase 9+

**Architecture** :
- 5 entites + RLS where applicable
- 6 services + 2 workers
- Terraform infrastructure-as-code complete
- CI/CD GitHub Actions canary + rollback
- Observability Datadog OR ELK
- Sprint 9 + 13 + 28 integration
- Foundation Phase 9+ scaling + multi-region + GitOps
